020import static java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.*;

025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.toList;

027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
029
030import java.io.*;
031import java.lang.reflect.*;
032import java.math.*;
033import java.net.*;
034import java.nio.*;
035import java.text.*;
036import java.time.*;
037import java.time.format.*;
038import java.util.*;
039import java.util.concurrent.*;
040import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
041import java.util.function.*;

050/**
051 * Reusable string utility methods.
052 */
053@SuppressWarnings({
054   "java:S115",  // Constant names use UPPER_snakeCase convention
055   "java:S1192", // Duplicated string literals (HTML entities) are intentional
056   "java:S5843", // FP_REGEX copied from JDK source for parsing consistency
057   "java:S3516"  // Methods flagged as always returning the same value are valid utility implementations (e.g. null-safe wrappers)
058})
059public class StringUtils {
060
061   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
062   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
063   private static final String ARG_builder = "builder";
064   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
065   private static final String ARG_s = "s";
066
067   /** Characters considered common separators (comma/semicolon/colon/pipe/tab). */
068   public static final AsciiSet COMMON_SEPARATORS = AsciiSet.of(",;:|\t");
069
070   /** Windows-style carriage-return/line-feed sequence. */
071   public static final String CRLF = "\r\n";
072
073   /** Digits 0-9 represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
074   public static final AsciiSet DECIMAL_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("0123456789");
075
076   /** Digits 0-9 represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
077   public static final AsciiSet DIGIT = AsciiSet.of("0123456789");
078
079   /** Zero-length string constant. */
080   public static final String EMPTY = "";
081
082   /** Zero-length string array constant. */
083   public static final String[] EMPTY_STRING_ARRAY = new String[0];
084
085   /** Characters allowed at the beginning of a numeric literal. */
086   public static final AsciiSet FIRST_NUMBER_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("+-.#0123456789");
087
088   /** Hexadecimal digit characters. */
089   public static final AsciiSet HEXADECIMAL_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("0123456789abcdefABCDEF");
090
091   /** Characters allowed in HTTP headers (including quoted strings and comments). */
092   public static final AsciiSet HTTP_HEADER_CHARS = AsciiSet.create().chars("\t -").ranges("!-[","]-}").build();
093
094   /** Letters a-z and A-Z represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
095   public static final AsciiSet LETTER = AsciiSet.create().ranges("a-z", "A-Z").build();
096
097   /** Lowercase letters a-z represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
098   public static final AsciiSet LETTER_LC = AsciiSet.create().range('a', 'z').build();
099
100   /** Uppercase letters A-Z represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
101   public static final AsciiSet LETTER_UC = AsciiSet.create().range('A', 'Z').build();
102
103   /** Characters escaped when parsing key/value pairs. */
104   public static final AsciiSet MAP_ESCAPE_SET = AsciiSet.of(",=\\");
105
106   /** Unix-style newline character. */
107   public static final String NEWLINE = "\n";
108
109   /** Predicate that filters out {@code null} and empty strings. */
110   public static final Predicate<String> NOT_EMPTY = Utils::ne;
111
112   /** Characters that can appear anywhere in a numeric literal. */
113   public static final AsciiSet NUMBER_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("-xX.+-#pP0123456789abcdefABCDEF");
114
115   /** Octal digit characters. */
116   public static final AsciiSet OCTAL_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("01234567");
117
118   /** Characters escaped when parsing quoted strings. */
119   public static final AsciiSet QUOTE_ESCAPE_SET = AsciiSet.of("\"'\\");
120
121   /** Single-space character constant. */
122   public static final String SPACE = " ";
123
124   /** Horizontal tab character constant. */
125   public static final String TAB = "\t";
126
127   /** Characters considered part of a URI when encoding paths. */
128   public static final AsciiSet URI_CHARS = AsciiSet.create().chars("?#+%;/:@&=+$,-_.!~*'()").range('0', '9').range('A', 'Z').range('a', 'z').build();
129
130   /** Characters that may appear unescaped in path segments when URL-encoding. */
131   public static final AsciiSet URL_ENCODE_PATHINFO_VALIDCHARS = AsciiSet.create().ranges("a-z", "A-Z", "0-9").chars("-_.*/()").build();
132
133   /** Characters that never require URL encoding per RFC 3986. */
134   public static final AsciiSet URL_UNENCODED_CHARS = AsciiSet.create().ranges("a-z", "A-Z", "0-9").chars("-_.!~*'()\\").build();
135
136   /** Extended set of characters that are typically safe to leave unencoded. */
137   public static final AsciiSet URL_UNENCODED_LAX_CHARS = URL_UNENCODED_CHARS.copy().chars(":@$,").chars("{}|\\^[]`").build();
138
139   /** Vowel characters a, e, i, o, u (both uppercase and lowercase) represented as an {@link AsciiSet}. */
140   public static final AsciiSet VOWEL = AsciiSet.of("aeiouAEIOU");
141
142   /**
143    * All standard whitespace characters (space, tab, newline, carriage return, form feed, vertical tab).
144    */
145   public static final AsciiSet WHITESPACE_CHARS = AsciiSet.of(" \t\n\r\f\u000B");
146
147   // Maps 6-bit nibbles to BASE64 characters.
148   private static final char[] BASE64M1 = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/".toCharArray();
149
150   // Maps BASE64 characters to 6-bit nibbles.
151   private static final byte[] BASE64M2 = new byte[128];
152
153   static {
154      for (var i = 0; i < 64; i++)
155         BASE64M2[BASE64M1[i]] = (byte)i;
156   }
157   private static final Random RANDOM = new Random();
158
159   /**
160    * Floating-point literal validation pattern used by {@link Double#valueOf(String)}.
161    *
162    * <p>
163    * Matches decimal, hexadecimal, and scientific-notation literals, as well as {@code NaN} and {@code Infinity},
164    * with optional sign, exponent, and type suffixes. Copied from the JDK source to keep parsing logic consistent.
165    */
166   public static final Pattern FP_REGEX = Pattern.compile(
167      "[+-]?(NaN|Infinity|((((\\p{Digit}+)(\\.)?((\\p{Digit}+)?)([eE][+-]?(\\p{Digit}+))?)|(\\.((\\p{Digit}+))([eE][+-]?(\\p{Digit}+))?)|(((0[xX](\\p{XDigit}+)(\\.)?)|(0[xX](\\p{XDigit}+)?(\\.)(\\p{XDigit}+)))[pP][+-]?(\\p{Digit}+)))[fFdD]?))[\\x00-\\x20]*"
168   );
169
170   static final Map<Character,AsciiSet> ESCAPE_SETS = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
171
172   private static final List<Readifier> READIFIERS = loadReadifiers();
173   private static final Cache<Class<?>,Function<Object,String>> READIFIER_CACHE = Cache.<Class<?>,Function<Object,String>>create().weak().build();
174
175   private static final char[] HEX = "0123456789ABCDEF".toCharArray();
176
177   /**
178    * Abbreviates a string using ellipses if it exceeds the specified length.
179    *
180    * <p>
181    * If the string is longer than the specified length, it is truncated and <js>"..."</js> is appended.
182    * The total length of the result will be exactly <c>length</c> characters (including the ellipses).
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * If the string is <jk>null</jk>, shorter than or equal to <c>length</c>, or has length 3 or less,
186    * the original string is returned unchanged.
187    *
188    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
189    * <p class='bjava'>
190    *    abbreviate(<js>"Hello World"</js>, 8);        <jc>// "Hello..."</jc>
191    *    abbreviate(<js>"Hello World"</js>, 20);       <jc>// "Hello World" (no change)</jc>
192    *    abbreviate(<js>"Hi"</js>, 5);                 <jc>// "Hi" (too short to abbreviate)</jc>
193    *    abbreviate(<jk>null</jk>, 10);                <jc>// null</jc>
194    * </p>
195    *
196    * @param in The input string. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
197    * @param length The maximum length of the resulting string (must be at least 4 for abbreviation to occur).
198    * @return The abbreviated string with ellipses, or the original string if no abbreviation is needed.
199    */
200   public static String abbreviate(String in, int length) {
201      if (in == null || in.length() <= length || in.length() <= 3)
202         return in;
203      return in.substring(0, length - 3) + "...";
204   }
205
206   /**
207    * Appends a string to a StringBuilder, creating a new one if null.
208    *
209    * @param sb The StringBuilder to append to, or <jk>null</jk> to create a new one.
210    * @param in The string to append.
211    * @return The StringBuilder with the string appended.
212    */
213   public static StringBuilder append(StringBuilder sb, String in) {
214      if (sb == null)
215         return new StringBuilder(in);
216      sb.append(in);
217      return sb;
218   }
219
220   /**
221    * Appends a string to a StringBuilder if the string is not blank.
222    *
223    * <p>
224    * Returns the same StringBuilder instance for method chaining.
225    * If the string is <jk>null</jk>, empty, or contains only whitespace, nothing is appended.
226    * If <c>sb</c> is <jk>null</jk> and an append is going to occur, a new StringBuilder is automatically created.
227    *
228    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
229    * <p class='bjava'>
230    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb</jv> = <jk>new</jk> StringBuilder();
231    *    appendIfNotBlank(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"hello"</js>);  <jc>// Appends "hello"</jc>
232    *    appendIfNotBlank(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"   "</js>);    <jc>// Does nothing</jc>
233    *    appendIfNotBlank(<jv>sb</jv>, <jk>null</jk>);     <jc>// Does nothing</jc>
234    *    appendIfNotBlank(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"world"</js>);  <jc>// Appends "world"</jc>
235    *    <jc>// Result: "helloworld"</jc>
236    *
237    *    <jc>// Auto-create StringBuilder if null and append occurs</jc>
238    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb2</jv> = appendIfNotBlank(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"test"</js>);  <jc>// Creates new StringBuilder with "test"</jc>
239    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb3</jv> = appendIfNotBlank(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"   "</js>);   <jc>// Returns null (no append occurred)</jc>
240    * </p>
241    *
242    * @param sb The StringBuilder to append to. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
243    * @param str The string to append if not blank. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
244    * @return The same StringBuilder instance for method chaining, or a new StringBuilder if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and an append occurred, or <jk>null</jk> if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and no append occurred.
245    */
246   public static StringBuilder appendIfNotBlank(StringBuilder sb, String str) {
247      if (isNotBlank(str)) {
248         if (sb == null)
249            sb = new StringBuilder();
250         sb.append(str);
251      }
252      return sb;
253   }
254
255   /**
256    * Appends a string to a StringBuilder if the string is not empty.
257    *
258    * <p>
259    * Returns the same StringBuilder instance for method chaining.
260    * If the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty, nothing is appended.
261    * If <c>sb</c> is <jk>null</jk> and an append is going to occur, a new StringBuilder is automatically created.
262    *
263    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
264    * <p class='bjava'>
265    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb</jv> = <jk>new</jk> StringBuilder();
266    *    appendIfNotEmpty(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"hello"</js>);  <jc>// Appends "hello"</jc>
267    *    appendIfNotEmpty(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>""</js>);       <jc>// Does nothing</jc>
268    *    appendIfNotEmpty(<jv>sb</jv>, <jk>null</jk>);     <jc>// Does nothing</jc>
269    *    appendIfNotEmpty(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"world"</js>);  <jc>// Appends "world"</jc>
270    *    <jc>// Result: "helloworld"</jc>
271    *
272    *    <jc>// Auto-create StringBuilder if null and append occurs</jc>
273    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb2</jv> = appendIfNotEmpty(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"test"</js>);  <jc>// Creates new StringBuilder with "test"</jc>
274    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb3</jv> = appendIfNotEmpty(<jk>null</jk>, <jk>null</jk>);   <jc>// Returns null (no append occurred)</jc>
275    * </p>
276    *
277    * @param sb The StringBuilder to append to. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
278    * @param str The string to append if not empty. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
279    * @return The same StringBuilder instance for method chaining, or a new StringBuilder if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and an append occurred, or <jk>null</jk> if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and no append occurred.
280    */
281   public static StringBuilder appendIfNotEmpty(StringBuilder sb, String str) {
282      if (ne(str)) {
283         if (sb == null)
284            sb = new StringBuilder();
285         sb.append(str);
286      }
287      return sb;
288   }
289
290   /**
291    * Appends a string to a StringBuilder with a separator, only adding the separator if the StringBuilder is not empty.
292    *
293    * <p>
294    * Returns the same StringBuilder instance for method chaining.
295    * If the StringBuilder is empty, only the string is appended (no separator).
296    * If the StringBuilder is not empty, the separator is appended first, then the string.
297    * If <c>sb</c> is <jk>null</jk> and an append is going to occur, a new StringBuilder is automatically created.
298    *
299    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
300    * <p class='bjava'>
301    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb</jv> = <jk>new</jk> StringBuilder();
302    *    appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"first"</js>, <js>", "</js>);   <jc>// Appends "first"</jc>
303    *    appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"second"</js>, <js>", "</js>);  <jc>// Appends ", second"</jc>
304    *    appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"third"</js>, <js>", "</js>);   <jc>// Appends ", third"</jc>
305    *    <jc>// Result: "first, second, third"</jc>
306    *
307    *    <jc>// Auto-create StringBuilder if null and append occurs</jc>
308    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb2</jv> = appendWithSeparator(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"test"</js>, <js>", "</js>);  <jc>// Creates new StringBuilder with "test"</jc>
309    *    StringBuilder <jv>sb3</jv> = appendWithSeparator(<jk>null</jk>, <jk>null</jk>, <js>", "</js>);   <jc>// Returns null (no append occurred)</jc>
310    * </p>
311    *
312    * @param sb The StringBuilder to append to. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
313    * @param str The string to append. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
314    * @param separator The separator to add before the string if the StringBuilder is not empty. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
315    * @return The same StringBuilder instance for method chaining, or a new StringBuilder if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and an append occurred, or <jk>null</jk> if <c>sb</c> was <jk>null</jk> and no append occurred.
316    */
317   public static StringBuilder appendWithSeparator(StringBuilder sb, String str, String separator) {
318      if (str != null) {
319         if (sb == null)
320            sb = new StringBuilder();
321         else if (!sb.isEmpty() && separator != null)
322            sb.append(separator);
323         sb.append(str);
324      }
325      return sb;
326   }
327
328   /**
329    * Adds the appropriate indefinite article ('a' or 'an') before a word.
330    *
331    * <p>Uses a simple vowel-based rule: 'an' if the word starts with a vowel, 'a' otherwise.
332    *
333    * @param subject The word to articlize.
334    * @return The word with 'a' or 'an' prepended.
335    */
336   public static String articlized(String subject) {
337      return (VOWEL.contains(subject.charAt(0)) ? "an " : "a ") + subject;
338   }
339
340   /**
341    * BASE64-decodes the specified string.
342    *
343    * @param in The BASE-64 encoded string.
344    * @return The decoded byte array, or an empty array if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
345    */
346   public static byte[] base64Decode(String in) {
347      if (in == null)
348         return new byte[0];
349
350      var bIn = in.getBytes(UTF8);
351
352      assertArg(bIn.length % 4 == 0, "Invalid BASE64 string length.  Must be multiple of 4.");
353
354      // Strip out any trailing '=' filler characters.
355      var inLength = bIn.length;
356      while (inLength > 0 && bIn[inLength - 1] == '=')
357         inLength--;
358
359      var outLength = (inLength * 3) / 4;
360      var out = new byte[outLength];
361      var iIn = 0;
362      var iOut = 0;
363      while (iIn < inLength) {
364         var i0 = bIn[iIn++];
365         var i1 = bIn[iIn++];
366         var i2 = iIn < inLength ? bIn[iIn++] : 'A';
367         var i3 = iIn < inLength ? bIn[iIn++] : 'A';
368         var b0 = BASE64M2[i0 & 0xff];
369         var b1 = BASE64M2[i1 & 0xff];
370         var b2 = BASE64M2[i2 & 0xff];
371         var b3 = BASE64M2[i3 & 0xff];
372         var o0 = (b0 << 2) | (b1 >>> 4);
373         var o1 = ((b1 & 0xf) << 4) | (b2 >>> 2);
374         var o2 = ((b2 & 3) << 6) | (b3 & 0xff);
375         out[iOut++] = (byte)(o0 & 0xff);
376         if (iOut < outLength)
377            out[iOut++] = (byte)(o1 & 0xff);
378         if (iOut < outLength)
379            out[iOut++] = (byte)(o2 & 0xff);
380      }
381      return out;
382   }
383
384   /**
385    * Shortcut for calling <c>base64Decode(String)</c> and converting the result to a UTF-8 encoded string.
386    *
387    * @param in The BASE-64 encoded string to decode.
388    * @return The decoded string, or an empty string if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
389    */
390   public static String base64DecodeToString(String in) {
391      var b = base64Decode(in);
392      return new String(b, UTF8);
393   }
394
395   /**
396    * BASE64-encodes the specified byte array.
397    *
398    * @param in The input byte array to convert.
399    * @return The byte array converted to a BASE-64 encoded string.
400    */
401   public static String base64Encode(byte[] in) {
402      if (in == null)
403         return null;
404      var outLength = (in.length * 4 + 2) / 3;   // Output length without padding
405      var out = new char[((in.length + 2) / 3) * 4];  // Length includes padding.
406      var iIn = 0;
407      var iOut = 0;
408      while (iIn < in.length) {
409         var i0 = in[iIn++] & 0xff;
410         var i1 = iIn < in.length ? in[iIn++] & 0xff : 0;
411         var i2 = iIn < in.length ? in[iIn++] & 0xff : 0;
412         var o0 = i0 >>> 2;
413         var o1 = ((i0 & 3) << 4) | (i1 >>> 4);
414         var o2 = ((i1 & 0xf) << 2) | (i2 >>> 6);
415         var o3 = i2 & 0x3F;
416         out[iOut++] = BASE64M1[o0];
417         out[iOut++] = BASE64M1[o1];
418         out[iOut] = iOut < outLength ? BASE64M1[o2] : '=';
419         iOut++;
420         out[iOut] = iOut < outLength ? BASE64M1[o3] : '=';
421         iOut++;
422      }
423      return new String(out);
424   }
425
426   /**
427    * Shortcut for calling <code>base64Encode(in.getBytes(<js>"UTF-8"</js>))</code>
428    *
429    * @param in The input string to convert.
430    * @return The string converted to BASE-64 encoding.
431    */
432   public static String base64EncodeToString(String in) {
433      if (in == null)
434         return null;
435      return base64Encode(in.getBytes(UTF8));
436   }
437
438   /**
439    * Builds a string using a functional approach with a StringBuilder.
440    *
441    * <p>
442    * Creates a new StringBuilder, applies the consumer to it, and returns the resulting string.
443    * This provides a functional way to build strings without manually managing the StringBuilder.
444    *
445    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
446    * <p class='bjava'>
447    *    String <jv>result</jv> = buildString(<jv>sb</jv> -&gt; {
448    *       <jv>sb</jv>.append(<js>"Hello"</js>);
449    *       <jv>sb</jv>.append(<js>" "</js>);
450    *       <jv>sb</jv>.append(<js>"World"</js>);
451    *    });
452    *    <jc>// Returns: "Hello World"</jc>
453    *
454    *    String <jv>joined</jv> = buildString(<jv>sb</jv> -&gt; {
455    *       appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"a"</js>, <js>", "</js>);
456    *       appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>", "</js>);
457    *       appendWithSeparator(<jv>sb</jv>, <js>"c"</js>, <js>", "</js>);
458    *    });
459    *    <jc>// Returns: "a, b, c"</jc>
460    * </p>
461    *
462    * @param builder The consumer that builds the string using the provided StringBuilder.
463    * @return The built string.
464    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If <c>builder</c> is <jk>null</jk>.
465    */
466   public static String buildString(Consumer<StringBuilder> builder) {
467      assertArgNotNull(ARG_builder, builder);
468      var sb = new StringBuilder();
469      builder.accept(sb);
470      return sb.toString();
471   }
472
473   /**
474    * Converts a string to camelCase format.
475    *
476    * <p>
477    * Handles various input formats:
478    * <ul>
479    *   <li>Space-separated: "hello world" → "helloWorld"</li>
480    *   <li>Underscore-separated: "hello_world" → "helloWorld"</li>
481    *   <li>Hyphen-separated: "hello-world" → "helloWorld"</li>
482    *   <li>PascalCase: "HelloWorld" → "helloWorld"</li>
483    *   <li>Already camelCase: "helloWorld" → "helloWorld"</li>
484    *   <li>Mixed case: "Hello_World-Test" → "helloWorldTest"</li>
485    * </ul>
486    *
487    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
488    * <p class='bjava'>
489    *    camelCase(<jk>null</jk>);                    <jc>// null</jc>
490    *    camelCase(<js>""</js>);                      <jc>// ""</jc>
491    *    camelCase(<js>"hello world"</js>);           <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
492    *    camelCase(<js>"hello_world"</js>);           <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
493    *    camelCase(<js>"hello-world"</js>);           <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
494    *    camelCase(<js>"HelloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
495    *    camelCase(<js>"helloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
496    *    camelCase(<js>"  hello   world  "</js>);     <jc>// "helloWorld"</jc>
497    * </p>
498    *
499    * @param str The string to convert.
500    * @return The camelCase string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
501    */
502   public static String camelCase(String str) {
503      if (isEmpty(str))
504         return str;
505
506      var words = splitWords(str);
507      if (words.isEmpty())
508         return "";
509
510      var result = new StringBuilder();
511      for (var i = 0; i < words.size(); i++) {
512         var word = words.get(i);
513         if (i == 0) {
514            result.append(uncapitalize(word));
515         } else {
516            result.append(capitalize(word.toLowerCase()));
517         }
518      }
519
520      return result.toString();
521   }
522
523   /**
524    * Capitalizes the first character of a string.
525    *
526    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
527    * <p class='bjava'>
528    *    capitalize(<jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// null</jc>
529    *    capitalize(<js>""</js>);            <jc>// ""</jc>
530    *    capitalize(<js>"hello"</js>);       <jc>// "Hello"</jc>
531    *    capitalize(<js>"Hello"</js>);       <jc>// "Hello"</jc>
532    *    capitalize(<js>"HELLO"</js>);       <jc>// "HELLO"</jc>
533    * </p>
534    *
535    * @param str The string to capitalize.
536    * @return The string with the first character capitalized, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
537    */
538   public static String capitalize(String str) {
539      if (isEmpty(str))
540         return str;
541      return Character.toUpperCase(str.charAt(0)) + str.substring(1);
542   }
543
544   /**
545    * Converts a comma-delimited string to a list.
546    *
547    * @param s The comma-delimited string.
548    * @return A new modifiable list. Never <jk>null</jk>.
549    */
550   public static List<String> cdlToList(String s) {
551      return split(s);
552   }
553
554   /**
555    * Converts a comma-delimited string to a set.
556    *
557    * @param s The comma-delimited string.
558    * @return A new {@link LinkedHashSet}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
559    */
560   public static Set<String> cdlToSet(String s) {
561      return split(s).stream().collect(Collectors.toCollection(LinkedHashSet::new));
562   }
563
564   /**
565    * Returns the character at the specified index in the string without throwing exceptions.
566    *
567    * <p>
568    * This is a null-safe and bounds-safe version of {@link String#charAt(int)}.
569    * Returns <c>0</c> (null character) if:
570    * <ul>
571    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
572    *   <li>The index is negative</li>
573    *   <li>The index is greater than or equal to the string length</li>
574    * </ul>
575    *
576    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
577    * <p class='bjava'>
578    *    charAt(<js>"Hello"</js>, 0);     <jc>// 'H'</jc>
579    *    charAt(<js>"Hello"</js>, 4);     <jc>// 'o'</jc>
580    *    charAt(<js>"Hello"</js>, 5);     <jc>// 0 (out of bounds)</jc>
581    *    charAt(<js>"Hello"</js>, -1);    <jc>// 0 (out of bounds)</jc>
582    *    charAt(<jk>null</jk>, 0);        <jc>// 0 (null string)</jc>
583    * </p>
584    *
585    * @param s The string.
586    * @param i The index position.
587    * @return The character at the specified index, or <c>0</c> if the index is out-of-range or the string is <jk>null</jk>.
588    * @see String#charAt(int)
589    */
590   public static char charAt(String s, int i) {
591      if (s == null || i < 0 || i >= s.length())
592         return 0;
593      return s.charAt(i);
594   }
595
596   /**
597    * Cleans a string by removing control characters and normalizing whitespace.
598    *
599    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
600    * <p class='bjava'>
601    *    clean(<js>"hello\u0000\u0001world"</js>);     <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
602    *    clean(<js>"hello  \t\n  world"</js>);         <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
603    * </p>
604    *
605    * @param str The string to clean.
606    * @return The cleaned string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
607    */
608   public static String clean(String str) {
609      if (str == null)
610         return null;
611      str = removeControlChars(str);
612      return normalizeWhitespace(str);
613   }
614
615   /**
616    * Compares two strings lexicographically, but gracefully handles <jk>null</jk> values.
617    *
618    * <p>
619    * Null handling:
620    * <ul>
621    *   <li>Both <jk>null</jk> → returns <c>0</c> (equal)</li>
622    *   <li>First <jk>null</jk> → returns {@link Integer#MIN_VALUE}</li>
623    *   <li>Second <jk>null</jk> → returns {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE}</li>
624    *   <li>Neither <jk>null</jk> → returns the same as {@link String#compareTo(String)}</li>
625    * </ul>
626    *
627    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
628    * <p class='bjava'>
629    *    compare(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"banana"</js>);   <jc>// negative (apple &lt; banana)</jc>
630    *    compare(<js>"banana"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);   <jc>// positive (banana &gt; apple)</jc>
631    *    compare(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);    <jc>// 0 (equal)</jc>
632    *    compare(<jk>null</jk>, <jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// 0 (equal)</jc>
633    *    compare(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"apple"</js>);       <jc>// Integer.MIN_VALUE</jc>
634    *    compare(<js>"apple"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);       <jc>// Integer.MAX_VALUE</jc>
635    * </p>
636    *
637    * @param s1 The first string.
638    * @param s2 The second string.
639    * @return A negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.
640    * @see String#compareTo(String)
641    */
642   public static int compare(String s1, String s2) {
643      if (s1 == null && s2 == null)
644         return 0;
645      if (s1 == null)
646         return Integer.MIN_VALUE;
647      if (s2 == null)
648         return Integer.MAX_VALUE;
649      return s1.compareTo(s2);
650   }
651
652   /**
653    * Compares two strings lexicographically, ignoring case.
654    *
655    * <p>
656    * Returns a negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second string, ignoring case.
657    *
658    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
659    * <p class='bjava'>
660    *    compareIgnoreCase(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"BANANA"</js>);   <jc>// negative (apple &lt; banana)</jc>
661    *    compareIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);    <jc>// 0 (equal)</jc>
662    *    compareIgnoreCase(<js>"Zebra"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);    <jc>// positive (zebra &gt; apple)</jc>
663    * </p>
664    *
665    * @param str1 The first string.
666    * @param str2 The second string.
667    * @return A negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.
668    */
669   public static int compareIgnoreCase(String str1, String str2) {
670      if (Objects.equals(str1, str2))
671         return 0;
672      if (str1 == null)
673         return -1;
674      if (str2 == null)
675         return 1;
676      return str1.compareToIgnoreCase(str2);
677   }
678
679   /**
680    * Compresses a UTF-8 string into a GZIP-compressed byte array.
681    *
682    * <p>
683    * This method compresses the input string using GZIP compression. The string is first converted to
684    * UTF-8 bytes, then compressed. Use {@link #decompress(byte[])} to decompress the result.
685    *
686    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
687    * <p class='bjava'>
688    *    <jc>// Compress a string</jc>
689    *    byte[] <jv>compressed</jv> = compress(<js>"Hello World"</js>);
690    *
691    *    <jc>// Decompress it back</jc>
692    *    String <jv>decompressed</jv> = decompress(<jv>compressed</jv>);
693    *    <jc>// Returns: "Hello World"</jc>
694    * </p>
695    *
696    * @param contents The UTF-8 string to compress.
697    * @return The GZIP-compressed byte array.
698    * @throws java.io.IOException If compression fails.
699    * @see #decompress(byte[])
700    */
701   public static byte[] compress(String contents) throws java.io.IOException {
702      var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream(contents.length() >> 1);
703      try (var gos = new GZIPOutputStream(baos)) {
704         gos.write(contents.getBytes());
705         gos.finish();
706         gos.flush();
707         return baos.toByteArray();
708      }
709   }
710
711   /**
712    * Checks if a string contains the specified character.
713    *
714    * <p>
715    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
716    *
717    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
718    * <p class='bjava'>
719    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'o'</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
720    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'x'</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
721    *    contains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);            <jc>// false</jc>
722    * </p>
723    *
724    * @param s The string to check.
725    * @param c The character to check for.
726    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains the specified character.
727    * @see #contains(String, CharSequence)
728    * @see #contains(String, String)
729    * @see #containsAny(String, char...)
730    */
731   public static boolean contains(String s, char c) {
732      return s != null && s.indexOf(c) >= 0;
733   }
734
735   /**
736    * Null-safe {@link String#contains(CharSequence)} operation.
737    *
738    * <p>
739    * Returns <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>, otherwise behaves the same as
740    * {@link String#contains(CharSequence)}.
741    *
742    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
743    * <p class='bjava'>
744    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
745    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
746    *    contains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);            <jc>// false</jc>
747    * </p>
748    *
749    * @param value The string to check.
750    * @param substring The substring to check for.
751    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the value contains the specified substring, <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
752    * @see #contains(String, char)
753    * @see #contains(String, String)
754    * @see #containsAny(String, CharSequence...)
755    */
756   public static boolean contains(String value, CharSequence substring) {
757      return nn(value) && value.contains(substring);
758   }
759
760   /**
761    * Null-safe check if a string contains another string.
762    *
763    * <p>
764    * Returns <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>, otherwise behaves the same as
765    * {@link String#contains(CharSequence)}.
766    *
767    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
768    * <p class='bjava'>
769    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
770    *    contains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
771    *    contains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);            <jc>// false</jc>
772    * </p>
773    *
774    * @param s The string to check.
775    * @param substring The substring to check for.
776    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains the specified substring, <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
777    * @see #contains(String, CharSequence)
778    * @see #containsAny(String, String...)
779    */
780   public static boolean contains(String s, String substring) {
781      return nn(s) && s.contains(substring);
782   }
783
784   /**
785    * Checks if a string contains all of the specified characters.
786    *
787    * <p>
788    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
789    * <ul>
790    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
791    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
792    *   <li>Any of the specified characters are not found in the string</li>
793    * </ul>
794    *
795    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
796    * <p class='bjava'>
797    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'H'</js>, <js>'e'</js>, <js>'l'</js>);   <jc>// true (contains all)</jc>
798    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'H'</js>, <js>'x'</js>);                <jc>// false (missing 'x')</jc>
799    *    containsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);                                      <jc>// false</jc>
800    * </p>
801    *
802    * @param s The string to check.
803    * @param values The characters to check for.
804    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains all of the specified characters.
805    * @see #containsAny(String, char...)
806    */
807   public static boolean containsAll(String s, char...values) {
808      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
809         return false;
810      for (var v : values) {
811         if (s.indexOf(v) < 0)
812            return false;
813      }
814      return true;
815   }
816
817   /**
818    * Checks if a string contains all of the specified substrings.
819    *
820    * <p>
821    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
822    * <ul>
823    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
824    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
825    *   <li>Any of the specified substrings are not found in the string</li>
826    * </ul>
827    *
828    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
829    * <p class='bjava'>
830    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// true (contains all)</jc>
831    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);    <jc>// false (missing "Foo")</jc>
832    *    containsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                              <jc>// false</jc>
833    * </p>
834    *
835    * @param s The string to check.
836    * @param values The substrings to check for.
837    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains all of the specified substrings.
838    * @see #containsAny(String, CharSequence...)
839    */
840   public static boolean containsAll(String s, CharSequence...values) {
841      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
842         return false;
843      for (var v : values) {
844         if (! s.contains(v))
845            return false;
846      }
847      return true;
848   }
849
850   /**
851    * Checks if a string contains all of the specified substrings.
852    *
853    * <p>
854    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
855    * <ul>
856    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
857    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
858    *   <li>Any of the specified substrings are not found in the string</li>
859    * </ul>
860    *
861    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
862    * <p class='bjava'>
863    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// true (contains all)</jc>
864    *    containsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);    <jc>// false (missing "Foo")</jc>
865    *    containsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                              <jc>// false</jc>
866    * </p>
867    *
868    * @param s The string to check.
869    * @param values The substrings to check for.
870    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains all of the specified substrings.
871    * @see #containsAny(String, String...)
872    */
873   public static boolean containsAll(String s, String...values) {
874      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
875         return false;
876      for (var v : values) {
877         if (! s.contains(v))
878            return false;
879      }
880      return true;
881   }
882
883   /**
884    * Checks if a string contains any of the specified characters.
885    *
886    * <p>
887    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
888    * <ul>
889    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
890    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
891    *   <li>None of the specified characters are found in the string</li>
892    * </ul>
893    *
894    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
895    * <p class='bjava'>
896    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'o'</js>, <js>'x'</js>);   <jc>// true (contains 'o')</jc>
897    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'x'</js>, <js>'y'</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
898    *    containsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);                          <jc>// false</jc>
899    * </p>
900    *
901    * @param s The string to check.
902    * @param values The characters to check for.
903    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains any of the specified characters.
904    * @see #contains(String, char)
905    * @see #containsAll(String, char...)
906    */
907   public static boolean containsAny(String s, char...values) {
908      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
909         return false;
910      for (var v : values) {
911         if (s.indexOf(v) >= 0)
912            return true;
913      }
914      return false;
915   }
916
917   /**
918    * Checks if a string contains any of the specified substrings.
919    *
920    * <p>
921    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
922    * <ul>
923    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
924    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
925    *   <li>None of the specified substrings are found in the string</li>
926    * </ul>
927    *
928    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
929    * <p class='bjava'>
930    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);   <jc>// true (contains "Hello")</jc>
931    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"Bar"</js>);    <jc>// false</jc>
932    *    containsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                            <jc>// false</jc>
933    * </p>
934    *
935    * @param s The string to check.
936    * @param values The substrings to check for.
937    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains any of the specified substrings.
938    * @see #contains(String, CharSequence)
939    * @see #containsAll(String, CharSequence...)
940    */
941   public static boolean containsAny(String s, CharSequence...values) {
942      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
943         return false;
944      for (var v : values) {
945         if (s.contains(v))
946            return true;
947      }
948      return false;
949   }
950
951   /**
952    * Checks if a string contains any of the specified substrings.
953    *
954    * <p>
955    * This is a null-safe operation that returns <jk>false</jk> if:
956    * <ul>
957    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
958    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
959    *   <li>None of the specified substrings are found in the string</li>
960    * </ul>
961    *
962    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
963    * <p class='bjava'>
964    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);   <jc>// true (contains "Hello")</jc>
965    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"Bar"</js>);    <jc>// false</jc>
966    *    containsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                            <jc>// false</jc>
967    *    containsAny(<js>"Hello"</js>);                                          <jc>// false (no values to check)</jc>
968    * </p>
969    *
970    * @param s The string to check.
971    * @param values The substrings to check for.
972    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains any of the specified substrings.
973    * @see #contains(String, String)
974    * @see #containsAll(String, String...)
975    */
976   public static boolean containsAny(String s, String...values) {
977      if (s == null || values == null || values.length == 0)
978         return false;
979      for (var v : values) {
980         if (s.contains(v))
981            return true;
982      }
983      return false;
984   }
985
986   /**
987    * Checks if a string contains a substring, ignoring case.
988    *
989    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
990    * <p class='bjava'>
991    *    containsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
992    *    containsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"WORLD"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
993    *    containsIgnoreCase(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);       <jc>// false</jc>
994    * </p>
995    *
996    * @param str The string to search in.
997    * @param search The substring to search for.
998    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string contains the substring (ignoring case), <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
999    */
1000   public static boolean containsIgnoreCase(String str, String search) {
1001      if (str == null || search == null)
1002         return false;
1003      return str.toLowerCase().contains(search.toLowerCase());
1004   }
1005
1006   /**
1007    * Counts the number of occurrences of the specified character in the specified string.
1008    *
1009    * <p>
1010    * Returns <c>0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
1011    *
1012    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1013    * <p class='bjava'>
1014    *    countChars(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'o'</js>);   <jc>// 2</jc>
1015    *    countChars(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'x'</js>);   <jc>// 0</jc>
1016    *    countChars(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);            <jc>// 0</jc>
1017    * </p>
1018    *
1019    * @param s The string to check.
1020    * @param c The character to count.
1021    * @return The number of occurrences of the character, or <c>0</c> if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
1022    */
1023   public static int countChars(String s, char c) {
1024      var count = 0;
1025      if (s == null)
1026         return count;
1027      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++)
1028         if (s.charAt(i) == c)
1029            count++;
1030      return count;
1031   }
1032
1033   /**
1034    * Counts the number of occurrences of a substring within a string.
1035    *
1036    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1037    * <p class='bjava'>
1038    *    countMatches(<js>"hello world world"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// 2</jc>
1039    *    countMatches(<js>"ababab"</js>, <js>"ab"</js>);                    <jc>// 3</jc>
1040    *    countMatches(<js>"hello"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);                    <jc>// 0</jc>
1041    * </p>
1042    *
1043    * @param str The string to search in.
1044    * @param search The substring to count.
1045    * @return The number of occurrences, or <c>0</c> if not found or if either parameter is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1046    */
1047   public static int countMatches(String str, String search) {
1048      if (isEmpty(str) || isEmpty(search))
1049         return 0;
1050      var count = 0;
1051      var index = 0;
1052      while ((index = str.indexOf(search, index)) != -1) {
1053         count++;
1054         index += search.length();
1055      }
1056      return count;
1057   }
1058
1059   /**
1060    * Debug method for rendering non-ASCII character sequences.
1061    *
1062    * <p>
1063    * Converts non-printable and non-ASCII characters (outside the range <c>0x20-0x7E</c>) to hexadecimal
1064    * sequences in the format <js>"[hex]"</js>. Printable ASCII characters are left unchanged.
1065    *
1066    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1067    * <p class='bjava'>
1068    *    decodeHex(<js>"Hello"</js>);              <jc>// "Hello"</jc>
1069    *    decodeHex(<js>"Hello\u0000World"</js>);   <jc>// "Hello[0]World"</jc>
1070    *    decodeHex(<js>"Hello\u00A9"</js>);        <jc>// "Hello[a9]"</jc>
1071    *    decodeHex(<jk>null</jk>);                 <jc>// null</jc>
1072    * </p>
1073    *
1074    * @param s The string to decode.
1075    * @return A string with non-ASCII characters converted to <js>"[hex]"</js> sequences, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1076    */
1077   public static String decodeHex(String s) {
1078      if (s == null)
1079         return null;
1080      var sb = new StringBuilder();
1081      for (var c : s.toCharArray()) {
1082         if (c < ' ' || c > '~')
1083            sb.append("[").append(Integer.toHexString(c)).append("]");
1084         else
1085            sb.append(c);
1086      }
1087      return sb.toString();
1088   }
1089
1090   /**
1091    * Decompresses a GZIP-compressed byte array into a UTF-8 string.
1092    *
1093    * <p>
1094    * This method is the inverse of {@link #compress(String)}. It takes a byte array that was compressed
1095    * using GZIP compression and decompresses it into a UTF-8 encoded string.
1096    *
1097    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1098    * <p class='bjava'>
1099    *    <jc>// Compress a string</jc>
1100    *    byte[] <jv>compressed</jv> = compress(<js>"Hello World"</js>);
1101    *
1102    *    <jc>// Decompress it back</jc>
1103    *    String <jv>decompressed</jv> = decompress(<jv>compressed</jv>);
1104    *    <jc>// Returns: "Hello World"</jc>
1105    * </p>
1106    *
1107    * @param is The GZIP-compressed byte array to decompress.
1108    * @return The decompressed UTF-8 string.
1109    * @throws java.io.IOException If decompression fails or the input is not valid GZIP data.
1110    * @see #compress(String)
1111    */
1112   public static String decompress(byte[] is) throws java.io.IOException {
1113      return read(new GZIPInputStream(new ByteArrayInputStream(is)));
1114   }
1115
1116   /**
1117    * Returns the specified string, or the default string if that string is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
1118    *
1119    * @param str The string value to check.
1120    * @param defaultStr The default string to return if the string is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
1121    * @return The string value, or the default string if the string is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
1122    */
1123   public static String defaultIfBlank(String str, String defaultStr) {
1124      return isBlank(str) ? defaultStr : str;
1125   }
1126
1127   /**
1128    * Returns the specified string, or the default string if that string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1129    *
1130    * @param str The string value to check.
1131    * @param defaultStr The default string to return if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1132    * @return The string value, or the default string if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1133    */
1134   public static String defaultIfEmpty(String str, String defaultStr) {
1135      return isEmpty(str) ? defaultStr : str;
1136   }
1137
1138   /**
1139    * Finds the position where the two strings first differ.
1140    *
1141    * <p>
1142    * This method compares strings character by character and returns the index of the first position
1143    * where they differ. If the strings are equal, returns <c>-1</c>. If one string is a prefix of the other,
1144    * returns the length of the shorter string.
1145    *
1146    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1147    * <p class='bjava'>
1148    *    diffPosition(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);      <jc>// -1 (equal)</jc>
1149    *    diffPosition(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"apricot"</js>);    <jc>// 2 (differs at 'p' vs 'r')</jc>
1150    *    diffPosition(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"app"</js>);        <jc>// 3 (shorter string ends here)</jc>
1151    *    diffPosition(<js>"app"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);        <jc>// 3 (shorter string ends here)</jc>
1152    * </p>
1153    *
1154    * @param s1 The first string.
1155    * @param s2 The second string.
1156    * @return The position where the two strings differ, or <c>-1</c> if they're equal.
1157    * @see #diffPositionIc(String, String)
1158    */
1159   public static int diffPosition(String s1, String s2) {
1160      s1 = emptyIfNull(s1);
1161      s2 = emptyIfNull(s2);
1162      var i = 0;
1163      var len = Math.min(s1.length(), s2.length());
1164      while (i < len) {
1165         var j = s1.charAt(i) - s2.charAt(i);
1166         if (j != 0)
1167            return i;
1168         i++;
1169      }
1170      if (eq(s1.length(), s2.length()))
1171         return -1;
1172      return i;
1173   }
1174
1175   /**
1176    * Finds the position where the two strings first differ, ignoring case.
1177    *
1178    * <p>
1179    * This method compares strings character by character (case-insensitive) and returns the index of the first position
1180    * where they differ. If the strings are equal (ignoring case), returns <c>-1</c>. If one string is a prefix of the other,
1181    * returns the length of the shorter string.
1182    *
1183    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1184    * <p class='bjava'>
1185    *    diffPositionIc(<js>"Apple"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>);      <jc>// -1 (equal ignoring case)</jc>
1186    *    diffPositionIc(<js>"Apple"</js>, <js>"Apricot"</js>);    <jc>// 2 (differs at 'p' vs 'r')</jc>
1187    *    diffPositionIc(<js>"APPLE"</js>, <js>"app"</js>);        <jc>// 3 (shorter string ends here)</jc>
1188    * </p>
1189    *
1190    * @param s1 The first string.
1191    * @param s2 The second string.
1192    * @return The position where the two strings differ, or <c>-1</c> if they're equal (ignoring case).
1193    * @see #diffPosition(String, String)
1194    */
1195   public static int diffPositionIc(String s1, String s2) {
1196      s1 = emptyIfNull(s1);
1197      s2 = emptyIfNull(s2);
1198      var i = 0;
1199      var len = Math.min(s1.length(), s2.length());
1200      while (i < len) {
1201         var j = toLowerCase(s1.charAt(i)) - toLowerCase(s2.charAt(i));
1202         if (j != 0)
1203            return i;
1204         i++;
1205      }
1206      if (eq(s1.length(), s2.length()))
1207         return -1;
1208      return i;
1209   }
1210
1211   /**
1212    * Removes duplicate elements from a string array, preserving order.
1213    *
1214    * <p>
1215    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
1216    * Uses a {@link LinkedHashSet} to preserve insertion order while removing duplicates.
1217    *
1218    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1219    * <p class='bjava'>
1220    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>, <js>"foo"</js>, <js>"baz"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>};
1221    *    String[] <jv>unique</jv> = distinct(<jv>array</jv>);
1222    *    <jc>// Returns: ["foo", "bar", "baz"]</jc>
1223    * </p>
1224    *
1225    * @param array The array to process. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1226    * @return A new array with duplicate elements removed, or an empty array if the array was <jk>null</jk>.
1227    */
1228   public static String[] distinct(String[] array) {
1229      if (array == null)
1230         return new String[0];
1231      return Arrays.stream(array).collect(Collectors.toCollection(LinkedHashSet::new)).toArray(new String[0]);
1232   }
1233
1234   /**
1235    * Generates a Double Metaphone code for a string.
1236    *
1237    * <p>
1238    * Double Metaphone is an improved version of Metaphone that returns two codes:
1239    * a primary code and an alternate code. This handles more edge cases and variations.
1240    *
1241    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1242    * <p class='bjava'>
1243    *    doubleMetaphone(<js>"Smith"</js>);   <jc>// "SM0"</jc>
1244    *    doubleMetaphone(<js>"Schmidt"</js>); <jc>// "XMT"</jc>
1245    * </p>
1246    *
1247    * @param str The string to generate a Double Metaphone code for. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1248    * @return An array with two elements: [primary code, alternate code]. Returns an empty array if input is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1249    */
1250   public static String[] doubleMetaphone(String str) {
1251      if (isEmpty(str))
1252         return new String[0];
1253
1254      // For simplicity, return the same code for both primary and alternate
1255      // A full Double Metaphone implementation would be much more complex
1256      var primary = metaphone(str);
1257
1258      // Generate alternate code (simplified - full implementation would have different rules)
1259      var alternate = primary;
1260
1261      return a(primary, alternate);
1262   }
1263
1264   /**
1265    * Returns the specified string, or an empty string if that string is <jk>null</jk>.
1266    *
1267    * @param str The string value to check.
1268    * @return The string value, or an empty string if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
1269    */
1270   public static String emptyIfNull(String str) {
1271      return str == null ? "" : str;
1272   }
1273
1274   /**
1275    * Checks if a string ends with the specified character.
1276    *
1277    * <p>
1278    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1279    *
1280    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1281    * <p class='bjava'>
1282    *    endsWith(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>'o'</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
1283    *    endsWith(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>'H'</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
1284    *    endsWith(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'o'</js>);        <jc>// false</jc>
1285    *    endsWith(<js>""</js>, <js>'o'</js>);          <jc>// false</jc>
1286    * </p>
1287    *
1288    * @param s The string to check. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1289    * @param c The character to check for.
1290    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified string is not <jk>null</jk> and ends with the specified character.
1291    * @see #endsWith(String, String)
1292    * @see #endsWithAny(String, char...)
1293    * @see String#endsWith(String)
1294    */
1295   public static boolean endsWith(String s, char c) {
1296      if (nn(s)) {
1297         var i = s.length();
1298         if (i > 0)
1299            return s.charAt(i - 1) == c;
1300      }
1301      return false;
1302   }
1303
1304   /**
1305    * Checks if a string ends with the specified string.
1306    *
1307    * <p>
1308    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns <jk>false</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk>.
1309    * Otherwise behaves the same as {@link String#endsWith(String)}.
1310    *
1311    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1312    * <p class='bjava'>
1313    *    endsWith(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
1314    *    endsWith(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
1315    *    endsWith(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"World"</js>);            <jc>// false</jc>
1316    * </p>
1317    *
1318    * @param s The string to check. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1319    * @param suffix The suffix to check for.
1320    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string ends with the specified suffix.
1321    * @see #endsWith(String, char)
1322    * @see #endsWithAny(String, String...)
1323    * @see String#endsWith(String)
1324    */
1325   public static boolean endsWith(String s, String suffix) {
1326      return s != null && s.endsWith(suffix);
1327   }
1328
1329   /**
1330    * Checks if a string ends with any of the specified characters.
1331    *
1332    * <p>
1333    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns <jk>false</jk> if:
1334    * <ul>
1335    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
1336    *   <li>The characters array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
1337    *   <li>The string does not end with any of the specified characters</li>
1338    * </ul>
1339    *
1340    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1341    * <p class='bjava'>
1342    *    endsWithAny(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>'o'</js>, <js>'x'</js>);     <jc>// true (ends with 'o')</jc>
1343    *    endsWithAny(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>'x'</js>, <js>'y'</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
1344    *    endsWithAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'o'</js>);                      <jc>// false</jc>
1345    * </p>
1346    *
1347    * @param s The string to check. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1348    * @param c The characters to check for.
1349    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string ends with any of the specified characters.
1350    * @see #endsWith(String, char)
1351    * @see #endsWithAny(String, String...)
1352    */
1353   public static boolean endsWithAny(String s, char...c) {
1354      if (nn(s)) {
1355         var i = s.length();
1356         if (i > 0) {
1357            var c2 = s.charAt(i - 1);
1358            for (var cc : c)
1359               if (c2 == cc)
1360                  return true;
1361         }
1362      }
1363      return false;
1364   }
1365
1366   /**
1367    * Checks if a string ends with any of the specified strings.
1368    *
1369    * <p>
1370    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns <jk>false</jk> if:
1371    * <ul>
1372    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
1373    *   <li>The suffixes array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
1374    *   <li>The string does not end with any of the specified suffixes</li>
1375    * </ul>
1376    *
1377    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1378    * <p class='bjava'>
1379    *    endsWithAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);   <jc>// true (ends with "World")</jc>
1380    *    endsWithAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
1381    *    endsWithAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"World"</js>);                            <jc>// false</jc>
1382    * </p>
1383    *
1384    * @param s The string to check. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1385    * @param suffixes The suffixes to check for.
1386    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string ends with any of the specified suffixes.
1387    * @see #endsWith(String, String)
1388    * @see #endsWithAny(String, char...)
1389    * @see String#endsWith(String)
1390    */
1391   public static boolean endsWithAny(String s, String...suffixes) {
1392      if (s == null || suffixes == null || suffixes.length == 0)
1393         return false;
1394      for (var suffix : suffixes) {
1395         if (s.endsWith(suffix))
1396            return true;
1397      }
1398      return false;
1399   }
1400
1401   /**
1402    * Checks if a string ends with a suffix, ignoring case.
1403    *
1404    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1405    * <p class='bjava'>
1406    *    endsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
1407    *    endsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"WORLD"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
1408    *    endsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
1409    * </p>
1410    *
1411    * @param str The string to check.
1412    * @param suffix The suffix to check for.
1413    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string ends with the suffix (ignoring case), <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
1414    */
1415   public static boolean endsWithIgnoreCase(String str, String suffix) {
1416      if (str == null || suffix == null)
1417         return false;
1418      return str.toLowerCase().endsWith(suffix.toLowerCase());
1419   }
1420
1421   /**
1422    * Calculates the entropy of a string.
1423    *
1424    * <p>
1425    * Entropy measures the randomness or information content of a string.
1426    * Higher entropy indicates more randomness. The formula used is:
1427    * <c>H(X) = -Σ P(x) * log2(P(x))</c>
1428    * where P(x) is the probability of character x.
1429    *
1430    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1431    * <p class='bjava'>
1432    *    entropy(<js>"aaaa"</js>);        <jc>// 0.0 (no randomness)</jc>
1433    *    entropy(<js>"abcd"</js>);        <jc>// 2.0 (high randomness)</jc>
1434    *    entropy(<js>"hello"</js>);       <jc>// ~2.32</jc>
1435    * </p>
1436    *
1437    * @param str The string to calculate entropy for. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1438    * @return The entropy value (0.0 or higher), or <c>0.0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1439    */
1440   public static double entropy(String str) {
1441      if (isEmpty(str))
1442         return 0.0;
1443
1444      var length = str.length();
1445
1446      // Count character frequencies
1447      var charCounts = new int[Character.MAX_VALUE + 1];
1448      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
1449         charCounts[str.charAt(i)]++;
1450      }
1451
1452      // Calculate entropy
1453      var entropy = 0.0;
1454      for (var count : charCounts) {
1455         if (count > 0) {
1456            var probability = (double)count / length;
1457            entropy -= probability * (Math.log(probability) / Math.log(2.0));
1458         }
1459      }
1460
1461      return entropy;
1462   }
1463
1464   /**
1465    * Tests two objects for case-insensitive string equality.
1466    *
1467    * <p>
1468    * Converts both objects to strings using {@link Object#toString()} before comparison.
1469    * This method handles <jk>null</jk> values gracefully:
1470    * <ul>
1471    *   <li>Both <jk>null</jk> → returns <jk>true</jk></li>
1472    *   <li>One <jk>null</jk> → returns <jk>false</jk></li>
1473    *   <li>Neither <jk>null</jk> → compares string representations ignoring case</li>
1474    * </ul>
1475    *
1476    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1477    * <p class='bjava'>
1478    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"HELLO"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
1479    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
1480    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<jk>null</jk>, <jk>null</jk>);            <jc>// true</jc>
1481    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);         <jc>// false</jc>
1482    *    equalsIgnoreCase(123, <js>"123"</js>);                    <jc>// true (converts 123 to "123")</jc>
1483    * </p>
1484    *
1485    * @param a Object 1.
1486    * @param b Object 2.
1487    * @return <jk>true</jk> if both objects are equal ignoring case.
1488    * @see #equalsIgnoreCase(String, String)
1489    * @see Utils#eqic(Object, Object)
1490    */
1491   public static boolean equalsIgnoreCase(Object a, Object b) {
1492      if (a == null && b == null)
1493         return true;
1494      if (a == null || b == null)
1495         return false;
1496      return a.toString().equalsIgnoreCase(b.toString());
1497   }
1498
1499   /**
1500    * Tests two strings for case-insensitive equality, but gracefully handles nulls.
1501    *
1502    * <p>
1503    * This method handles <jk>null</jk> values gracefully:
1504    * <ul>
1505    *   <li>Both <jk>null</jk> → returns <jk>true</jk> (same reference check)</li>
1506    *   <li>One <jk>null</jk> → returns <jk>false</jk></li>
1507    *   <li>Neither <jk>null</jk> → compares strings ignoring case</li>
1508    * </ul>
1509    *
1510    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1511    * <p class='bjava'>
1512    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
1513    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"WORLD"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
1514    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<jk>null</jk>, <jk>null</jk>);           <jc>// true</jc>
1515    *    equalsIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);        <jc>// false</jc>
1516    * </p>
1517    *
1518    * @param str1 The first string.
1519    * @param str2 The second string.
1520    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the strings are equal ignoring case, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
1521    * @see #equalsIgnoreCase(Object, Object)
1522    * @see Utils#eqic(String, String)
1523    */
1524   public static boolean equalsIgnoreCase(String str1, String str2) {
1525      if (Objects.equals(str1, str2))
1526         return true;
1527      if (str1 == null || str2 == null)
1528         return false;
1529      return str1.equalsIgnoreCase(str2);
1530   }
1531
1532   /**
1533    * Escapes the specified characters in the string.
1534    *
1535    * @param s The string with characters to escape.
1536    * @param escaped The characters to escape.
1537    * @return The string with characters escaped, or the same string if no escapable characters were found.
1538    */
1539   public static String escapeChars(String s, AsciiSet escaped) {
1540      if (s == null || s.isEmpty())
1541         return s;
1542
1543      var count = 0;
1544      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++)
1545         if (escaped.contains(s.charAt(i)))
1546            count++;
1547      if (count == 0)
1548         return s;
1549
1550      var sb = new StringBuffer(s.length() + count);
1551      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
1552         var c = s.charAt(i);
1553         if (escaped.contains(c))
1554            sb.append('\\');
1555         sb.append(c);
1556      }
1557      return sb.toString();
1558   }
1559
1560   /**
1561    * Escapes a string for safe inclusion in Java source code.
1562    *
1563    * <p>This method converts special characters to their Java escape sequences and
1564    * converts non-printable ASCII characters to Unicode escape sequences.
1565    *
1566    * <h5 class='section'>Escape mappings:</h5>
1567    * <ul>
1568    *   <li>{@code "} → {@code \"}</li>
1569    *   <li>{@code \} → {@code \\}</li>
1570    *   <li>{@code \n} → {@code \\n}</li>
1571    *   <li>{@code \r} → {@code \\r}</li>
1572    *   <li>{@code \t} → {@code \\t}</li>
1573    *   <li>{@code \f} → {@code \\f}</li>
1574    *   <li>{@code \b} → {@code \\b}</li>
1575    *   <li>Non-printable characters → {@code \\uXXXX}</li>
1576    * </ul>
1577    *
1578    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1579    * <p class='bjava'>
1580    *   <jk>var</jk> <jv>escaped</jv> = <jsm>escapeForJava</jsm>(<js>"Hello\nWorld\"Test\""</js>);
1581    *   <jc>// Returns: "Hello\\nWorld\\\"Test\\\""</jc>
1582    * </p>
1583    *
1584    * @param s The string to escape.
1585    * @return The escaped string safe for Java source code, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1586    */
1587   public static String escapeForJava(String s) {
1588      if (s == null)
1589         return null;
1590      var sb = new StringBuilder();
1591      for (var c : s.toCharArray()) {
1592         sb.append(switch (c) {
1593            case '\"' -> "\\\"";
1594            case '\\' -> "\\\\";
1595            case '\n' -> "\\n";
1596            case '\r' -> "\\r";
1597            case '\t' -> "\\t";
1598            case '\f' -> "\\f";
1599            case '\b' -> "\\b";
1600            default -> {
1601               if (c < 0x20 || c > 0x7E)
1602                  yield String.format("\\u%04x", (int)c);
1603               yield String.valueOf(c);
1604            }
1605         });
1606      }
1607      return sb.toString();
1608   }
1609
1610   /**
1611    * Escapes HTML entities in a string.
1612    *
1613    * <p>
1614    * Escapes the following characters:
1615    * <ul>
1616    *   <li><js>'&amp;'</js> → <js>"&amp;amp;"</js></li>
1617    *   <li><js>'&lt;'</js> → <js>"&amp;lt;"</js></li>
1618    *   <li><js>'&gt;'</js> → <js>"&amp;gt;"</js></li>
1619    *   <li><js>'"'</js> → <js>"&amp;quot;"</js></li>
1620    *   <li><js>'\''</js> → <js>"&amp;#39;"</js></li>
1621    * </ul>
1622    *
1623    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1624    * <p class='bjava'>
1625    *    escapeHtml(<js>"&lt;script&gt;alert('xss')&lt;/script&gt;"</js>);
1626    *    <jc>// Returns: "&amp;lt;script&amp;gt;alert(&amp;#39;xss&amp;#39;)&amp;lt;/script&amp;gt;"</jc>
1627    * </p>
1628    *
1629    * @param str The string to escape.
1630    * @return The escaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1631    */
1632   public static String escapeHtml(String str) {
1633      if (str == null)
1634         return null;
1635      var sb = new StringBuilder(str.length() * 2);
1636      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
1637         var c = str.charAt(i);
1638         switch (c) {
1639            case '&' -> sb.append("&amp;");
1640            case '<' -> sb.append("&lt;");
1641            case '>' -> sb.append("&gt;");
1642            case '"' -> sb.append("&quot;");
1643            case '\'' -> sb.append("&#39;");
1644            default -> sb.append(c);
1645         }
1646      }
1647      return sb.toString();
1648   }
1649
1650   /**
1651    * Escapes regex special characters in a string.
1652    *
1653    * <p>
1654    * Escapes the following regex special characters: <js>\.*+?^${}()[]|\\</js>
1655    *
1656    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1657    * <p class='bjava'>
1658    *    escapeRegex(<js>"file.txt"</js>);        <jc>// Returns: "file\\.txt"</jc>
1659    *    escapeRegex(<js>"price: $10.99"</js>);   <jc>// Returns: "price: \\$10\\.99"</jc>
1660    * </p>
1661    *
1662    * @param str The string to escape.
1663    * @return The escaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1664    */
1665   public static String escapeRegex(String str) {
1666      if (str == null)
1667         return null;
1668      // Escape regex special characters: . * + ? ^ $ { } ( ) [ ] | \
1669      return str.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace(".", "\\.").replace("*", "\\*").replace("+", "\\+").replace("?", "\\?").replace("^", "\\^").replace("$", "\\$").replace("{", "\\{").replace("}", "\\}")
1670         .replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)").replace("[", "\\[").replace("]", "\\]").replace("|", "\\|");
1671   }
1672
1673   /**
1674    * Escapes SQL string literals by doubling single quotes.
1675    *
1676    * <p>
1677    * Basic SQL escaping for string literals. Escapes single quotes by doubling them.
1678    * This is a basic implementation - for production use, consider using prepared statements.
1679    *
1680    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1681    * <p class='bjava'>
1682    *    escapeSql(<js>"O'Brien"</js>);     <jc>// Returns: "O''Brien"</jc>
1683    *    escapeSql(<js>"It's a test"</js>); <jc>// Returns: "It''s a test"</jc>
1684    * </p>
1685    *
1686    * @param str The string to escape.
1687    * @return The escaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1688    */
1689   public static String escapeSql(String str) {
1690      if (str == null)
1691         return null;
1692      return str.replace("'", "''");
1693   }
1694
1695   /**
1696    * Escapes XML entities in a string.
1697    *
1698    * <p>
1699    * Escapes the following characters:
1700    * <ul>
1701    *   <li><js>'&amp;'</js> → <js>"&amp;amp;"</js></li>
1702    *   <li><js>'&lt;'</js> → <js>"&amp;lt;"</js></li>
1703    *   <li><js>'&gt;'</js> → <js>"&amp;gt;"</js></li>
1704    *   <li><js>'"'</js> → <js>"&amp;quot;"</js></li>
1705    *   <li><js>'\''</js> → <js>"&amp;apos;"</js></li>
1706    * </ul>
1707    *
1708    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1709    * <p class='bjava'>
1710    *    escapeXml(<js>"&lt;tag attr='value'&gt;text&lt;/tag&gt;"</js>);
1711    *    <jc>// Returns: "&amp;lt;tag attr=&amp;apos;value&amp;apos;&amp;gt;text&amp;lt;/tag&amp;gt;"</jc>
1712    * </p>
1713    *
1714    * @param str The string to escape.
1715    * @return The escaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
1716    */
1717   public static String escapeXml(String str) {
1718      if (str == null)
1719         return null;
1720      var sb = new StringBuilder(str.length() * 2);
1721      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
1722         var c = str.charAt(i);
1723         switch (c) {
1724            case '&' -> sb.append("&amp;");
1725            case '<' -> sb.append("&lt;");
1726            case '>' -> sb.append("&gt;");
1727            case '"' -> sb.append("&quot;");
1728            case '\'' -> sb.append("&apos;");
1729            default -> sb.append(c);
1730         }
1731      }
1732      return sb.toString();
1733   }
1734
1735   /**
1736    * Extracts all text segments between start and end markers.
1737    *
1738    * <p>
1739    * Finds all occurrences of text between the start and end markers (non-overlapping).
1740    *
1741    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1742    * <p class='bjava'>
1743    *    extractBetween(<js>"&lt;tag&gt;content&lt;/tag&gt;"</js>, <js>"&lt;"</js>, <js>"&gt;"</js>);
1744    *    <jc>// ["tag", "/tag"]</jc>
1745    *    extractBetween(<js>"[one][two][three]"</js>, <js>"["</js>, <js>"]"</js>);
1746    *    <jc>// ["one", "two", "three"]</jc>
1747    * </p>
1748    *
1749    * @param str The string to extract from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1750    * @param start The start marker. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1751    * @param end The end marker. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1752    * @return A list of text segments found between the markers, or an empty list if any parameter is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1753    */
1754   @SuppressWarnings({
1755      "java:S135" // Multiple break statements in mutually exclusive branches - necessary for early termination
1756   })
1757   public static List<String> extractBetween(String str, String start, String end) {
1758      if (isEmpty(str) || isEmpty(start) || isEmpty(end))
1759         return Collections.emptyList();
1760
1761      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
1762      var startIndex = 0;
1763      while (true) {
1764         var startPos = str.indexOf(start, startIndex);
1765         if (startPos == -1)
1766            break;
1767         var endPos = str.indexOf(end, startPos + start.length());
1768         if (endPos == -1)
1769            break;
1770         result.add(str.substring(startPos + start.length(), endPos));
1771         startIndex = endPos + end.length();
1772      }
1773      return result;
1774   }
1775
1776   /**
1777    * Extracts all email addresses from a string.
1778    *
1779    * <p>
1780    * Uses a basic email regex pattern to find email addresses.
1781    *
1782    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1783    * <p class='bjava'>
1784    *    extractEmails(<js>"Contact: user@example.com or admin@test.org"</js>);
1785    *    <jc>// ["user@example.com", "admin@test.org"]</jc>
1786    * </p>
1787    *
1788    * @param str The string to extract emails from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1789    * @return A list of email addresses found in the input, or an empty list if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1790    */
1791   public static List<String> extractEmails(String str) {
1792      if (isEmpty(str))
1793         return Collections.emptyList();
1794
1795      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
1796      // Email regex pattern (same as isEmail but without ^ and $ anchors)
1797      var pattern = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9._%+-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9-]+(?:\\.[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)*\\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}");
1798      var matcher = pattern.matcher(str);
1799      while (matcher.find()) {
1800         result.add(matcher.group());
1801      }
1802      return result;
1803   }
1804
1805   /**
1806    * Extracts all numeric sequences from a string.
1807    *
1808    * <p>
1809    * Finds all sequences of digits (including decimal numbers with dots).
1810    *
1811    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1812    * <p class='bjava'>
1813    *    extractNumbers(<js>"Price: $19.99, Quantity: 5"</js>);
1814    *    <jc>// ["19.99", "5"]</jc>
1815    *    extractNumbers(<js>"Version 1.2.3"</js>);
1816    *    <jc>// ["1.2", "3"]</jc>
1817    * </p>
1818    *
1819    * @param str The string to extract numbers from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1820    * @return A list of numeric strings found in the input, or an empty list if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1821    */
1822   public static List<String> extractNumbers(String str) {
1823      if (isEmpty(str))
1824         return Collections.emptyList();
1825
1826      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
1827      var pattern = Pattern.compile("\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?");
1828      var matcher = pattern.matcher(str);
1829      while (matcher.find()) {
1830         result.add(matcher.group());
1831      }
1832      return result;
1833   }
1834
1835   /**
1836    * Extracts all URLs from a string.
1837    *
1838    * <p>
1839    * Uses a basic URL regex pattern to find URLs (http, https, ftp).
1840    *
1841    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1842    * <p class='bjava'>
1843    *    extractUrls(<js>"Visit https://example.com or http://test.org"</js>);
1844    *    <jc>// ["https://example.com", "http://test.org"]</jc>
1845    * </p>
1846    *
1847    * @param str The string to extract URLs from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1848    * @return A list of URLs found in the input, or an empty list if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1849    */
1850   public static List<String> extractUrls(String str) {
1851      if (isEmpty(str))
1852         return Collections.emptyList();
1853
1854      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
1855      // Basic URL pattern: protocol://domain/path
1856      var pattern = Pattern.compile("(?:https?|ftp)://[\\w\\-.~:/?#\\[\\]@!$&'()*+,;=%]+", Pattern.CASE_INSENSITIVE);
1857      var matcher = pattern.matcher(str);
1858      while (matcher.find()) {
1859         result.add(matcher.group());
1860      }
1861      return result;
1862   }
1863
1864   /**
1865    * Extracts all words from a string.
1866    *
1867    * <p>
1868    * A word is defined as a sequence of letters, digits, and underscores.
1869    *
1870    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1871    * <p class='bjava'>
1872    *    extractWords(<js>"Hello world! This is a test."</js>);
1873    *    <jc>// ["Hello", "world", "This", "is", "a", "test"]</jc>
1874    * </p>
1875    *
1876    * @param str The string to extract words from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1877    * @return A list of words found in the input, or an empty list if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1878    */
1879   public static List<String> extractWords(String str) {
1880      if (isEmpty(str))
1881         return Collections.emptyList();
1882
1883      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
1884      // Word pattern: sequence of word characters (letters, digits, underscore)
1885      var pattern = Pattern.compile("\\w+");
1886      var matcher = pattern.matcher(str);
1887      while (matcher.find()) {
1888         result.add(matcher.group());
1889      }
1890      return result;
1891   }
1892
1893   /**
1894    * Filters a string array using the specified predicate.
1895    *
1896    * <p>
1897    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
1898    * Returns an empty array if the predicate is <jk>null</jk> or no elements match.
1899    *
1900    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1901    * <p class='bjava'>
1902    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"foo"</js>, <js>""</js>, <js>"bar"</js>, <jk>null</jk>, <js>"baz"</js>};
1903    *    String[] <jv>filtered</jv> = filter(<jv>array</jv>, StringUtils.<jsf>NOT_EMPTY</jsf>);
1904    *    <jc>// Returns: ["foo", "bar", "baz"]</jc>
1905    *
1906    *    String[] <jv>longStrings</jv> = filter(<jv>array</jv>, s -&gt; s != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; s.length() &gt; 3);
1907    *    <jc>// Returns: ["baz"]</jc>
1908    * </p>
1909    *
