3844    *   <li>Hyphen-separated: <js>"00-1B-44-11-3A-B7"</js></li>
3845    *   <li>No separators: <js>"001B44113AB7"</js></li>
3846    * </ul>
3847    *
3848    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3849    * <p class='bjava'>
3850    *    isValidMacAddress(<js>"00:1B:44:11:3A:B7"</js>);  <jc>// true</jc>
3851    *    isValidMacAddress(<js>"00-1B-44-11-3A-B7"</js>);  <jc>// true</jc>
3852    *    isValidMacAddress(<js>"001B44113AB7"</js>);       <jc>// true</jc>
3853    *    isValidMacAddress(<js>"00:1B:44:11:3A"</js>);     <jc>// false (too short)</jc>
3854    * </p>
3855    *
3856    * @param mac The MAC address string to validate. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
3857    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string is a valid MAC address, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
3858    */
3859   public static boolean isValidMacAddress(String mac) {
3860      if (isEmpty(mac))
3861         return false;
3862
3863      // Remove separators and check if it's 12 hex digits
3864      var cleaned = mac.replaceAll("[:-]", "").toUpperCase();
3865      if (cleaned.length() != 12)
3866         return false;
3867
3868      // Check if all characters are valid hex digits
3869      return cleaned.matches("^[0-9A-F]{12}$");
3870   }
3871
3872   /**
3873    * Validates if a string is a valid regular expression pattern.
3874    *
3875    * <p>
3876    * Attempts to compile the regex pattern to verify it's syntactically correct.
3877    *
3878    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3879    * <p class='bjava'>
3880    *    isValidRegex(<js>"[a-z]+"</js>);        <jc>// true</jc>
3881    *    isValidRegex(<js>"[a-z"</js>);          <jc>// false (unclosed bracket)</jc>
3882    *    isValidRegex(<js>"(test"</js>);         <jc>// false (unclosed parenthesis)</jc>
3883    * </p>
3884    *
3885    * @param regex The regex pattern to validate. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
3886    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string is a valid regex pattern, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
3887    */
3888   public static boolean isValidRegex(String regex) {
3889      if (isEmpty(regex))
3890         return false;
3891      try {
3892         Pattern.compile(regex);
3893         return true;
3894      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") PatternSyntaxException e) {
3895         return false;
3896      }
3897   }
3898
3899   /**
3900    * Validates if a time string matches the specified time format.
3901    *
3902    * <p>
3903    * Uses {@link SimpleDateFormat} to parse the time string according to the format pattern.
3904    *
3905    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3906    * <p class='bjava'>
3907    *    isValidTimeFormat(<js>"14:30:00"</js>, <js>"HH:mm:ss"</js>);  <jc>// true</jc>
3908    *    isValidTimeFormat(<js>"2:30 PM"</js>, <js>"h:mm a"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
3909    *    isValidTimeFormat(<js>"25:00:00"</js>, <js>"HH:mm:ss"</js>);  <jc>// false (invalid hour)</jc>
3910    * </p>
3911    *
3912    * @param timeStr The time string to validate. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
3913    * @param format The time format pattern (e.g., "HH:mm:ss"). Can be <jk>null</jk>.
3914    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the time string matches the format, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
3915    */
3916   public static boolean isValidTimeFormat(String timeStr, String format) {
3917      if (isEmpty(timeStr) || isEmpty(format))
3918         return false;
3919      try {
3920         var sdf = new SimpleDateFormat(format);
3921         sdf.setLenient(false); // Strict parsing
3922         sdf.parse(timeStr);
3923         return true;
3924      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") ParseException | IllegalArgumentException e) {
3925         // IllegalArgumentException thrown for invalid format patterns
3926         return false;
3927      }
3928   }
3929
3930   /**
3931    * Checks if a character is whitespace.
3932    *
3933    * @param c The character to check.
3934    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the character is whitespace.
3935    */
3936   public static boolean isWhitespace(int c) {
3937      return Character.isWhitespace(c);
3938   }
3939
3940   /**
3941    * Checks if a string contains only whitespace characters.
3942    *
3943    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3944    * <p class='bjava'>
3945    *    isWhitespace(<jk>null</jk>);         <jc>// false</jc>
3946    *    isWhitespace(<js>""</js>);           <jc>// true</jc>
3947    *    isWhitespace(<js>"   "</js>);        <jc>// true</jc>
3948    *    isWhitespace(<js>"\t\n"</js>);       <jc>// true</jc>
3949    *    isWhitespace(<js>" a "</js>);        <jc>// false</jc>
3950    * </p>
3951    *
3952    * @param str The string to check.
3953    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string is not null and contains only whitespace characters (or is empty).
3954    */
3955   public static boolean isWhitespace(String str) {
3956      if (str == null)
3957         return false;
3958      if (str.isEmpty())
3959         return true;
3960      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
3961         if (! isWhitespace(str.charAt(i)))
3962            return false;
3963      }
3964      return true;
3965   }
3966
3967   /**
3968    * Combines collection values into a simple comma-delimited string.
3969    *
3970    * @param values The values to join.
3971    * @return A comma-delimited string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
3972    */
3973   public static String join(Collection<?> values) {
3974      if (values == null)
3975         return null;
3976      return joine(toList(values), ',');
3977   }
3978
3979   /**
3980    * Join the specified tokens into a delimited string.
3981    *
3982    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
3983    * @param d The delimiter.
3984    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
3985    */
3986   public static String join(Collection<?> tokens, char d) {
3987      if (tokens == null)
3988         return null;
3989      var sb = new StringBuilder();
3990      for (var iter = tokens.iterator(); iter.hasNext();) {
3991         sb.append(iter.next());
3992         if (iter.hasNext())
3993            sb.append(d);
3994      }
3995      return sb.toString();
3996   }
3997
3998   /**
3999    * Join the specified tokens into a delimited string.
4000    *
4001    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4002    * @param d The delimiter.
4003    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
4004    */
4005   public static String join(Collection<?> tokens, String d) {
4006      if (tokens == null)
4007         return null;
4008      return StringUtils.join(tokens, d, new StringBuilder()).toString();
4009   }
4010
4011   /**
4012    * Joins the specified tokens into a delimited string and writes the output to the specified string builder.
4013    *
4014    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4015    * @param d The delimiter.
4016    * @param sb The string builder to append the response to.
4017    * @return The same string builder passed in as <c>sb</c>.
4018    */
4019   public static StringBuilder join(Collection<?> tokens, String d, StringBuilder sb) {
4020      if (tokens == null)
4021         return sb;
4022      for (var iter = tokens.iterator(); iter.hasNext();) {
4023         sb.append(iter.next());
4024         if (iter.hasNext())
4025            sb.append(d);
4026      }
4027      return sb;
4028   }
4029
4030   /**
4031    * Join the specified tokens into a delimited string.
4032    *
4033    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4034    * @param d The delimiter.
4035    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
4036    */
4037   public static String join(int[] tokens, char d) {
4038      if (tokens == null)
4039         return null;
4040      var sb = new StringBuilder();
4041      for (var i = 0; i < tokens.length; i++) {
4042         if (i > 0)
4043            sb.append(d);
4044         sb.append(tokens[i]);
4045      }
4046      return sb.toString();
4047   }
4048
4049   /**
4050    * Joins an array of integers with a delimiter.
4051    *
4052    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4053    * <p class='bjava'>
4054    *    join(<jk>new int</jk>[]{1, 2, 3}, <js>","</js>);   <jc>// "1,2,3"</jc>
4055    *    join(<jk>new int</jk>[]{}, <js>","</js>);          <jc>// ""</jc>
4056    * </p>
4057    *
4058    * @param array The array to join.
4059    * @param delimiter The delimiter string.
4060    * @return The joined string.
4061    */
4062   public static String join(int[] array, String delimiter) {
4063      if (array == null || array.length == 0)
4064         return "";
4065      if (delimiter == null)
4066         delimiter = "";
4067      return Arrays.stream(array).mapToObj(String::valueOf).collect(Collectors.joining(delimiter));
4068   }
4069
4070
4071   /**
4072    * Joins the specified tokens into a delimited string.
4073    *
4074    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4075    * @param d The delimiter.
4076    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
4077    */
4078   public static String join(Object[] tokens, char d) {
4079      if (tokens == null)
4080         return null;
4081      if (tokens.length == 1)
4082         return emptyIfNull(s(tokens[0]));
4083      return StringUtils.join(tokens, d, new StringBuilder()).toString();
4084   }
4085
4086   /**
4087    * Join the specified tokens into a delimited string and writes the output to the specified string builder.
4088    *
4089    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4090    * @param d The delimiter.
4091    * @param sb The string builder to append the response to.
4092    * @return The same string builder passed in as <c>sb</c>.
4093    */
4094   public static StringBuilder join(Object[] tokens, char d, StringBuilder sb) {
4095      if (tokens == null)
4096         return sb;
4097      for (var i = 0; i < tokens.length; i++) {
4098         if (i > 0)
4099            sb.append(d);
4100         sb.append(tokens[i]);
4101      }
4102      return sb;
4103   }
4104
4105   /**
4106    * Join the specified tokens into a delimited string.
4107    *
4108    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4109    * @param separator The delimiter.
4110    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
4111    */
4112   public static String join(Object[] tokens, String separator) {
4113      if (tokens == null)
4114         return null;
4115      var sb = new StringBuilder();
4116      for (var i = 0; i < tokens.length; i++) {
4117         if (i > 0)
4118            sb.append(separator);
4119         sb.append(tokens[i]);
4120      }
4121      return sb.toString();
4122   }
4123
4124   /**
4125    * Combines values into a simple comma-delimited string.
4126    *
4127    * @param values The values to join.
4128    * @return A comma-delimited string.
4129    */
4130   public static String join(String...values) {
4131      return join(values, ',');
4132   }
4133
4134   /**
4135    * Same as {@link StringUtils#join(Collection, char)} but escapes the delimiter if found in the tokens.
4136    *
4137    * @param tokens The tokens to join.
4138    * @param d The delimiter.
4139    * @return The delimited string.  If <c>tokens</c> is <jk>null</jk>, returns <jk>null</jk>.
4140    */
4141   public static String joine(List<?> tokens, char d) {
4142      if (tokens == null)
4143         return null;
4144      var as = getEscapeSet(d);
4145      var sb = new StringBuilder();
4146      for (int i = 0, j = tokens.size(); i < j; i++) {
4147         if (i > 0)
4148            sb.append(d);
4149         sb.append(escapeChars(s(tokens.get(i)), as));
4150      }
4151      return sb.toString();
4152   }
4153
4154   /**
4155    * Joins tokens with newlines.
4156    *
4157    * @param tokens The tokens to concatenate.
4158    * @return A string with the specified tokens contatenated with newlines.
4159    */
4160   public static String joinnl(Object[] tokens) {
4161      return join(tokens, '\n');
4162   }
4163
4164   /**
4165    * Converts a string to kebab-case format.
4166    *
4167    * <p>
4168    * Handles various input formats:
4169    * <ul>
4170    *   <li>Space-separated: "hello world" → "hello-world"</li>
4171    *   <li>CamelCase: "helloWorld" → "hello-world"</li>
4172    *   <li>PascalCase: "HelloWorld" → "hello-world"</li>
4173    *   <li>Snake_case: "hello_world" → "hello-world"</li>
4174    * </ul>
4175    *
4176    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4177    * <p class='bjava'>
4178    *    kebabCase(<jk>null</jk>);                    <jc>// null</jc>
4179    *    kebabCase(<js>""</js>);                      <jc>// ""</jc>
4180    *    kebabCase(<js>"hello world"</js>);           <jc>// "hello-world"</jc>
4181    *    kebabCase(<js>"helloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "hello-world"</jc>
4182    *    kebabCase(<js>"HelloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "hello-world"</jc>
4183    *    kebabCase(<js>"hello_world"</js>);           <jc>// "hello-world"</jc>
4184    * </p>
4185    *
4186    * @param str The string to convert.
4187    * @return The kebab-case string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
4188    */
4189   public static String kebabCase(String str) {
4190      if (isEmpty(str))
4191         return str;
4192
4193      var words = splitWords(str);
4194      if (words.isEmpty())
4195         return "";
4196
4197      var result = new StringBuilder();
4198      for (var i = 0; i < words.size(); i++) {
4199         if (i > 0)
4200            result.append('-');
4201         result.append(words.get(i).toLowerCase());
4202      }
4203
4204      return result.toString();
4205   }
4206
4207   /**
4208    * Finds the index of the last occurrence of a substring within a string.
4209    *
4210    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4211    * <p class='bjava'>
4212    *    lastIndexOf(<js>"hello world world"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// 12</jc>
4213    *    lastIndexOf(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);             <jc>// -1</jc>
4214    *    lastIndexOf(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"test"</js>);                     <jc>// -1</jc>
4215    * </p>
4216    *
4217    * @param str The string to search in.
4218    * @param search The substring to search for.
4219    * @return The index of the last occurrence, or <c>-1</c> if not found or if either parameter is <jk>null</jk>.
4220    */
4221   public static int lastIndexOf(String str, String search) {
4222      if (str == null || search == null)
4223         return -1;
4224      return str.lastIndexOf(search);
4225   }
4226
4227   /**
4228    * Finds the index of the last occurrence of a substring within a string, ignoring case.
4229    *
4230    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4231    * <p class='bjava'>
4232    *    lastIndexOfIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World World"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// 12</jc>
4233    *    lastIndexOfIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"WORLD"</js>);           <jc>// 6</jc>
4234    *    lastIndexOfIgnoreCase(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);             <jc>// -1</jc>
4235    * </p>
4236    *
4237    * @param str The string to search in.
4238    * @param search The substring to search for.
4239    * @return The index of the last occurrence, or <c>-1</c> if not found or if either parameter is <jk>null</jk>.
4240    */
4241   public static int lastIndexOfIgnoreCase(String str, String search) {
4242      if (str == null || search == null)
4243         return -1;
4244      return str.toLowerCase().lastIndexOf(search.toLowerCase());
4245   }
4246
4247   /**
4248    * Returns the last non-whitespace character in the string.
4249    *
4250    * @param s The string to check.
4251    * @return
4252    *    The last non-whitespace character, or <c>0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk>, empty, or composed
4253    *    of only whitespace.
4254    */
4255   public static char lastNonWhitespaceChar(String s) {
4256      if (nn(s))
4257         for (var i = s.length() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
4258            if (! isWhitespace(s.charAt(i)))
4259               return s.charAt(i);
4260      return 0;
4261   }
4262
4263   /**
4264    * Returns the leftmost characters of a string.
4265    *
4266    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4267    * <p class='bjava'>
4268    *    left(<jk>null</jk>, 3);          <jc>// null</jc>
4269    *    left(<js>""</js>, 3);            <jc>// ""</jc>
4270    *    left(<js>"hello"</js>, 3);       <jc>// "hel"</jc>
4271    *    left(<js>"hello"</js>, 10);      <jc>// "hello"</jc>
4272    * </p>
4273    *
4274    * @param str The string to get characters from.
4275    * @param len The number of characters to get.
4276    * @return The leftmost characters, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
4277    */
4278   public static String left(String str, int len) {
4279      if (str == null)
4280         return null;
4281      if (len < 0)
4282         return "";
4283      if (len >= str.length())
4284         return str;
4285      return str.substring(0, len);
4286   }
4287
4288   /**
4289    * Calculates the Levenshtein distance (edit distance) between two strings.
4290    *
4291    * <p>
4292    * The Levenshtein distance is the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions, or substitutions) required to change one string into another.
4293    *
4294    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4295    * <p class='bjava'>
4296    *    levenshteinDistance(<js>"kitten"</js>, <js>"sitting"</js>);     <jc>// 3</jc>
4297    *    levenshteinDistance(<js>"hello"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);        <jc>// 0</jc>
4298    *    levenshteinDistance(<js>"abc"</js>, <js>""</js>);               <jc>// 3</jc>
4299    * </p>
4300    *
4301    * @param str1 The first string.
4302    * @param str2 The second string.
4303    * @return The Levenshtein distance between the two strings.
4304    */
4305   public static int levenshteinDistance(String str1, String str2) {
4306      if (str1 == null)
4307         str1 = "";
4308      if (str2 == null)
4309         str2 = "";
4310
4311      var len1 = str1.length();
4312      var len2 = str2.length();
4313
4314      // Use dynamic programming with optimized space (only need previous row)
4315      var prev = new int[len2 + 1];
4316      var curr = new int[len2 + 1];
4317
4318      // Initialize first row
4319      for (var j = 0; j <= len2; j++)
4320         prev[j] = j;
4321
4322      for (var i = 1; i <= len1; i++) {
4323         curr[0] = i;
4324         for (var j = 1; j <= len2; j++) {
4325            if (str1.charAt(i - 1) == str2.charAt(j - 1)) {
4326               curr[j] = prev[j - 1];
4327            } else {
4328               curr[j] = 1 + Math.min(Math.min(prev[j], curr[j - 1]), prev[j - 1]);
4329            }
4330         }
4331         // Swap arrays
4332         var temp = prev;
4333         prev = curr;
4334         curr = temp;
4335      }
4336
4337      return prev[len2];
4338   }
4339
4340   /**
4341    * Counts the number of lines in a string.
4342    *
4343    * <p>
4344    * Counts newline characters. A string ending without a newline is counted as one line.
4345    *
4346    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4347    * <p class='bjava'>
4348    *    lineCount(<js>"line1\nline2\nline3"</js>);      <jc>// 3</jc>
4349    *    lineCount(<js>"single line"</js>);              <jc>// 1</jc>
4350    *    lineCount(<js>"line1\r\nline2"</js>);          <jc>// 2</jc>
4351    * </p>
4352    *
4353    * @param str The string to count lines in. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4354    * @return The number of lines, or <c>0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
4355    */
4356   @SuppressWarnings({
4357      "java:S127" // Loop counter advances to skip \r\n as single line break
4358   })
4359   public static int lineCount(String str) {
4360      if (isEmpty(str))
4361         return 0;
4362
4363      var count = 1; // At least one line
4364      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
4365         var c = str.charAt(i);
4366         if (c == '\n') {
4367            count++;
4368         } else if (c == '\r') {
4369            // Handle \r\n as a single line break
4370            if (i + 1 < str.length() && str.charAt(i + 1) == '\n')
4371               i++;
4372            count++;
4373         }
4374      }
4375
4376      return count;
4377   }
4378
4379   /**
4380    * Null-safe convenience method for {@link String#toLowerCase()}.
4381    *
4382    * <p>
4383    * Converts the string to lowercase if not null.
4384    *
4385    * @param s The string to convert.
4386    * @return The lowercase string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
4387    * @see #upperCase(String)
4388    * @see Utils#lc(String)
4389    */
4390   public static String lowerCase(String s) {
4391      return s == null ? null : s.toLowerCase();
4392   }
4393
4394   /**
4395    * Maps each element of a string array using the specified function.
4396    *
4397    * <p>
4398    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
4399    * Returns an array with <jk>null</jk> elements if the function is <jk>null</jk> or returns <jk>null</jk>.
4400    *
4401    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
4402    * <p class='bjava'>
4403    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>, <js>"baz"</js>};
4404    *    String[] <jv>uppercased</jv> = map(<jv>array</jv>, String::toUpperCase);
4405    *    <jc>// Returns: ["FOO", "BAR", "BAZ"]</jc>
4406    *
4407    *    String[] <jv>prefixed</jv> = map(<jv>array</jv>, s -&gt; <js>"prefix-"</js> + s);
4408    *    <jc>// Returns: ["prefix-foo", "prefix-bar", "prefix-baz"]</jc>
4409    * </p>
4410    *
4411    * @param array The array to map. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4412    * @param mapper The function to apply to each element. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4413    * @return A new array with the mapped elements, or an empty array if the array was <jk>null</jk>.
4414    */
4415   public static String[] mapped(String[] array, UnaryOperator<String> mapper) {
4416      if (array == null)
4417         return new String[0];
4418      if (mapper == null)
4419         return Arrays.copyOf(array, array.length);
4420      return Arrays.stream(array).map(mapper).toArray(String[]::new);
4421   }
4422
4423   /**
4424    * Checks if a string matches a regular expression pattern.
4425    *
4426    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4427    * <p class='bjava'>
4428    *    matches(<js>"12345"</js>, <js>"\\d+"</js>);              <jc>// true</jc>
4429    *    matches(<js>"abc123"</js>, <js>"^[a-z]+\\d+$"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
4430    *    matches(<js>"abc"</js>, <js>"\\d+"</js>);                <jc>// false</jc>
4431    * </p>
4432    *
4433    * @param str The string to check.
4434    * @param regex The regular expression pattern.
4435    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string matches the pattern, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
4436    * @throws PatternSyntaxException If the regex pattern is invalid.
4437    */
4438   public static boolean matches(String str, String regex) {
4439      if (str == null || regex == null)
4440         return false;
4441      return str.matches(regex);
4442   }
4443
4444   /**
4445    * Generates a Metaphone code for a string.
4446    *
4447    * <p>
4448    * Metaphone is a phonetic algorithm that produces codes representing how words sound.
4449    * It's more accurate than Soundex for English words.
4450    *
4451    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4452    * <p class='bjava'>
4453    *    metaphone(<js>"Smith"</js>);   <jc>// "SM0"</jc>
4454    *    metaphone(<js>"Smythe"</js>);  <jc>// "SM0"</jc>
4455    *    metaphone(<js>"Robert"</js>);  <jc>// "RBRT"</jc>
4456    * </p>
4457    *
4458    * @param str The string to generate a Metaphone code for. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4459    * @return The Metaphone code, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
4460    */
4461   @SuppressWarnings({
4462      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for metaphone algorithm
4463      "java:S6541", // Thread-safe singleton pattern acceptable
4464      "java:S1871" // Multiple cases in phonetic algorithm intentionally map to the same code (e.g. 'Q' -> 'K')
4465   })
4466   public static String metaphone(String str) {
4467      if (isEmpty(str))
4468         return null;
4469
4470      var upper = str.toUpperCase().replaceAll("[^A-Z]", "");
4471      if (upper.isEmpty())
4472         return "";
4473
4474      var result = new StringBuilder();
4475      var i = 0;
4476      var len = upper.length();
4477
4478      // Handle initial characters
4479      if (upper.startsWith("KN") || upper.startsWith("GN") || upper.startsWith("PN") || upper.startsWith("AE") || upper.startsWith("WR")) {
4480         i = 1;
4481      } else if (upper.startsWith("X")) {
4482         result.append('S');
4483         i = 1;
4484      } else if (upper.startsWith("WH")) {
4485         result.append('W');
4486         i = 2;
4487      }
4488
4489      // Process remaining characters
4490      while (i < len && result.length() < 4) {
4491         var c = upper.charAt(i);
4492         var prev = i > 0 ? upper.charAt(i - 1) : '\0';
4493         var next = i < len - 1 ? upper.charAt(i + 1) : '\0';
4494         var next2 = i < len - 2 ? upper.charAt(i + 2) : '\0';
4495
4496         // Skip duplicates (except C)
4497         if (c == prev && c != 'C') {
4498            i++;
4499            continue;
4500         }
4501
4502         switch (c) {
4503            case 'B':
4504               if (prev != 'M' || next != '\0')
4505                  result.append('B');
4506               break;
4507            case 'C':
4508               if (next == 'H') {
4509                  if (prev == 'S')
4510                     result.append('K');
4511                  else
4512                     result.append('X');
4513                  i++;
4514               } else if (next == 'I' || next == 'E' || next == 'Y') {
4515                  result.append('S');
4516               } else {
4517                  result.append('K');
4518               }
4519               break;
4520            case 'D':
4521               if (next == 'G' && (next2 == 'E' || next2 == 'I' || next2 == 'Y')) {
4522                  result.append('J');
4523                  i++;
4524               } else {
4525                  result.append('T');
4526               }
4527               break;
4528            case 'F', 'J', 'L', 'M', 'N', 'R':
4529               result.append(c);
4530               break;
4531            case 'G':
4532               if (next == 'H' && (next2 == 'A' || next2 == 'E' || next2 == 'I' || next2 == 'O' || next2 == 'U')) {
4533                  // Silent GH
4534               } else if (next == 'N' && (next2 == 'E' || next2 == 'D')) {
4535                  // Silent GN
4536               } else if ((next == 'E' || next == 'I' || next == 'Y') && prev != 'G') {
4537                  result.append('J');
4538               } else {
4539                  result.append('K');
4540               }
4541               break;
4542            case 'H':
4543               if (! VOWEL.contains(prev) || ! VOWEL.contains(next))
4544                  result.append('H');
4545               break;
4546            case 'K':
4547               if (prev != 'C')
4548                  result.append('K');
4549               break;
4550            case 'P':
4551               if (next == 'H') {
4552                  result.append('F');
4553                  i++;
4554               } else {
4555                  result.append('P');
4556               }
4557               break;
4558            case 'Q':
4559               result.append('K');
4560               break;
4561            case 'S':
4562               if (next == 'H') {
4563                  result.append('X');
4564                  i++;
4565               } else if (next == 'I' && (next2 == 'O' || next2 == 'A')) {
4566                  result.append('X');
4567                  i++;
4568               } else {
4569                  result.append('S');
4570               }
4571               break;
4572            case 'T':
4573               if (next == 'H') {
4574                  result.append('0'); // TH sound
4575                  i++;
4576               } else if (next == 'I' && (next2 == 'O' || next2 == 'A')) {
4577                  result.append('X');
4578                  i++;
4579               } else {
4580                  result.append('T');
4581               }
4582               break;
4583            case 'V':
4584               result.append('F');
4585               break;
4586            case 'W', 'Y':
4587               if (VOWEL.contains(next))
4588                  result.append(c);
4589               break;
4590            case 'X':
4591               // X at start is handled in initial section (line 4346-4348), so i is never 0 here
4592               result.append("KS");
4593               break;
4594            case 'Z':
4595               result.append('S');
4596               break;
4597            default:
4598               break;
4599         }
4600         i++;
4601      }
4602
4603      return !result.isEmpty() ? result.toString() : upper.substring(0, Math.min(1, upper.length()));
4604   }
4605
4606   /**
4607    * Returns the middle characters of a string.
4608    *
4609    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4610    * <p class='bjava'>
4611    *    mid(<jk>null</jk>, 1, 3);          <jc>// null</jc>
4612    *    mid(<js>""</js>, 1, 3);            <jc>// ""</jc>
4613    *    mid(<js>"hello"</js>, 1, 3);       <jc>// "ell"</jc>
4614    *    mid(<js>"hello"</js>, 1, 10);      <jc>// "ello"</jc>
4615    * </p>
4616    *
4617    * @param str The string to get characters from.
4618    * @param pos The starting position (0-based).
4619    * @param len The number of characters to get.
4620    * @return The middle characters, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
4621    */
4622   public static String mid(String str, int pos, int len) {
4623      if (str == null)
4624         return null;
4625      if (pos < 0 || len < 0)
4626         return "";
4627      if (pos >= str.length())
4628         return "";
4629      int end = Math.min(pos + len, str.length());
4630      return str.substring(pos, end);
4631   }
4632
4633   /**
4634    * Finds the most frequent character in a string.
4635    *
4636    * <p>
4637    * Returns the character that appears most often. If multiple characters have the same
4638    * frequency, returns the first one encountered.
4639    *
4640    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4641    * <p class='bjava'>
4642    *    mostFrequentChar(<js>"hello"</js>);     <jc>// 'l'</jc>
4643    *    mostFrequentChar(<js>"aabbcc"</js>);    <jc>// 'a' (first encountered)</jc>
4644    * </p>
4645    *
4646    * @param str The string to analyze. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4647    * @return The most frequent character, or <c>'\0'</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
4648    */
4649   public static char mostFrequentChar(String str) {
4650      if (isEmpty(str))
4651         return '\0';
4652
4653      var charCounts = new int[Character.MAX_VALUE + 1];
4654      var maxCount = 0;
4655      var maxChar = '\0';
4656
4657      // Count occurrences of each character
4658      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
4659         var c = str.charAt(i);
4660         charCounts[c]++;
4661         if (charCounts[c] > maxCount) {
4662            maxCount = charCounts[c];
4663            maxChar = c;
4664         }
4665      }
4666
4667      return maxChar;
4668   }
4669
4670   /**
4671    * Performs natural string comparison that handles numbers correctly.
4672    *
4673    * <p>
4674    * Compares strings in a way that numbers are compared numerically rather than lexicographically.
4675    * For example, "file2.txt" comes before "file10.txt" in natural order.
4676    *
4677    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4678    * <p class='bjava'>
4679    *    naturalCompare(<js>"file2.txt"</js>, <js>"file10.txt"</js>);   <jc>// negative (2 &lt; 10)</jc>
4680    *    naturalCompare(<js>"file10.txt"</js>, <js>"file2.txt"</js>);   <jc>// positive (10 &gt; 2)</jc>
4681    *    naturalCompare(<js>"file1.txt"</js>, <js>"file1.txt"</js>);    <jc>// 0 (equal)</jc>
4682    * </p>
4683    *
4684    * @param str1 The first string.
4685    * @param str2 The second string.
4686    * @return A negative integer, zero, or a positive integer as the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.
4687    */
4688   @SuppressWarnings({
4689      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for natural comparison algorithm
4690   })
4691   public static int naturalCompare(String str1, String str2) {
4692      if (Objects.equals(str1, str2))
4693         return 0;
4694      if (str1 == null)
4695         return -1;
4696      if (str2 == null)
4697         return 1;
4698
4699      var len1 = str1.length();
4700      var len2 = str2.length();
4701      var i1 = 0;
4702      var i2 = 0;
4703
4704      while (i1 < len1 && i2 < len2) {
4705         var c1 = str1.charAt(i1);
4706         var c2 = str2.charAt(i2);
4707
4708         // If both are digits, compare numerically
4709         if (DIGIT.contains(c1) && DIGIT.contains(c2)) {
4710            // Skip leading zeros
4711            while (i1 < len1 && str1.charAt(i1) == '0')
4712               i1++;
4713            while (i2 < len2 && str2.charAt(i2) == '0')
4714               i2++;
4715
4716            // Find end of number sequences
4717            var end1 = i1;
4718            var end2 = i2;
4719            while (end1 < len1 && DIGIT.contains(str1.charAt(end1)))
4720               end1++;
4721            while (end2 < len2 && DIGIT.contains(str2.charAt(end2)))
4722               end2++;
4723
4724            // Compare lengths first (longer number is larger)
4725            var lenNum1 = end1 - i1;
4726            var lenNum2 = end2 - i2;
4727            if (lenNum1 != lenNum2)
4728               return lenNum1 - lenNum2;
4729
4730            // Same length, compare digit by digit
4731            for (var j = 0; j < lenNum1; j++) {
4732               var d1 = str1.charAt(i1 + j);
4733               var d2 = str2.charAt(i2 + j);
4734               if (d1 != d2)
4735                  return d1 - d2;
4736            }
4737
4738            i1 = end1;
4739            i2 = end2;
4740         } else {
4741            // Compare characters (case-insensitive)
4742            var cmp = Character.toLowerCase(c1) - Character.toLowerCase(c2);
4743            if (cmp != 0)
4744               return cmp;
4745            i1++;
4746            i2++;
4747         }
4748      }
4749
4750      return len1 - len2;
4751   }
4752
4753   /**
4754    * Normalizes Unicode characters in a string.
4755    *
4756    * <p>
4757    * Uses Unicode normalization form NFD (Canonical Decomposition).
4758    *
4759    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4760    * <p class='bjava'>
4761    *    normalizeUnicode(<js>"café"</js>);
4762    *    <jc>// Normalized form</jc>
4763    * </p>
4764    *
4765    * @param str The string to normalize. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
4766    * @return The normalized string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
4767    */
4768   public static String normalizeUnicode(String str) {
4769      if (str == null)
4770         return null;
4771      return Normalizer.normalize(str, Normalizer.Form.NFD);
4772   }
4773
4774   /**
4775    * Normalizes all whitespace in a string to single spaces.
4776    *
4777    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4778    * <p class='bjava'>
4779    *    normalizeWhitespace(<js>"hello  \t\n  world"</js>);   <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
4780    *    normalizeWhitespace(<js>"  hello  world  "</js>);     <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
4781    * </p>
4782    *
4783    * @param str The string to normalize.
4784    * @return The normalized string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
4785    */
4786   public static String normalizeWhitespace(String str) {
4787      if (str == null)
4788         return null;
4789      return str.replaceAll("\\s+", " ").trim();
4790   }
4791
4792   /**
4793    * Checks if a string does not contain the specified character.
4794    *
4795    * <p>
4796    * This is the inverse of {@link #contains(String, char)}.
4797    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or does not contain the character.
4798    *
4799    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4800    * <p class='bjava'>
4801    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'x'</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
4802    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'o'</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
4803    *    notContains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);            <jc>// true</jc>
4804    * </p>
4805    *
4806    * @param s The string to check.
4807    * @param c The character to check for.
4808    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain the specified character.
4809    * @see #contains(String, char)
4810    * @see #notContainsAny(String, char...)
4811    */
4812   public static boolean notContains(String s, char c) {
4813      return ! contains(s, c);
4814   }
4815
4816   /**
4817    * Checks if a string does not contain the specified substring.
4818    *
4819    * <p>
4820    * This is the inverse of {@link #contains(String, CharSequence)}.
4821    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or does not contain the substring.
4822    *
4823    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4824    * <p class='bjava'>
4825    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
4826    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
4827    *    notContains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);            <jc>// true</jc>
4828    * </p>
4829    *
4830    * @param s The string to check.
4831    * @param substring The substring to check for.
4832    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain the specified substring.
4833    * @see #contains(String, CharSequence)
4834    * @see #notContainsAny(String, CharSequence...)
4835    */
4836   public static boolean notContains(String s, CharSequence substring) {
4837      return ! contains(s, substring);
4838   }
4839
4840   /**
4841    * Checks if a string does not contain the specified substring.
4842    *
4843    * <p>
4844    * This is the inverse of {@link #contains(String, String)}.
4845    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or does not contain the substring.
4846    *
4847    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4848    * <p class='bjava'>
4849    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
4850    *    notContains(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// false</jc>
4851    *    notContains(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);            <jc>// true</jc>
4852    * </p>
4853    *
4854    * @param s The string to check.
4855    * @param substring The substring to check for.
4856    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain the specified substring.
4857    * @see #contains(String, String)
4858    * @see #notContainsAny(String, String...)
4859    */
4860   public static boolean notContains(String s, String substring) {
4861      return ! contains(s, substring);
4862   }
4863
4864   /**
4865    * Checks if a string does not contain all of the specified characters.
4866    *
4867    * <p>
4868    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAll(String, char...)}.
4869    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
4870    * <ul>
4871    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
4872    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
4873    *   <li>Any of the specified characters are not found in the string</li>
4874    * </ul>
4875    *
4876    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4877    * <p class='bjava'>
4878    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'H'</js>, <js>'x'</js>);                <jc>// true (missing 'x')</jc>
4879    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'H'</js>, <js>'e'</js>, <js>'l'</js>);   <jc>// false (contains all)</jc>
4880    *    notContainsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);                                      <jc>// true</jc>
4881    * </p>
4882    *
4883    * @param s The string to check.
4884    * @param values The characters to check for.
4885    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain all of the specified characters.
4886    * @see #containsAll(String, char...)
4887    */
4888   public static boolean notContainsAll(String s, char...values) {
4889      return ! containsAll(s, values);
4890   }
4891
4892   /**
4893    * Checks if a string does not contain all of the specified substrings.
4894    *
4895    * <p>
4896    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAll(String, CharSequence...)}.
4897    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
4898    * <ul>
4899    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
4900    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
4901    *   <li>Any of the specified substrings are not found in the string</li>
4902    * </ul>
4903    *
4904    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4905    * <p class='bjava'>
4906    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);    <jc>// true (missing "Foo")</jc>
4907    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// false (contains all)</jc>
4908    *    notContainsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                              <jc>// true</jc>
4909    * </p>
4910    *
4911    * @param s The string to check.
4912    * @param values The substrings to check for.
4913    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain all of the specified substrings.
4914    * @see #containsAll(String, CharSequence...)
4915    */
4916   public static boolean notContainsAll(String s, CharSequence...values) {
4917      return ! containsAll(s, values);
4918   }
4919
4920   /**
4921    * Checks if a string does not contain all of the specified substrings.
4922    *
4923    * <p>
4924    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAll(String, String...)}.
4925    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
4926    * <ul>
4927    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
4928    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
4929    *   <li>Any of the specified substrings are not found in the string</li>
4930    * </ul>
4931    *
4932    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4933    * <p class='bjava'>
4934    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);    <jc>// true (missing "Foo")</jc>
4935    *    notContainsAll(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"World"</js>);   <jc>// false (contains all)</jc>
4936    *    notContainsAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                              <jc>// true</jc>
4937    * </p>
4938    *
4939    * @param s The string to check.
4940    * @param values The substrings to check for.
4941    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain all of the specified substrings.
4942    * @see #containsAll(String, String...)
4943    */
4944   public static boolean notContainsAll(String s, String...values) {
4945      return ! containsAll(s, values);
4946   }
4947
4948   /**
4949    * Checks if a string does not contain any of the specified characters.
4950    *
4951    * <p>
4952    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAny(String, char...)}.
4953    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
4954    * <ul>
4955    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
4956    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
4957    *   <li>None of the specified characters are found in the string</li>
4958    * </ul>
4959    *
4960    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4961    * <p class='bjava'>
4962    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'x'</js>, <js>'y'</js>);   <jc>// true</jc>
4963    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>'o'</js>, <js>'x'</js>);   <jc>// false (contains 'o')</jc>
4964    *    notContainsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>'a'</js>);                          <jc>// true</jc>
4965    * </p>
4966    *
4967    * @param s The string to check.
4968    * @param values The characters to check for.
4969    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain any of the specified characters.
4970    * @see #containsAny(String, char...)
4971    * @see #notContainsAll(String, char...)
4972    */
4973   public static boolean notContainsAny(String s, char...values) {
4974      return ! containsAny(s, values);
4975   }
4976
4977   /**
4978    * Checks if a string does not contain any of the specified substrings.
4979    *
4980    * <p>
4981    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAny(String, CharSequence...)}.
4982    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
4983    * <ul>
4984    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
4985    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
4986    *   <li>None of the specified substrings are found in the string</li>
4987    * </ul>
4988    *
4989    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4990    * <p class='bjava'>
4991    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"Bar"</js>);    <jc>// true</jc>
4992    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);  <jc>// false (contains "Hello")</jc>
4993    *    notContainsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                            <jc>// true</jc>
4994    * </p>
4995    *
4996    * @param s The string to check.
4997    * @param values The substrings to check for.
4998    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain any of the specified substrings.
4999    * @see #containsAny(String, CharSequence...)
5000    * @see #notContainsAll(String, CharSequence...)
5001    */
5002   public static boolean notContainsAny(String s, CharSequence...values) {
5003      return ! containsAny(s, values);
5004   }
5005
5006   /**
5007    * Checks if a string does not contain any of the specified substrings.
5008    *
5009    * <p>
5010    * This is the inverse of {@link #containsAny(String, String...)}.
5011    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if:
5012    * <ul>
5013    *   <li>The string is <jk>null</jk></li>
5014    *   <li>The values array is <jk>null</jk> or empty</li>
5015    *   <li>None of the specified substrings are found in the string</li>
5016    * </ul>
5017    *
5018    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5019    * <p class='bjava'>
5020    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"Bar"</js>);    <jc>// true</jc>
5021    *    notContainsAny(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"Hello"</js>, <js>"Foo"</js>);  <jc>// false (contains "Hello")</jc>
5022    *    notContainsAny(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"Hello"</js>);                            <jc>// true</jc>
5023    * </p>
5024    *
5025    * @param s The string to check.
5026    * @param values The substrings to check for.
5027    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string does not contain any of the specified substrings.
5028    * @see #containsAny(String, String...)
5029    * @see #notContainsAll(String, String...)
5030    */
5031   public static boolean notContainsAny(String s, String...values) {
5032      return ! containsAny(s, values);
5033   }
5034
5035   /**
5036    * Returns an obfuscated version of the specified string.
5037    *
5038    * @param s The string to obfuscate.
5039    * @return The obfuscated string with most characters replaced by asterisks.
5040    */
5041   public static String obfuscate(String s) {
5042      if (s == null || s.length() < 2)
5043         return "*";
5044      return s.substring(0, 1) + s.substring(1).replaceAll(".", "*");  // NOSONAR - Dot matches any character, which is intended
5045   }
5046
5047   /**
5048    * Converts a number to its ordinal form (1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, etc.).
5049    *
5050    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5051    * <p class='bjava'>
5052    *    ordinal(<js>1</js>);     <jc>// "1st"</jc>
5053    *    ordinal(<js>2</js>);     <jc>// "2nd"</jc>
5054    *    ordinal(<js>3</js>);     <jc>// "3rd"</jc>
5055    *    ordinal(<js>4</js>);     <jc>// "4th"</jc>
5056    *    ordinal(<js>11</js>);    <jc>// "11th"</jc>
5057    *    ordinal(<js>21</js>);    <jc>// "21st"</jc>
5058    * </p>
5059    *
5060    * @param number The number to convert.
5061    * @return The ordinal string representation of the number.
5062    */
5063   public static String ordinal(int number) {
5064      var abs = Math.abs(number);
5065      var suffix = "th";
5066
5067      // Special cases for 11, 12, 13 (all use "th")
5068      if (abs % 100 != 11 && abs % 100 != 12 && abs % 100 != 13) {
5069         var lastDigit = abs % 10;
5070         if (lastDigit == 1)
5071            suffix = "st";
5072         else if (lastDigit == 2)
5073            suffix = "nd";
5074         else if (lastDigit == 3)
5075            suffix = "rd";
5076      }
5077
5078      return number + suffix;
5079   }
5080
5081   /**
5082    * Center pads a string with a specified character.
5083    *
5084    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5085    * <p class='bjava'>
5086    *    padCenter(<jk>null</jk>, 5, <js>' '</js>);          <jc>// "     "</jc>
5087    *    padCenter(<js>""</js>, 5, <js>' '</js>);            <jc>// "     "</jc>
5088    *    padCenter(<js>"hi"</js>, 6, <js>' '</js>);          <jc>// "  hi  "</jc>
5089    *    padCenter(<js>"hi"</js>, 7, <js>' '</js>);          <jc>// "   hi  "</jc>
5090    *    padCenter(<js>"hello"</js>, 3, <js>' '</js>);       <jc>// "hello"</jc>
5091    * </p>
5092    *
5093    * @param str The string to pad.
5094    * @param size The desired total string length.
5095    * @param padChar The character to pad with.
5096    * @return The center-padded string.
5097    */
5098   public static String padCenter(String str, int size, char padChar) {
5099      if (str == null)
5100         str = "";
5101      int pads = size - str.length();
5102      if (pads <= 0)
5103         return str;
5104      int rightPads = pads / 2;
5105      int leftPads = pads - rightPads;
5106      return String.valueOf(padChar).repeat(leftPads) + str + String.valueOf(padChar).repeat(rightPads);
5107   }
5108
5109   /**
5110    * Left pads a string with a specified character.
5111    *
5112    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5113    * <p class='bjava'>
5114    *    padLeft(<jk>null</jk>, 5, <js>' '</js>);          <jc>// "     "</jc>
5115    *    padLeft(<js>""</js>, 5, <js>' '</js>);            <jc>// "     "</jc>
5116    *    padLeft(<js>"hello"</js>, 8, <js>' '</js>);       <jc>// "   hello"</jc>
5117    *    padLeft(<js>"hello"</js>, 3, <js>' '</js>);       <jc>// "hello"</jc>
5118    *    padLeft(<js>"123"</js>, 5, <js>'0'</js>);         <jc>// "00123"</jc>
5119    * </p>
5120    *
5121    * @param str The string to pad.
5122    * @param size The desired total string length.
5123    * @param padChar The character to pad with.
5124    * @return The left-padded string.
5125    */
5126   public static String padLeft(String str, int size, char padChar) {
5127      if (str == null)
5128         str = "";
5129      int pads = size - str.length();
5130      if (pads <= 0)
5131         return str;
5132      return String.valueOf(padChar).repeat(pads) + str;
5133   }
5134
5135   /**
5136    * Right pads a string with a specified character.
5137    *
5138    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5139    * <p class='bjava'>
5140    *    padRight(<jk>null</jk>, 5, <js>' '</js>);          <jc>// "     "</jc>
5141    *    padRight(<js>""</js>, 5, <js>' '</js>);            <jc>// "     "</jc>
5142    *    padRight(<js>"hello"</js>, 8, <js>' '</js>);       <jc>// "hello   "</jc>
5143    *    padRight(<js>"hello"</js>, 3, <js>' '</js>);       <jc>// "hello"</jc>
5144    *    padRight(<js>"123"</js>, 5, <js>'0'</js>);         <jc>// "12300"</jc>
5145    * </p>
5146    *
5147    * @param str The string to pad.
5148    * @param size The desired total string length.
5149    * @param padChar The character to pad with.
5150    * @return The right-padded string.
5151    */
5152   public static String padRight(String str, int size, char padChar) {
5153      if (str == null)
5154         str = "";
5155      int pads = size - str.length();
5156      if (pads <= 0)
5157         return str;
5158      return str + String.valueOf(padChar).repeat(pads);
5159   }
5160
5161   /**
5162    * Converts a <c>String</c> to a <c>Character</c>
5163    *
5164    * @param o The string to convert.
5165    * @return The first character of the string if the string is of length 1, or <jk>null</jk> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
5166    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the string length is not 1.
5167    */
5168   public static Character parseCharacter(Object o) {
5169      if (o == null)
5170         return null;
5171      var s = o.toString();
5172      if (s.isEmpty())
5173         return null;
5174      if (s.length() == 1)
5175         return s.charAt(0);
5176      throw illegalArg("Invalid character: ''{0}''", s);
5177   }
5178
5179   /**
5180    * Same as {@link Float#parseFloat(String)} but removes any underscore characters first.
5181    *
5182    * <p>Allows for better readability of numeric literals (e.g., <js>"1_000.5"</js>).
5183    *
5184    * @param value The string to parse.
5185    * @return The parsed float value.
5186    * @throws NumberFormatException If the string cannot be parsed.
5187    * @throws NullPointerException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5188    */
5189   public static float parseFloat(String value) {
5190      return Float.parseFloat(StringUtils.removeUnderscores(value));
5191   }
5192
5193   /**
5194    * Same as {@link Integer#parseInt(String)} but removes any underscore characters first.
5195    *
5196    * <p>Allows for better readability of numeric literals (e.g., <js>"1_000_000"</js>).
5197    *
5198    * @param value The string to parse.
5199    * @return The parsed integer value.
5200    * @throws NumberFormatException If the string cannot be parsed.
5201    * @throws NullPointerException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5202    */
5203   public static int parseInt(String value) {
5204      return Integer.parseInt(StringUtils.removeUnderscores(value));
5205   }
5206
5207   /**
5208    * Same as {@link #parseInt(String)} but converts a {@link NumberFormatException} into a caller-supplied exception.
5209    *
5210    * <p>
5211    * Lets callers wrap the parse failure with a domain-specific error (typically an {@link IllegalArgumentException})
5212    * without having to write a {@code try}/{@code catch} block at the call site.
5213    *
5214    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5215    * <p class='bjava'>
5216    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>port</jv> = StringUtils.<jsm>parseInt</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>,
5217    *       ()-&gt; <jsm>illegalArg</jsm>(<js>"Invalid port: {0}"</js>, <jv>value</jv>));
5218    * </p>
5219    *
5220    * @param <T> The exception type produced by the supplier.
5221    * @param value The string to parse.
5222    * @param errorSupplier Supplier of the exception to throw if the string cannot be parsed.
5223    * @return The parsed integer value.
5224    * @throws T If the string cannot be parsed.
5225    * @throws NullPointerException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5226    */
5227   public static <T extends RuntimeException> int parseInt(String value, Supplier<T> errorSupplier) {
5228      try {
5229         return parseInt(value);
5230      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
5231         throw errorSupplier.get();
5232      }
5233   }
5234
5235   /**
5236    * Converts a string containing a possible multiplier suffix to an integer.
5237    *
5238    * <p>
5239    * The string can contain any of the following multiplier suffixes:
5240    * <ul>
5241    *    <li><js>"K"</js> - x 1024
5242    *    <li><js>"M"</js> - x 1024*1024
5243    *    <li><js>"G"</js> - x 1024*1024*1024
5244    *    <li><js>"k"</js> - x 1000
5245    *    <li><js>"m"</js> - x 1000*1000
5246    *    <li><js>"g"</js> - x 1000*1000*1000
5247    * </ul>
5248    *
5249    * @param s The string to parse.
5250    * @return The parsed value.
5251    */
5252   public static int parseIntWithSuffix(String s) {
5253      assertArgNotNull(ARG_s, s);
5254      var m = multiplierInt(s);
5255      if (m == 1)
5256         return Integer.decode(s);
5257      return Integer.decode(s.substring(0, s.length() - 1).trim()) * m;
5258   }
5259
5260   /**
5261    * Same as {@link #parseIntWithSuffix(String)} but converts a {@link NumberFormatException} into a caller-supplied exception.
5262    *
5263    * <p>
5264    * Lets callers wrap the parse failure with a domain-specific error (typically an {@link IllegalArgumentException})
5265    * without having to write a {@code try}/{@code catch} block at the call site.
5266    *
5267    * @param <T> The exception type produced by the supplier.
5268    * @param value The string to parse.
5269    * @param errorSupplier Supplier of the exception to throw if the string cannot be parsed.
5270    * @return The parsed integer value.
5271    * @throws T If the string cannot be parsed.
5272    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5273    */
5274   public static <T extends RuntimeException> int parseIntWithSuffix(String value, Supplier<T> errorSupplier) {
5275      try {
5276         return parseIntWithSuffix(value);
5277      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
5278         throw errorSupplier.get();
5279      }
5280   }
5281
5282   /**
5283    * Same as {@link Long#parseLong(String)} but removes any underscore characters first.
5284    *
5285    * <p>Allows for better readability of numeric literals (e.g., <js>"1_000_000"</js>).
5286    *
5287    * @param value The string to parse.
5288    * @return The parsed long value.
5289    * @throws NumberFormatException If the string cannot be parsed.
5290    * @throws NullPointerException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5291    */
5292   public static long parseLong(String value) {
5293      return Long.parseLong(StringUtils.removeUnderscores(value));
5294   }
5295
5296   /**
5297    * Same as {@link #parseLong(String)} but converts a {@link NumberFormatException} into a caller-supplied exception.
5298    *
5299    * <p>
5300    * Lets callers wrap the parse failure with a domain-specific error (typically an {@link IllegalArgumentException})
5301    * without having to write a {@code try}/{@code catch} block at the call site.
5302    *
5303    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5304    * <p class='bjava'>
5305    *    <jk>long</jk> <jv>size</jv> = StringUtils.<jsm>parseLong</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>,
5306    *       ()-&gt; <jsm>illegalArg</jsm>(<js>"Invalid size: {0}"</js>, <jv>value</jv>));
5307    * </p>
5308    *
5309    * @param <T> The exception type produced by the supplier.
5310    * @param value The string to parse.
5311    * @param errorSupplier Supplier of the exception to throw if the string cannot be parsed.
5312    * @return The parsed long value.
5313    * @throws T If the string cannot be parsed.
5314    * @throws NullPointerException If the string is <jk>null</jk>.
5315    */
5316   public static <T extends RuntimeException> long parseLong(String value, Supplier<T> errorSupplier) {
5317      try {
5318         return parseLong(value);
5319      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
5320         throw errorSupplier.get();
5321      }
5322   }
5323
5324   /**
5325    * Converts a string containing a possible multiplier suffix to a long.
5326    *
5327    * <p>
5328    * The string can contain any of the following multiplier suffixes:
5329    * <ul>
5330    *    <li><js>"K"</js> - x 1024
5331    *    <li><js>"M"</js> - x 1024*1024
5332    *    <li><js>"G"</js> - x 1024*1024*1024
5333    *    <li><js>"T"</js> - x 1024*1024*1024*1024
5334    *    <li><js>"P"</js> - x 1024*1024*1024*1024*1024
5335    *    <li><js>"k"</js> - x 1000
5336    *    <li><js>"m"</js> - x 1000*1000
5337    *    <li><js>"g"</js> - x 1000*1000*1000
5338    *    <li><js>"t"</js> - x 1000*1000*1000*1000
5339    *    <li><js>"p"</js> - x 1000*1000*1000*1000*1000
5340    * </ul>
5341    *
5342    * @param s The string to parse.
5343    * @return The parsed value.
5344    */
5345   public static long parseLongWithSuffix(String s) {
5346      assertArgNotNull(ARG_s, s);
5347      var m = multiplierLong(s);
5348      if (m == 1) {
5349         // If multiplier is 1, try to decode the whole string
5350         // This will throw NumberFormatException if it contains a suffix character
5351         return Long.decode(s);
5352      }
5353      var baseStr = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1).trim();
5354      var base = Long.decode(baseStr);
5355      try {
5356         return Math.multiplyExact(base, m);
5357      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") ArithmeticException e) {
5358         throw new NumberFormatException("Value " + s + " exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE");
5359      }
5360   }
5361
5362   /**
5363    * Parses a string containing key-value pairs into a map.
5364    *
5365    * <p>
5366    * Splits the string by the entry delimiter, then splits each entry by the key-value delimiter.
5367    *
5368    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5369    * <p class='bjava'>
5370    *    parseMap(<js>"key1=value1,key2=value2"</js>, <js>'='</js>, <js>','</js>, <jk>false</jk>);
5371    *    <jc>// {"key1":"value1","key2":"value2"}</jc>
5372    *    parseMap(<js>" key1 = value1 ; key2 = value2 "</js>, <js>'='</js>, <js>';'</js>, <jk>true</jk>);
5373    *    <jc>// {"key1":"value1","key2":"value2"}</jc>
5374    * </p>
5375    *
5376    * @param str The string to parse. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
5377    * @param keyValueDelimiter The character that separates keys from values.
5378    * @param entryDelimiter The character that separates entries.
5379    * @param trimKeys If <jk>true</jk>, trims whitespace from keys and values.
5380    * @return A map containing the parsed key-value pairs, or an empty map if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
5381    */
5382   public static Map<String,String> parseMap(String str, char keyValueDelimiter, char entryDelimiter, boolean trimKeys) {
5383      var result = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
5384      if (isEmpty(str))
5385         return result;
5386
5387      var entries = split(str, entryDelimiter);
5388      for (var entry : entries) {
5389         if (isEmpty(entry))
5390            continue;
5391         var delimiterIndex = entry.indexOf(keyValueDelimiter);
5392         if (delimiterIndex == -1) {
5393            // No delimiter found, treat entire entry as key with empty value
5394            var key = trimKeys ? entry.trim() : entry;
5395            result.put(key, "");
5396         } else {
5397            var key = entry.substring(0, delimiterIndex);
5398            var value = entry.substring(delimiterIndex + 1);
5399            if (trimKeys) {
5400               key = key.trim();
5401               value = value.trim();
5402            }
5403            result.put(key, value);
5404         }
5405      }
5406      return result;
5407   }
5408
5409   /**
5410    * Parses a number from the specified string.
5411    *
5412    * <p>
5413    * Supports Java 7+ numeric literals with underscores (e.g., <js>"1_000_000"</js>).
5414    * The underscores are automatically removed before parsing.
5415    *
5416    * @param s The string to parse the number from.
5417    * @param type
5418    *    The number type to created.
5419    *    Can be any of the following:
5420    *    <ul>
5421    *       <li> Integer (or <c>int</c> primitive)
5422    *       <li> Long (or <c>long</c> primitive)
5423    *       <li> Short (or <c>short</c> primitive)
5424    *       <li> Byte (or <c>byte</c> primitive)
5425    *       <li> Float (or <c>float</c> primitive)
5426    *       <li> Double (or <c>double</c> primitive)
5427    *       <li> BigInteger
5428    *       <li> BigDecimal
5429    *       <li> AtomicInteger
5430    *       <li> AtomicLong
5431    *    </ul>
5432    *    If <jk>null</jk> or <c>Number</c>, uses the best guess.
5433    * @return The parsed number, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was null.
5434    */
5435   @SuppressWarnings({
5436      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for number parsing logic
5437   })
5438   public static Number parseNumber(String s, Class<? extends Number> type) {
5439      if (s == null)
5440         return null;
5441      if (s.isEmpty())
5442         s = "0";
5443      if (type == null)
5444         type = Number.class;
5445
5446      // Remove underscores (Java 7+ numeric literal support) before parsing
5447      // Note: We do this before type detection to ensure clean parsing
5448      s = s.replace("_", "");
5449
5450      // Determine the data type if it wasn't specified.
5451      var isAutoDetect = (type == Number.class);
5452      var isDecimal = false;
5453      if (isAutoDetect) {
5454         // If we're auto-detecting, then we use either an Integer, Long, or Double depending on how
5455         // long the string is.
5456         // An integer range is -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647
5457         // An long range is -9,223,372,036,854,775,808 to +9,223,372,036,854,775,807
5458         isDecimal = isDecimal(s);
5459         if (isDecimal) {
5460            if (s.length() > 20)
5461               type = Double.class;
5462            else if (s.length() >= 10)
5463               type = Long.class;
5464            else
5465               type = Integer.class;
5466         } else if (isFloat(s))
5467            type = Double.class;
5468         else
5469            throw new NumberFormatException(s);
5470      }
5471
5472      if (type == Double.class || type == Double.TYPE) {
5473         return Double.valueOf(s);
5474      }
5475      if (type == Float.class || type == Float.TYPE)
5476         return Float.valueOf(s);
5477      if (type == BigDecimal.class)
5478         return new BigDecimal(s);
5479      if (type == Long.class || type == Long.TYPE || type == AtomicLong.class) {
5480         try {
5481            var l = parseLongWithSuffix(s);
5482            if (type == AtomicLong.class)
5483               return new AtomicLong(l);
5484            if (isAutoDetect && l >= Integer.MIN_VALUE && l <= Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
5485               // This occurs if the string is 10 characters long but is still a valid integer value.
5486               return (int)l;
5487            }
5488            return l;
5489         } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
5490            if (isAutoDetect) {
5491               // This occurs if the string is 20 characters long but still falls outside the range of a valid long.
5492               return Double.valueOf(s);
5493            }
5494            throw e;
5495         }
5496      }
5497      if (type == Integer.class || type == Integer.TYPE)
5498         return Integer.decode(s);
5499      if (type == Short.class || type == Short.TYPE)
5500         return Short.decode(s);
5501      if (type == Byte.class || type == Byte.TYPE)
5502         return Byte.decode(s);
5503      if (type == BigInteger.class)
5504         return new BigInteger(s);
5505      if (type == AtomicInteger.class)
5506         return new AtomicInteger(Integer.decode(s));
5507      throw new NumberFormatException("Unsupported Number type: " + type.getName());
5508   }
5509
5510   /**
5511    * Converts a string to PascalCase format.
5512    *
5513    * <p>
5514    * Handles various input formats:
5515    * <ul>
5516    *   <li>Space-separated: "hello world" → "HelloWorld"</li>
5517    *   <li>CamelCase: "helloWorld" → "HelloWorld"</li>
5518    *   <li>Snake_case: "hello_world" → "HelloWorld"</li>
5519    *   <li>Kebab-case: "hello-world" → "HelloWorld"</li>
5520    * </ul>
5521    *
5522    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5523    * <p class='bjava'>
5524    *    pascalCase(<jk>null</jk>);                    <jc>// null</jc>
5525    *    pascalCase(<js>""</js>);                      <jc>// ""</jc>
5526    *    pascalCase(<js>"hello world"</js>);           <jc>// "HelloWorld"</jc>
5527    *    pascalCase(<js>"helloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "HelloWorld"</jc>
5528    *    pascalCase(<js>"hello_world"</js>);           <jc>// "HelloWorld"</jc>
5529    *    pascalCase(<js>"hello-world"</js>);           <jc>// "HelloWorld"</jc>
5530    * </p>
5531    *
5532    * @param str The string to convert.
5533    * @return The PascalCase string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
5534    */
5535   public static String pascalCase(String str) {
5536      if (isEmpty(str))
5537         return str;
5538
5539      var words = splitWords(str);
5540      if (words.isEmpty())
5541         return "";
5542
5543      var result = new StringBuilder();
5544      for (var word : words) {
5545         result.append(capitalize(word.toLowerCase()));
5546      }
5547
5548      return result.toString();
5549   }
5550
5551   /**
5552    * Pluralizes a word based on a count.
5553    *
5554    * <p>
5555    * Simple pluralization that adds "s" to most words, with basic rules for words ending in "s", "x", "z", "ch", "sh" (add "es"),
5556    * and words ending in "y" preceded by a consonant (replace "y" with "ies").
5557    *
5558    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5559    * <p class='bjava'>
5560    *    pluralize(<js>"cat"</js>, <js>1</js>);        <jc>// "cat"</jc>
5561    *    pluralize(<js>"cat"</js>, <js>2</js>);        <jc>// "cats"</jc>
5562    *    pluralize(<js>"box"</js>, <js>2</js>);        <jc>// "boxes"</jc>
5563    *    pluralize(<js>"city"</js>, <js>2</js>);       <jc>// "cities"</jc>
5564    * </p>
5565    *
5566    * @param word The word to pluralize.
5567    * @param count The count to determine if pluralization is needed.
5568    * @return The pluralized word if count is not 1, otherwise the original word.
5569    */
5570   public static String pluralize(String word, int count) {
5571      if (word == null || word.isEmpty())
5572         return word;
5573      if (count == 1)
5574         return word;
5575
5576      var lower = word.toLowerCase();
5577      var length = word.length();
5578
5579      // Words ending in s, x, z, ch, sh -> add "es"
5580      if (lower.endsWith("s") || lower.endsWith("x") || lower.endsWith("z") || lower.endsWith("ch") || lower.endsWith("sh")) {
5581         return word + "es";
5582      }
5583
5584      // Words ending in "y" preceded by a consonant -> replace "y" with "ies"
5585      if (length > 1 && lower.endsWith("y")) {
5586         var secondLast = lower.charAt(length - 2);
5587         if (! VOWEL.contains(secondLast)) {
5588            return word.substring(0, length - 1) + "ies";
5589         }
5590      }
5591
5592      // Words ending in "f" or "fe" -> replace with "ves" (basic rule)
5593      if (lower.endsWith("f")) {
5594         return word.substring(0, length - 1) + "ves";
5595      }
5596      if (lower.endsWith("fe")) {
5597         return word.substring(0, length - 2) + "ves";
5598      }
5599
5600      // Default: add "s"
5601      return word + "s";
5602   }
5603
5604   /**
5605    * Generated a random UUID with the specified number of characters.
5606    *
5607    * <p>
5608    * Characters are composed of lower-case ASCII letters and numbers only.
5609    *
5610    * <p>
5611    * This method conforms to the restrictions for hostnames as specified in <a class="doclink" href="https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc952">RFC 952</a>
5612    * Since each character has 36 possible values, the square approximation formula for the number of generated IDs
5613    * that would produce a 50% chance of collision is:
5614    * <c>sqrt(36^N)</c>.
5615    * Dividing this number by 10 gives you an approximation of the number of generated IDs needed to produce a
5616    * &lt;1% chance of collision.
5617    *
5618    * <p>
5619    * For example, given 5 characters, the number of generated IDs need to produce a &lt;1% chance of collision would
5620    * be:
5621    * <c>sqrt(36^5)/10=777</c>
5622    *
5623    * @param numchars The number of characters in the generated UUID.
5624    * @return A new random UUID.
5625    */
5626   public static String random(int numchars) {
5627      var sb = new StringBuilder(numchars);
5628      for (var i = 0; i < numchars; i++) {
5629         var c = RANDOM.nextInt(36) + 97;
5630         if (c > 'z')
5631            c -= ('z' - '0' + 1);
5632         sb.append((char)c);
5633      }
5634      return sb.toString();
5635   }
5636
5637   /**
5638    * Generates a random alphabetic string of the specified length.
5639    *
5640    * <p>
5641    * Characters are composed of upper-case and lower-case ASCII letters (a-z, A-Z).
5642    *
5643    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5644    * <p class='bjava'>
5645    *    randomAlphabetic(<jk>5</jk>);  <jc>// "aBcDe"</jc>
5646    * </p>
5647    *
5648    * @param length The length of the generated string.
5649    * @return A new random alphabetic string.
5650    */
5651   public static String randomAlphabetic(int length) {
5652      if (length < 0)
5653         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Length must be non-negative: " + length);
5654      var sb = new StringBuilder(length);
5655      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
5656         var c = RANDOM.nextInt(52);
5657         if (c < 26)
5658            sb.append((char)('a' + c));
5659         else
5660            sb.append((char)('A' + c - 26));
5661      }
5662      return sb.toString();
5663   }
5664
5665   /**
5666    * Generates a random alphanumeric string of the specified length.
5667    *
5668    * <p>
5669    * Characters are composed of upper-case and lower-case ASCII letters and digits (a-z, A-Z, 0-9).
5670    *
5671    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5672    * <p class='bjava'>
5673    *    randomAlphanumeric(<jk>8</jk>);  <jc>// "aB3dE5fG"</jc>
5674    * </p>
5675    *
5676    * @param length The length of the generated string.
5677    * @return A new random alphanumeric string.
5678    */
5679   public static String randomAlphanumeric(int length) {
5680      if (length < 0)
5681         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Length must be non-negative: " + length);
5682      var sb = new StringBuilder(length);
5683      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
5684         var c = RANDOM.nextInt(62);
5685         if (c < 10)
5686            sb.append((char)('0' + c));
5687         else if (c < 36)
5688            sb.append((char)('a' + c - 10));
5689         else
5690            sb.append((char)('A' + c - 36));
5691      }
5692      return sb.toString();
5693   }
5694
5695   /**
5696    * Generates a random ASCII string of the specified length.
5697    *
5698    * <p>
5699    * Characters are composed of printable ASCII characters (32-126).
5700    *
5701    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5702    * <p class='bjava'>
5703    *    randomAscii(<jk>10</jk>);  <jc>// "!@#$%^&amp;*()"</jc>
5704    * </p>
5705    *
5706    * @param length The length of the generated string.
5707    * @return A new random ASCII string.
5708    */
5709   public static String randomAscii(int length) {
5710      if (length < 0)
5711         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Length must be non-negative: " + length);
5712      var sb = new StringBuilder(length);
5713      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
5714         sb.append((char)(32 + RANDOM.nextInt(95))); // 95 printable ASCII chars (32-126)
5715      }
5716      return sb.toString();
5717   }
5718
5719   /**
5720    * Generates a random numeric string of the specified length.
5721    *
5722    * <p>
5723    * Characters are composed of digits (0-9).
5724    *
5725    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5726    * <p class='bjava'>
5727    *    randomNumeric(<jk>6</jk>);  <jc>// "123456"</jc>
5728    * </p>
5729    *
5730    * @param length The length of the generated string.
5731    * @return A new random numeric string.
5732    */
5733   public static String randomNumeric(int length) {
5734      if (length < 0)
5735         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Length must be non-negative: " + length);
5736      var sb = new StringBuilder(length);
5737      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
5738         sb.append((char)('0' + RANDOM.nextInt(10)));
5739      }
5740      return sb.toString();
5741   }
5742
5743   /**
5744    * Generates a random string of the specified length using characters from the given character set.
5745    *
5746    * <p>
5747    * Each character in the generated string is randomly selected from the provided character set.
5748    *
5749    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5750    * <p class='bjava'>
5751    *    randomString(<jk>5</jk>, <js>"ABC"</js>);  <jc>// "BACAB"</jc>
5752    * </p>
5753    *
5754    * @param length The length of the generated string.
5755    * @param chars The character set to use. Must not be null or empty.
5756    * @return A new random string.
5757    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If chars is null or empty, or length is negative.
5758    */
5759   public static String randomString(int length, String chars) {
5760      if (length < 0)
5761         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Length must be non-negative: " + length);
5762      if (chars == null || chars.isEmpty())
5763         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Character set must not be null or empty");
5764      var sb = new StringBuilder(length);
5765      var charsLen = chars.length();
5766      for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
5767         sb.append(chars.charAt(RANDOM.nextInt(charsLen)));
5768      }
5769      return sb.toString();
5770   }
5771
5772   /**
5773    * Calculates a simple readability score for a string.
5774    *
5775    * <p>
5776    * Uses a simplified Flesch Reading Ease-like formula based on:
5777    * <ul>
5778    *   <li>Average words per sentence</li>
5779    *   <li>Average syllables per word (estimated)</li>
5780    * </ul>
5781    * Returns a score from 0-100, where higher scores indicate easier reading.
5782    *
5783    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5784    * <p class='bjava'>
5785    *    readabilityScore(<js>"The cat sat."</js>);      <jc>// Higher score (simple)</jc>
5786    *    readabilityScore(<js>"The sophisticated..."</js>); <jc>// Lower score (complex)</jc>
5787    * </p>
5788    *
5789    * @param str The string to analyze. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
5790    * @return A readability score from 0-100, or <c>0.0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
5791    */
5792   public static double readabilityScore(String str) {
5793      if (isEmpty(str))
5794         return 0.0;
5795
5796      var words = extractWords(str);
5797      if (words.isEmpty())
5798         return 0.0;
5799
5800      // Count sentences (ending with . ! ?)
5801      var sentenceCount = 0;
5802      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
5803         var c = str.charAt(i);
5804         if (c == '.' || c == '!' || c == '?') {
5805            sentenceCount++;
5806         }
5807      }
5808      if (sentenceCount == 0)
5809         sentenceCount = 1; // At least one sentence
5810
5811      // Calculate average words per sentence
5812      var avgWordsPerSentence = (double)words.size() / sentenceCount;
5813
5814      // Estimate average syllables per word (simplified: count vowel groups)
5815      var totalSyllables = 0;
5816      for (var word : words) {
5817         totalSyllables += estimateSyllables(word);
5818      }
5819      var avgSyllablesPerWord = (double)totalSyllables / words.size();
5820
5821      // Simplified Flesch Reading Ease formula
5822      // Score = 206.835 - (1.015 * ASL) - (84.6 * ASW)
5823      // Where ASL = average sentence length (words), ASW = average syllables per word
5824      var score = 206.835 - (1.015 * avgWordsPerSentence) - (84.6 * avgSyllablesPerWord);
5825
5826      // Clamp to 0-100 range
5827      return Math.max(0.0, Math.min(100.0, score));
5828   }
5829
5830   /**
5831    * Converts an arbitrary object to a readable string format suitable for debugging and testing.
5832    *
5833    * <p>This method provides intelligent formatting for various Java types, recursively processing
5834    * nested structures to create human-readable representations. It's extensively used throughout
5835    * the Juneau framework for test assertions and debugging output.</p>
5836    *
