5837    * <h5 class='section'>Type-Specific Formatting:</h5>
5838    * <ul>
5839    *    <li><b>null:</b> Returns <js>null</js></li>
5840    *    <li><b>Optional:</b> Recursively formats the contained value (or <js>null</js> if empty)</li>
5841    *    <li><b>Collections:</b> Formats as <js>"[item1,item2,item3]"</js> with comma-separated elements</li>
5842    *    <li><b>Maps:</b> Formats as <js>"{key1=value1,key2=value2}"</js> with comma-separated entries</li>
5843    *    <li><b>Map.Entry:</b> Formats as <js>"key=value"</js></li>
5844    *    <li><b>Arrays:</b> Converts to list format <js>"[item1,item2,item3]"</js></li>
5845    *    <li><b>Iterables/Iterators/Enumerations:</b> Converts to list and formats recursively</li>
5846    *    <li><b>GregorianCalendar:</b> Formats as ISO instant timestamp</li>
5847    *    <li><b>Date:</b> Formats as ISO instant string (e.g., <js>"2023-12-25T10:30:00Z"</js>)</li>
5848    *    <li><b>InputStream:</b> Converts to hexadecimal representation</li>
5849    *    <li><b>Reader:</b> Reads content and returns as string</li>
5850    *    <li><b>File:</b> Reads file content and returns as string</li>
5851    *    <li><b>byte[]:</b> Converts to hexadecimal representation</li>
5852    *    <li><b>Enum:</b> Returns the enum name via {@link Enum#name()}</li>
5853    *    <li><b>All other types:</b> Uses {@link Object#toString()}</li>
5854    * </ul>
5855    *
5856    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
5857    * <p class='bjava'>
5858    *    <jc>// Collections</jc>
5859    *    readable(List.of("a", "b", "c")) <jc>// Returns: "[a,b,c]"</jc>
5860    *    readable(Set.of(1, 2, 3)) <jc>// Returns: "[1,2,3]" (order may vary)</jc>
5861    *
5862    *    <jc>// Maps</jc>
5863    *    readable(Map.of("foo", "bar", "baz", 123)) <jc>// Returns: "{foo=bar,baz=123}"</jc>
5864    *
5865    *    <jc>// Arrays</jc>
5866    *    readable(ints(1, 2, 3)) <jc>// Returns: "[1,2,3]"</jc>
5867    *    readable(new String[]{"a", "b"}) <jc>// Returns: "[a,b]"</jc>
5868    *
5869    *    <jc>// Nested structures</jc>
5870    *    readable(List.of(Map.of("x", 1), Set.of("a", "b"))) <jc>// Returns: "[{x=1},[a,b]]"</jc>
5871    *
5872    *    <jc>// Special types</jc>
5873    *    readable(Optional.of("test")) <jc>// Returns: "test"</jc>
5874    *    readable(Optional.empty()) <jc>// Returns: null</jc>
5875    *    readable(new Date(1640995200000L)) <jc>// Returns: "2022-01-01T00:00:00Z"</jc>
5876    *    readable(MyEnum.FOO) <jc>// Returns: "FOO"</jc>
5877    * </p>
5878    *
5879    * <h5 class='section'>Recursive Processing:</h5>
5880    * <p>The method recursively processes nested structures, so complex objects containing
5881    * collections, maps, and arrays are fully flattened into readable format. This makes it
5882    * ideal for test assertions where you need to compare complex object structures.</p>
5883    *
5884    * <h5 class='section'>Error Handling:</h5>
5885    * <p>IO operations (reading files, streams) are wrapped in safe() calls, converting
5886    * any exceptions to RuntimeExceptions. Binary data (InputStreams, byte arrays) is
5887    * converted to hexadecimal representation for readability.</p>
5888    *
5889    * @param o The object to convert to readable format. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
5890    * @return A readable string representation of the object, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
5891    */
5892   public static String readable(Object o) {
5893      if (o == null)
5894         return null;
5895      var c = o.getClass();
5896
5897      // Special case for byte[] - must be handled before general array check
5898      if (c == byte[].class) {
5899         return toHex((byte[])o);
5900      }
5901
5902      // Check cache first
5903      var f = READIFIER_CACHE.get(c, () -> {
5904         // Find readifier from READIFIERS list
5905         // First try exact match, then isAssignableFrom
5906         var readifier = READIFIERS.stream()
5907            .filter(r -> r.forClass() == c || r.forClass().isAssignableFrom(c))
5908            .map(Readifier::toFunction)
5909            .findFirst()
5910            .orElse(null);
5911
5912         // If readifier found, use it
5913         if (readifier != null)
5914            return readifier;
5915
5916         // If no readifier found, check if it's an array
5917         if (c.isArray()) {
5918            return x -> {
5919               var l = list();
5920               for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(x); i++) {
5921                  l.add(Array.get(x, i));
5922               }
5923               return readable(l);
5924            };
5925         }
5926
5927         // If no readifier found, use toString() as fallback
5928         return Object::toString;
5929      });
5930
5931      return f.apply(o);
5932   }
5933
5934   /**
5935    * Removes all occurrences of a substring from a string.
5936    *
5937    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5938    * <p class='bjava'>
5939    *    remove(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"x"</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
5940    *    remove(<js>"hello"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// "hello"</jc>
5941    *    remove(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"o"</js>);     <jc>// "hell wrld"</jc>
5942    *    remove(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);   <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
5943    * </p>
5944    *
5945    * @param str The string to process.
5946    * @param remove The substring to remove.
5947    * @return The string with all occurrences of the substring removed, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
5948    */
5949   public static String remove(String str, String remove) {
5950      if (isEmpty(str) || isEmpty(remove))
5951         return str;
5952      return str.replace(remove, "");
5953   }
5954
5955   /**
5956    * Removes diacritical marks (accents) from characters in a string.
5957    *
5958    * <p>
5959    * Normalizes the string to NFD form and removes combining diacritical marks.
5960    *
5961    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5962    * <p class='bjava'>
5963    *    removeAccents(<js>"café"</js>);     <jc>// "cafe"</jc>
5964    *    removeAccents(<js>"naïve"</js>);    <jc>// "naive"</jc>
5965    *    removeAccents(<js>"résumé"</js>);   <jc>// "resume"</jc>
5966    * </p>
5967    *
5968    * @param str The string to remove accents from. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
5969    * @return The string with accents removed, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
5970    */
5971   public static String removeAccents(String str) {
5972      if (str == null)
5973         return null;
5974
5975      // Normalize to NFD (decomposed form)
5976      var normalized = Normalizer.normalize(str, Normalizer.Form.NFD);
5977
5978      // Remove combining diacritical marks (Unicode category Mn)
5979      var sb = new StringBuilder(normalized.length());
5980      for (var i = 0; i < normalized.length(); i++) {
5981         var c = normalized.charAt(i);
5982         var type = Character.getType(c);
5983         // Mn = Nonspacing_Mark (combining marks)
5984         if (type != Character.NON_SPACING_MARK) {
5985            sb.append(c);
5986         }
5987      }
5988
5989      return sb.toString();
5990   }
5991
5992   /**
5993    * Removes multiple substrings from a string.
5994    *
5995    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5996    * <p class='bjava'>
5997    *    removeAll(<js>"hello world test"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>, <js>"test"</js>);  <jc>// " world "</jc>
5998    *    removeAll(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"x"</js>);                                     <jc>// null</jc>
5999    * </p>
6000    *
6001    * @param str The string to process.
6002    * @param remove The substrings to remove.
6003    * @return The string with all specified substrings removed, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6004    */
6005   public static String removeAll(String str, String...remove) {
6006      if (str == null)
6007         return null;
6008      if (isEmpty(str) || remove == null || remove.length == 0)
6009         return str;
6010      var result = str;
6011      for (var r : remove) {
6012         if (r != null)
6013            result = result.replace(r, "");
6014      }
6015      return result;
6016   }
6017
6018   /**
6019    * Removes control characters from a string, replacing them with spaces.
6020    *
6021    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6022    * <p class='bjava'>
6023    *    removeControlChars(<js>"hello\u0000\u0001world"</js>);   <jc>// "hello  world"</jc>
6024    *    removeControlChars(<js>"hello\nworld"</js>);             <jc>// "hello\nworld"</jc>
6025    * </p>
6026    *
6027    * @param str The string to process.
6028    * @return The string with control characters replaced by spaces (except whitespace control chars), or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6029    */
6030   public static String removeControlChars(String str) {
6031      if (str == null)
6032         return null;
6033      var sb = new StringBuilder();
6034      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
6035         var c = str.charAt(i);
6036         if (Character.isISOControl(c) && ! Character.isWhitespace(c))
6037            sb.append(' ');
6038         else
6039            sb.append(c);
6040      }
6041      return sb.toString();
6042   }
6043
6044   /**
6045    * Removes a suffix from a string if present.
6046    *
6047    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6048    * <p class='bjava'>
6049    *    removeEnd(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"x"</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
6050    *    removeEnd(<js>"hello"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// "hello"</jc>
6051    *    removeEnd(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"world"</js>); <jc>// "hello "</jc>
6052    *    removeEnd(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);   <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
6053    * </p>
6054    *
6055    * @param str The string to process.
6056    * @param suffix The suffix to remove.
6057    * @return The string with the suffix removed if present, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6058    */
6059   public static String removeEnd(String str, String suffix) {
6060      if (isEmpty(str) || isEmpty(suffix))
6061         return str;
6062      if (str.endsWith(suffix))
6063         return str.substring(0, str.length() - suffix.length());
6064      return str;
6065   }
6066
6067   /**
6068    * Removes non-printable characters from a string.
6069    *
6070    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6071    * <p class='bjava'>
6072    *    removeNonPrintable(<js>"hello\u0000world"</js>);   <jc>// "helloworld"</jc>
6073    * </p>
6074    *
6075    * @param str The string to process.
6076    * @return The string with non-printable characters removed, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6077    */
6078   public static String removeNonPrintable(String str) {
6079      if (str == null)
6080         return null;
6081      return str.replaceAll("\\p{C}", "");
6082   }
6083
6084   /**
6085    * Removes a prefix from a string if present.
6086    *
6087    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6088    * <p class='bjava'>
6089    *    removeStart(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"x"</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
6090    *    removeStart(<js>"hello"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// "hello"</jc>
6091    *    removeStart(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>); <jc>// " world"</jc>
6092    *    removeStart(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);   <jc>// "hello world"</jc>
6093    * </p>
6094    *
6095    * @param str The string to process.
6096    * @param prefix The prefix to remove.
6097    * @return The string with the prefix removed if present, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6098    */
6099   public static String removeStart(String str, String prefix) {
6100      if (isEmpty(str) || isEmpty(prefix))
6101         return str;
6102      if (str.startsWith(prefix))
6103         return str.substring(prefix.length());
6104      return str;
6105   }
6106
6107   /**
6108    * Removes all underscore characters from a string.
6109    *
6110    * <p>
6111    * This method is commonly used to process numeric literals that may contain underscores for readability
6112    * (e.g., <js>"1_000_000"</js> becomes <js>"1000000"</js>), as Java allows underscores in numeric literals
6113    * but some parsing methods do not support them.
6114    *
6115    * <p>
6116    * If the string does not contain any underscores, the original string is returned (no new object created).
6117    *
6118    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6119    * <p class='bjava'>
6120    *    removeUnderscores(<js>"1_000_000"</js>);        <jc>// "1000000"</jc>
6121    *    removeUnderscores(<js>"1_000.5"</js>);          <jc>// "1000.5"</jc>
6122    *    removeUnderscores(<js>"hello_world"</js>);      <jc>// "helloworld"</jc>
6123    *    removeUnderscores(<js>"no_underscores"</js>);   <jc>// "nounderscores"</jc>
6124    *    removeUnderscores(<js>"Hello"</js>);            <jc>// "Hello" (no change, same object returned)</jc>
6125    * </p>
6126    *
6127    * @param value The string from which to remove underscores. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
6128    * @return A new string with all underscores removed, or the original string if it contains no underscores.
6129    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If <c>value</c> is <jk>null</jk>.
6130    * @see #parseInt(String)
6131    * @see #parseLong(String)
6132    * @see #parseFloat(String)
6133    * @see #parseNumber(String, Class)
6134    */
6135   public static String removeUnderscores(String value) {
6136      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
6137      return notContains(value, '_') ? value : value.replace("_", "");
6138   }
6139
6140   /**
6141    * Creates a repeated pattern.
6142    *
6143    * @param count The number of times to repeat the pattern.
6144    * @param pattern The pattern to repeat.
6145    * @return A new string consisting of the repeated pattern.
6146    */
6147   public static String repeat(int count, String pattern) {
6148      var sb = new StringBuilder(pattern.length() * count);
6149      for (var i = 0; i < count; i++)
6150         sb.append(pattern);
6151      return sb.toString();
6152   }
6153
6154   /**
6155    * Replaces <js>"\\uXXXX"</js> character sequences with their unicode characters.
6156    *
6157    * @param s The string to replace unicode sequences in.
6158    * @return A string with unicode sequences replaced.
6159    */
6160   public static String replaceUnicodeSequences(String s) {
6161
6162      if (s.indexOf('\\') == -1)
6163         return s;
6164
6165      var p = Pattern.compile("\\\\u(\\p{XDigit}{4})");
6166      var m = p.matcher(s);
6167      var sb = new StringBuffer(s.length());
6168
6169      while (m.find()) {
6170         var ch = String.valueOf((char)Integer.parseInt(m.group(1), 16));
6171         m.appendReplacement(sb, Matcher.quoteReplacement(ch));
6172      }
6173
6174      m.appendTail(sb);
6175      return sb.toString();
6176   }
6177
6178   /**
6179    * Reverses a string.
6180    *
6181    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6182    * <p class='bjava'>
6183    *    reverse(<jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// null</jc>
6184    *    reverse(<js>""</js>);            <jc>// ""</jc>
6185    *    reverse(<js>"hello"</js>);       <jc>// "olleh"</jc>
6186    * </p>
6187    *
6188    * @param str The string to reverse.
6189    * @return The reversed string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6190    */
6191   public static String reverse(String str) {
6192      if (str == null)
6193         return null;
6194      return new StringBuilder(str).reverse().toString();
6195   }
6196
6197   /**
6198    * Returns the rightmost characters of a string.
6199    *
6200    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6201    * <p class='bjava'>
6202    *    right(<jk>null</jk>, 3);          <jc>// null</jc>
6203    *    right(<js>""</js>, 3);            <jc>// ""</jc>
6204    *    right(<js>"hello"</js>, 3);       <jc>// "llo"</jc>
6205    *    right(<js>"hello"</js>, 10);      <jc>// "hello"</jc>
6206    * </p>
6207    *
6208    * @param str The string to get characters from.
6209    * @param len The number of characters to get.
6210    * @return The rightmost characters, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6211    */
6212   public static String right(String str, int len) {
6213      if (str == null)
6214         return null;
6215      if (len < 0)
6216         return "";
6217      if (len >= str.length())
6218         return str;
6219      return str.substring(str.length() - len);
6220   }
6221
6222   /**
6223    * Basic HTML/XML sanitization - removes or escapes potentially dangerous content.
6224    *
6225    * <p>
6226    * Removes HTML/XML tags and escapes special characters to prevent XSS attacks.
6227    * This is a basic sanitization - for production use, consider a more robust library.
6228    *
6229    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6230    * <p class='bjava'>
6231    *    sanitize(<js>"&lt;script&gt;alert('xss')&lt;/script&gt;"</js>);     <jc>// "&amp;lt;script&amp;gt;alert('xss')&amp;lt;/script&amp;gt;"</jc>
6232    *    sanitize(<js>"Hello &lt;b&gt;World&lt;/b&gt;"</js>);                <jc>// "Hello &amp;lt;b&amp;gt;World&amp;lt;/b&amp;gt;"</jc>
6233    * </p>
6234    *
6235    * @param str The string to sanitize.
6236    * @return The sanitized string with HTML/XML tags escaped, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6237    */
6238   public static String sanitize(String str) {
6239      if (str == null)
6240         return null;
6241      // Escape HTML/XML special characters
6242      return escapeHtml(str);
6243   }
6244
6245   /**
6246    * Calculates the similarity percentage between two strings using Levenshtein distance.
6247    *
6248    * <p>
6249    * Returns a value between 0.0 (completely different) and 1.0 (identical).
6250    *
6251    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6252    * <p class='bjava'>
6253    *    similarity(<js>"hello"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);           <jc>// 1.0 (100%)</jc>
6254    *    similarity(<js>"kitten"</js>, <js>"sitting"</js>);        <jc>// ~0.57 (57%)</jc>
6255    *    similarity(<js>"abc"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);               <jc>// 0.0 (0%)</jc>
6256    * </p>
6257    *
6258    * @param str1 The first string.
6259    * @param str2 The second string.
6260    * @return A similarity value between 0.0 and 1.0, where 1.0 means identical.
6261    */
6262   public static double similarity(String str1, String str2) {
6263      if (str1 == null)
6264         str1 = "";
6265      if (str2 == null)
6266         str2 = "";
6267
6268      if (str1.equals(str2))
6269         return 1.0;
6270
6271      var maxLen = Math.max(str1.length(), str2.length());
6272
6273      var distance = levenshteinDistance(str1, str2);
6274      return 1.0 - ((double)distance / maxLen);
6275   }
6276
6277   /**
6278    * Converts a string to snake_case format.
6279    *
6280    * <p>
6281    * Handles various input formats:
6282    * <ul>
6283    *   <li>Space-separated: "hello world" → "hello_world"</li>
6284    *   <li>CamelCase: "helloWorld" → "hello_world"</li>
6285    *   <li>PascalCase: "HelloWorld" → "hello_world"</li>
6286    *   <li>Kebab-case: "hello-world" → "hello_world"</li>
6287    * </ul>
6288    *
6289    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6290    * <p class='bjava'>
6291    *    snakeCase(<jk>null</jk>);                    <jc>// null</jc>
6292    *    snakeCase(<js>""</js>);                      <jc>// ""</jc>
6293    *    snakeCase(<js>"hello world"</js>);           <jc>// "hello_world"</jc>
6294    *    snakeCase(<js>"helloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "hello_world"</jc>
6295    *    snakeCase(<js>"HelloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "hello_world"</jc>
6296    *    snakeCase(<js>"hello-world"</js>);           <jc>// "hello_world"</jc>
6297    * </p>
6298    *
6299    * @param str The string to convert.
6300    * @return The snake_case string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
6301    */
6302   public static String snakeCase(String str) {
6303      if (str == null)
6304         return null;
6305      if (isEmpty(str))
6306         return str;
6307
6308      var words = splitWords(str);
6309      if (words.isEmpty())
6310         return "";
6311
6312      var result = new StringBuilder();
6313      for (var i = 0; i < words.size(); i++) {
6314         if (i > 0)
6315            result.append('_');
6316         result.append(words.get(i).toLowerCase());
6317      }
6318
6319      return result.toString();
6320   }
6321
6322   /**
6323    * Sorts a string array in natural order.
6324    *
6325    * <p>
6326    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
6327    * This method creates a copy of the array and sorts it, leaving the original array unchanged.
6328    *
6329    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
6330    * <p class='bjava'>
6331    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"zebra"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>, <js>"banana"</js>};
6332    *    String[] <jv>sorted</jv> = sort(<jv>array</jv>);
6333    *    <jc>// Returns: ["apple", "banana", "zebra"]</jc>
6334    * </p>
6335    *
6336    * @param array The array to sort. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6337    * @return A new sorted array, or an empty array if the array was <jk>null</jk>.
6338    */
6339   public static String[] sort(String[] array) {
6340      if (array == null)
6341         return new String[0];
6342      var result = Arrays.copyOf(array, array.length);
6343      Arrays.sort(result);
6344      return result;
6345   }
6346
6347   /**
6348    * Sorts a string array in case-insensitive order.
6349    *
6350    * <p>
6351    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
6352    * This method creates a copy of the array and sorts it using case-insensitive comparison,
6353    * leaving the original array unchanged.
6354    *
6355    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
6356    * <p class='bjava'>
6357    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"Zebra"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>, <js>"Banana"</js>};
6358    *    String[] <jv>sorted</jv> = sortIgnoreCase(<jv>array</jv>);
6359    *    <jc>// Returns: ["apple", "Banana", "Zebra"]</jc>
6360    * </p>
6361    *
6362    * @param array The array to sort. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6363    * @return A new sorted array (case-insensitive), or an empty array if the array was <jk>null</jk>.
6364    */
6365   public static String[] sortIgnoreCase(String[] array) {
6366      if (array == null)
6367         return new String[0];
6368      var result = Arrays.copyOf(array, array.length);
6369      Arrays.sort(result, String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER);
6370      return result;
6371   }
6372
6373   /**
6374    * Generates a Soundex code for a string.
6375    *
6376    * <p>
6377    * Soundex is a phonetic algorithm for indexing names by sound. The code consists of
6378    * a letter followed by three digits. Similar-sounding names produce the same code.
6379    *
6380    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6381    * <p class='bjava'>
6382    *    soundex(<js>"Smith"</js>);   <jc>// "S530"</jc>
6383    *    soundex(<js>"Smythe"</js>);  <jc>// "S530"</jc>
6384    *    soundex(<js>"Robert"</js>);  <jc>// "R163"</jc>
6385    * </p>
6386    *
6387    * @param str The string to generate a Soundex code for. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6388    * @return The Soundex code (1 letter + 3 digits), or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
6389    */
6390   public static String soundex(String str) {
6391      if (isEmpty(str))
6392         return null;
6393
6394      var upper = str.toUpperCase();
6395      var result = new StringBuilder(4);
6396      result.append(upper.charAt(0));
6397
6398      // Soundex mapping: 0 = AEIOUHWY, 1 = BFPV, 2 = CGJKQSXZ, 3 = DT, 4 = L, 5 = MN, 6 = R
6399      // H/W/Y don't get codes but don't break sequences either
6400      // Initialize lastCode to a value that won't match any real code
6401      var lastCode = '\0';
6402
6403      for (var i = 1; i < upper.length() && result.length() < 4; i++) {
6404         var c = upper.charAt(i);
6405         var code = getSoundexCode(c);
6406         if (code == '0') {
6407            // H/W/Y/vowels - don't add code, but don't update lastCode either
6408            // This allows sequences to continue across H/W/Y/vowels
6409            continue;
6410         }
6411         if (code != lastCode) {
6412            result.append(code);
6413            lastCode = code;
6414         }
6415         // If code == lastCode, skip it (consecutive same codes)
6416      }
6417
6418      // Pad with zeros if needed
6419      while (result.length() < 4) {
6420         result.append('0');
6421      }
6422
6423      return result.toString();
6424   }
6425
6426   /**
6427    * Splits a comma-delimited list into a list of strings.
6428    *
6429    * @param s The string to split.
6430    * @return A list of split strings, or an empty list if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
6431    */
6432   public static List<String> split(String s) {
6433      return s == null ? Collections.emptyList() : split(s, ',');
6434   }
6435
6436   /**
6437    * Splits a character-delimited string into a string array.
6438    *
6439    * <p>
6440    * Does not split on escaped-delimiters (e.g. "\,");
6441    * Resulting tokens are trimmed of whitespace.
6442    *
6443    * <p>
6444    * <b>NOTE:</b>  This behavior is different than the Jakarta equivalent.
6445    * split("a,b,c",',') -&gt; {"a","b","c"}
6446    * split("a, b ,c ",',') -&gt; {"a","b","c"}
6447    * split("a,,c",',') -&gt; {"a","","c"}
6448    * split(",,",',') -&gt; {"","",""}
6449    * split("",',') -&gt; {}
6450    * split(null,',') -&gt; {}
6451    * split("a,b\,c,d", ',', false) -&gt; {"a","b\,c","d"}
6452    * split("a,b\\,c,d", ',', false) -&gt; {"a","b\","c","d"}
6453    * split("a,b\,c,d", ',', true) -&gt; {"a","b,c","d"}
6454    *
6455    * @param s The string to split.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6456    * @param c The character to split on.
6457    * @return The tokens, or an empty list if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
6458    */
6459   public static List<String> split(String s, char c) {
6460      return split(s, c, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
6461   }
6462
6463   /**
6464    * Same as {@link #splita(String, char)} but consumes the tokens instead of creating an array.
6465    *
6466    * @param s The string to split.
6467    * @param c The character to split on.
6468    * @param consumer The consumer of the tokens.
6469    */
6470   public static void split(String s, char c, Consumer<String> consumer) {
6471      var escapeChars = getEscapeSet(c);
6472
6473      if (isEmpty(s))
6474         return;
6475      if (s.indexOf(c) == -1) {
6476         consumer.accept(s);
6477         return;
6478      }
6479
6480      var x1 = 0;
6481      var escapeCount = 0;
6482
6483      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
6484         if (s.charAt(i) == '\\')
6485            escapeCount++;
6486         else if (s.charAt(i) == c && escapeCount % 2 == 0) {
6487            var s2 = s.substring(x1, i);
6488            var s3 = unescapeChars(s2, escapeChars);
6489            consumer.accept(s3.trim());
6490            x1 = i + 1;
6491         }
6492         if (s.charAt(i) != '\\')
6493            escapeCount = 0;
6494      }
6495      var s2 = s.substring(x1);
6496      var s3 = unescapeChars(s2, escapeChars);
6497      consumer.accept(s3.trim());
6498   }
6499
6500   /**
6501    * Same as {@link #splita(String, char, int)} but returns a {@link List}.
6502    *
6503    * @param s The string to split.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6504    * @param c The character to split on.
6505    * @param limit The maximum number of tokens to return.
6506    * @return The tokens, or an empty list if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
6507    */
6508   public static List<String> split(String s, char c, int limit) {
6509
6510      var escapeChars = getEscapeSet(c);
6511
6512      if (s == null)
6513         return Collections.emptyList();
6514      if (isEmpty(s))
6515         return Collections.emptyList();
6516      if (s.indexOf(c) == -1)
6517         return Collections.singletonList(s);
6518
6519      var l = new LinkedList<String>();
6520      var sArray = s.toCharArray();
6521      var x1 = 0;
6522      var escapeCount = 0;
6523      limit--;
6524      for (var i = 0; i < sArray.length && limit > 0; i++) {
6525         if (sArray[i] == '\\')
6526            escapeCount++;
6527         else if (sArray[i] == c && escapeCount % 2 == 0) {
6528            var s2 = new String(sArray, x1, i - x1);
6529            var s3 = unescapeChars(s2, escapeChars);
6530            l.add(s3.trim());
6531            limit--;
6532            x1 = i + 1;
6533         }
6534         if (sArray[i] != '\\')
6535            escapeCount = 0;
6536      }
6537      var s2 = new String(sArray, x1, sArray.length - x1);
6538      var s3 = unescapeChars(s2, escapeChars);
6539      l.add(s3.trim());
6540
6541      return l;
6542   }
6543
6544   /**
6545    * Same as {@link #splita(String)} but consumes the tokens instead of creating an array.
6546    *
6547    * @param s The string to split.
6548    * @param consumer The consumer of the tokens.
6549    */
6550   public static void split(String s, Consumer<String> consumer) {
6551      StringUtils.split(s, ',', consumer);
6552   }
6553
6554   /**
6555    * Splits a comma-delimited list into an array of strings.
6556    *
6557    * @param s The string to split.
6558    * @return An array of split strings.
6559    */
6560   public static String[] splita(String s) {
6561      return splita(s, ',');
6562   }
6563
6564   /**
6565    * Splits a character-delimited string into a string array.
6566    *
6567    * <p>
6568    * Does not split on escaped-delimiters (e.g. "\,");
6569    * Resulting tokens are trimmed of whitespace.
6570    *
6571    * <p>
6572    * <b>NOTE:</b>  This behavior is different than the Jakarta equivalent.
6573    * split("a,b,c",',') -&gt; {"a","b","c"}
6574    * split("a, b ,c ",',') -&gt; {"a","b","c"}
6575    * split("a,,c",',') -&gt; {"a","","c"}
6576    * split(",,",',') -&gt; {"","",""}
6577    * split("",',') -&gt; {}
6578    * split(null,',') -&gt; null
6579    * split("a,b\,c,d", ',', false) -&gt; {"a","b\,c","d"}
6580    * split("a,b\\,c,d", ',', false) -&gt; {"a","b\","c","d"}
6581    * split("a,b\,c,d", ',', true) -&gt; {"a","b,c","d"}
6582    *
6583    * @param s The string to split.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6584    * @param c The character to split on.
6585    * @return The tokens, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was null.
6586    */
6587   public static String[] splita(String s, char c) {
6588      return splita(s, c, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
6589   }
6590
6591   /**
6592    * Same as {@link #splita(String, char)} but limits the number of tokens returned.
6593    *
6594    * @param s The string to split.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6595    * @param c The character to split on.
6596    * @param limit The maximum number of tokens to return.
6597    * @return The tokens, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was null.
6598    */
6599   @SuppressWarnings({
6600      "java:S1168"     // splita used widely; null propagates from split(). Consider empty array. See BasicCsvHeader, etc.
6601   })
6602   public static String[] splita(String s, char c, int limit) {
6603      if (s == null)
6604         return null;
6605      var l = StringUtils.split(s, c, limit);
6606      return l.toArray(new String[l.size()]);
6607   }
6608
6609   /**
6610    * Same as {@link #splita(String, char)} except splits all strings in the input and returns a single result.
6611    *
6612    * @param s The string to split.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6613    * @param c The character to split on.
6614    * @return The tokens, or null if the input array was null
6615    */
6616   @SuppressWarnings({
6617      "java:S1168"     // splita used widely. Consider empty array. See BasicCsvHeader, etc.
6618   })
6619   public static String[] splita(String[] s, char c) {
6620      if (s == null)
6621         return null;
6622      var l = new LinkedList<String>();
6623      for (var ss : s) {
6624         if (ss == null || ss.indexOf(c) == -1)
6625            l.add(ss);
6626         else
6627            Collections.addAll(l, splita(ss, c));
6628      }
6629      return l.toArray(new String[l.size()]);
6630   }
6631
6632   /**
6633    * Splits a list of key-value pairs into an ordered map.
6634    *
6635    * <p>
6636    * Example:
6637    * <p class='bjava'>
6638    *    String <jv>in</jv> = <js>"foo=1;bar=2"</js>;
6639    *    Map <jv>map</jv> = StringUtils.<jsm>splitMap</jsm>(in, <js>';'</js>, <js>'='</js>, <jk>true</jk>);
6640    * </p>
6641    *
6642    * @param s The string to split.
6643    * @param trim Trim strings after parsing.
6644    * @return The parsed map, or an empty map if the string was null.
6645    */
6646   @SuppressWarnings({
6647      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for map splitting logic
6648   })
6649   public static Map<String,String> splitMap(String s, boolean trim) {
6650
6651      if (s == null)
6652         return mape();
6653      if (isEmpty(s))
6654         return mape();
6655
6656      var m = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
6657
6658      // S1: Found start of key, looking for equals.
6659      // S2: Found equals, looking for delimiter (or end).
6660
6661      var state = S1;
6662
6663      var sArray = s.toCharArray();
6664      var x1 = 0;
6665      var escapeCount = 0;
6666      String key = null;
6667      for (var i = 0; i < sArray.length + 1; i++) {
6668         var c = i == sArray.length ? ',' : sArray[i];
6669         if (c == '\\')
6670            escapeCount++;
6671         if (escapeCount % 2 == 0) {
6672            if (state == S1) {
6673               if (c == '=') {
6674                  key = s.substring(x1, i);
6675                  if (trim)
6676                     key = trim(key);
6677                  key = unescapeChars(key, MAP_ESCAPE_SET);
6678                  state = S2;
6679                  x1 = i + 1;
6680               } else if (c == ',') {
6681                  key = s.substring(x1, i);
6682                  if (trim)
6683                     key = trim(key);
6684                  key = unescapeChars(key, MAP_ESCAPE_SET);
6685                  m.put(key, "");
6686                  state = S1;
6687                  x1 = i + 1;
6688               }
6689            } else /* state == S2 */ {
6690               if (c == ',') {
6691                  var val = s.substring(x1, i);
6692                  if (trim)
6693                     val = trim(val);
6694                  val = unescapeChars(val, MAP_ESCAPE_SET);
6695                  m.put(key, val);
6696                  key = null;
6697                  x1 = i + 1;
6698                  state = S1;
6699               }
6700            }
6701         }
6702         if (c != '\\')
6703            escapeCount = 0;
6704      }
6705
6706      return m;
6707   }
6708
6709   /**
6710    * Splits the method arguments in the signature of a method.
6711    *
6712    * @param s The arguments to split.
6713    * @return The split arguments, or null if the input string is null.
6714    */
6715   public static String[] splitMethodArgs(String s) {
6716
6717      if (s == null)
6718         return new String[0];
6719      if (isEmpty(s))
6720         return new String[0];
6721      if (s.indexOf(',') == -1)
6722         return a(s);
6723
6724      var l = new LinkedList<String>();
6725      var sArray = s.toCharArray();
6726      var x1 = 0;
6727      var paramDepth = 0;
6728
6729      for (var i = 0; i < sArray.length; i++) {
6730         var c = s.charAt(i);
6731         if (c == '>')
6732            paramDepth++;
6733         else if (c == '<')
6734            paramDepth--;
6735         else if (c == ',' && paramDepth == 0) {
6736            var s2 = new String(sArray, x1, i - x1);
6737            l.add(s2.trim());
6738            x1 = i + 1;
6739         }
6740      }
6741
6742      var s2 = new String(sArray, x1, sArray.length - x1);
6743      l.add(s2.trim());
6744
6745      return l.toArray(new String[l.size()]);
6746   }
6747
6748   /**
6749    * Splits a comma-delimited list containing "nesting constructs".
6750    *
6751    * Nesting constructs are simple embedded "{...}" comma-delimted lists.
6752    *
6753    * Example:
6754    *    "a{b,c},d" -> ["a{b,c}","d"]
6755    *
6756    * Handles escapes and trims whitespace from tokens.
6757    *
6758    * @param s The input string.
6759    * @return
6760    *    The results, or an empty list if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
6761    *    <br>An empty string results in an empty list.
6762    */
6763   @SuppressWarnings({
6764      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for nested string splitting
6765   })
6766   public static List<String> splitNested(String s) {
6767      var escapeChars = getEscapeSet(',');
6768
6769      if (s == null)
6770         return Collections.emptyList();
6771      if (isEmpty(s))
6772         return Collections.emptyList();
6773      if (s.indexOf(',') == -1)
6774         return Collections.singletonList(trim(s));
6775
6776      var l = new LinkedList<String>();
6777
6778      var x1 = 0;
6779      var inEscape = false;
6780      var depthCount = 0;
6781
6782      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
6783         var c = s.charAt(i);
6784         if (inEscape) {
6785            if (c == '\\') {
6786               inEscape = false;
6787            }
6788         } else {
6789            if (c == '\\') {
6790               inEscape = true;
6791            } else if (c == '{') {
6792               depthCount++;
6793            } else if (c == '}') {
6794               depthCount--;
6795            } else if (c == ',' && depthCount == 0) {
6796               l.add(trim(unescapeChars(s.substring(x1, i), escapeChars)));
6797               x1 = i + 1;
6798            }
6799         }
6800      }
6801      l.add(trim(unescapeChars(s.substring(x1, s.length()), escapeChars)));
6802
6803      return l;
6804   }
6805
6806   /**
6807    * Splits a nested comma-delimited list.
6808    *
6809    * Nesting constructs are simple embedded "{...}" comma-delimted lists.
6810    *
6811    * Example:
6812    *    "a{b,c{d,e}}" -> ["b","c{d,e}"]
6813    *
6814    * Handles escapes and trims whitespace from tokens.
6815    *
6816    * @param s The input string.
6817    * @return
6818    *    The results, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
6819    *    <br>An empty string results in an empty array.
6820    */
6821   @SuppressWarnings({
6822      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for nested inner string splitting
6823   })
6824   public static List<String> splitNestedInner(String s) {
6825      assertArg(nn(s), "String was null.");
6826      assertArg(ne(s), "String was empty.");
6827
6828      // S1: Looking for '{'
6829      // S2: Found '{', looking for '}'
6830
6831      var start = -1;
6832      var end = -1;
6833      var state = S1;
6834
6835      var depth = 0;
6836      var inEscape = false;
6837
6838      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
6839         var c = s.charAt(i);
6840         if (inEscape) {
6841            if (c == '\\') {
6842               inEscape = false;
6843            }
6844         } else {
6845            if (c == '\\') {
6846               inEscape = true;
6847            } else if (state == S1) {
6848               if (c == '{') {
6849                  start = i + 1;
6850                  state = S2;
6851               }
6852            } else /* state == S2 */ {
6853               if (c == '{') {
6854                  depth++;
6855               } else if (depth > 0 && c == '}') {
6856                  depth--;
6857               } else if (c == '}') {
6858                  end = i;
6859                  break;
6860               }
6861            }
6862         }
6863      }
6864
6865      if (start == -1)
6866         throw illegalArg("Start character '{' not found in string:  {0}", s);
6867      if (end == -1)
6868         throw illegalArg("End character '}' not found in string  {0}", s);
6869      return splitNested(s.substring(start, end));
6870   }
6871
6872   /**
6873    * Splits a space-delimited string with optionally quoted arguments.
6874    *
6875    * <p>
6876    * Examples:
6877    * <ul>
6878    *    <li><js>"foo"</js> =&gt; <c>["foo"]</c>
6879    *    <li><js>" foo "</js> =&gt; <c>["foo"]</c>
6880    *    <li><js>"foo bar baz"</js> =&gt; <c>["foo","bar","baz"]</c>
6881    *    <li><js>"foo 'bar baz'"</js> =&gt; <c>["foo","bar baz"]</c>
6882    *    <li><js>"foo \"bar baz\""</js> =&gt; <c>["foo","bar baz"]</c>
6883    *    <li><js>"foo 'bar\'baz'"</js> =&gt; <c>["foo","bar'baz"]</c>
6884    * </ul>
6885    *
6886    * @param s The input string.
6887    * @return
6888    *    The results, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
6889    *    <br>An empty string results in an empty array.
6890    */
6891   public static String[] splitQuoted(String s) {
6892      return splitQuoted(s, false);
6893   }
6894
6895   /**
6896    * Same as {@link StringUtils#splitQuoted(String)} but allows you to optionally keep the quote characters.
6897    *
6898    * @param s The input string.
6899    * @param keepQuotes If <jk>true</jk>, quote characters are kept on the tokens.
6900    * @return
6901    *    The results, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
6902    *    <br>An empty string results in an empty array.
6903    */
6904   @SuppressWarnings({
6905      "java:S3776",    // Cognitive complexity acceptable for quoted string splitting
6906      "java:S2583"     // State variables persist across loop iterations
6907   })
6908   public static String[] splitQuoted(String s, boolean keepQuotes) {
6909
6910      if (s == null)
6911         return new String[0];
6912
6913      s = s.trim();
6914
6915      if (isEmpty(s))
6916         return a();
6917
6918      if (! containsAny(s, ' ', '\t', '\'', '"'))
6919         return a(s);
6920
6921      // S1: Looking for start of token.
6922      // S2: Found ', looking for end '
6923      // S3: Found ", looking for end "
6924      // S4: Found non-whitespace, looking for end whitespace.
6925
6926      var state = S1;
6927
6928      var isInEscape = false;
6929      var needsUnescape = false;
6930      var mark = 0;
6931
6932      var l = new ArrayList<String>();
6933      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
6934         var c = s.charAt(i);
6935
6936         if (state == S1) {
6937            if (c == '\'') {
6938               state = S2;
6939               mark = keepQuotes ? i : i + 1;
6940            } else if (c == '"') {
6941               state = S3;
6942               mark = keepQuotes ? i : i + 1;
6943            } else if (c != ' ' && c != '\t') {
6944               state = S4;
6945               mark = i;
6946            }
6947         } else if (state == S2 || state == S3) {
6948            if (c == '\\') {
6949               isInEscape = ! isInEscape;
6950               if (isInEscape)
6951                  needsUnescape = ! keepQuotes;
6952            } else if (! isInEscape) {
6953               if (c == (state == S2 ? '\'' : '"')) {
6954                  var s2 = s.substring(mark, keepQuotes ? i + 1 : i);
6955                  if (needsUnescape)
6956                     s2 = unescapeChars(s2, QUOTE_ESCAPE_SET);
6957                  l.add(s2);
6958                  state = S1;
6959                  isInEscape = needsUnescape = false;
6960               }
6961            } else {
6962               isInEscape = false;
6963            }
6964         } else /* state == S4 */ {
6965            if (c == ' ' || c == '\t') {
6966               l.add(s.substring(mark, i));
6967               state = S1;
6968            }
6969         }
6970      }
6971      if (state == S4)
6972         l.add(s.substring(mark));
6973      else if (state == S2 || state == S3)
6974         throw illegalArg("Unmatched string quotes: {0}", s);
6975      return l.toArray(new String[l.size()]);
6976   }
6977
6978   /**
6979    * An efficient method for checking if a string starts with a character.
6980    *
6981    * @param s The string to check.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
6982    * @param c The character to check for.
6983    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified string is not <jk>null</jk> and starts with the specified character.
6984    */
6985   public static boolean startsWith(String s, char c) {
6986      if (nn(s)) {
6987         var i = s.length();
6988         if (i > 0)
6989            return s.charAt(0) == c;
6990      }
6991      return false;
6992   }
6993
6994   /**
6995    * Checks if a string starts with a prefix, ignoring case.
6996    *
6997    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
6998    * <p class='bjava'>
6999    *    startsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"hello"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
7000    *    startsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>, <js>"HELLO"</js>);     <jc>// true</jc>
7001    *    startsWithIgnoreCase(<js>"hello world"</js>, <js>"world"</js>);     <jc>// false</jc>
7002    * </p>
7003    *
7004    * @param str The string to check.
7005    * @param prefix The prefix to check for.
7006    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string starts with the prefix (ignoring case), <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
7007    */
7008   public static boolean startsWithIgnoreCase(String str, String prefix) {
7009      if (str == null || prefix == null)
7010         return false;
7011      return str.toLowerCase().startsWith(prefix.toLowerCase());
7012   }
7013
7014   /**
7015    * Takes a supplier of any type and returns a {@link Supplier}{@code <String>}.
7016    *
7017    * <p>Useful when passing arguments to loggers.
7018    *
7019    * @param s The supplier.
7020    * @return A string supplier that calls {@link #readable(Object)} on the supplied value.
7021    */
7022   public static Supplier<String> stringSupplier(Supplier<?> s) {
7023      return () -> readable(s.get());
7024   }
7025
7026   /**
7027    * Strips the first and last character from a string.
7028    *
7029    * @param s The string to strip.
7030    * @return The striped string, or the same string if the input was <jk>null</jk> or less than length 2.
7031    */
7032   public static String strip(String s) {
7033      if (s == null || s.length() <= 1)
7034         return s;
7035      return s.substring(1, s.length() - 1);
7036   }
7037
7038   /**
7039    * Strips invalid characters such as CTRL characters from a string meant to be encoded
7040    * as an HTTP header value.
7041    *
7042    * @param s The string to strip chars from.
7043    * @return The string with invalid characters removed.
7044    */
7045   public static String stripInvalidHttpHeaderChars(String s) {
7046
7047      if (s == null)
7048         return null;
7049
7050      var needsReplace = false;
7051      for (var i = 0; i < s.length() && ! needsReplace; i++)
7052         needsReplace |= HTTP_HEADER_CHARS.contains(s.charAt(i));
7053
7054      if (! needsReplace)
7055         return s;
7056
7057      var sb = new StringBuilder(s.length());
7058      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7059         var c = s.charAt(i);
7060         if (HTTP_HEADER_CHARS.contains(c))
7061            sb.append(c);
7062      }
7063
7064      return sb.toString();
7065   }
7066
7067   /**
7068    * Returns the substring after the first occurrence of a separator.
7069    *
7070    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7071    * <p class='bjava'>
7072    *    substringAfter(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"."</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
7073    *    substringAfter(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);    <jc>// ""</jc>
7074    *    substringAfter(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <js>"."</js>);     <jc>// "world"</jc>
7075    *    substringAfter(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);   <jc>// ""</jc>
7076    * </p>
7077    *
7078    * @param str The string to get a substring from.
7079    * @param separator The separator string.
7080    * @return The substring after the first occurrence of the separator, or empty string if separator not found.
7081    */
7082   public static String substringAfter(String str, String separator) {
7083      if (isEmpty(str))
7084         return str;
7085      if (separator == null)
7086         return "";
7087      var pos = str.indexOf(separator);
7088      if (pos == -1)
7089         return "";
7090      return str.substring(pos + separator.length());
7091   }
7092
7093   /**
7094    * Returns the substring before the first occurrence of a separator.
7095    *
7096    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7097    * <p class='bjava'>
7098    *    substringBefore(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"."</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
7099    *    substringBefore(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);    <jc>// "hello.world"</jc>
7100    *    substringBefore(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <js>"."</js>);     <jc>// "hello"</jc>
7101    *    substringBefore(<js>"hello.world"</js>, <js>"xyz"</js>);   <jc>// "hello.world"</jc>
7102    * </p>
7103    *
7104    * @param str The string to get a substring from.
7105    * @param separator The separator string.
7106    * @return The substring before the first occurrence of the separator, or the original string if separator not found.
7107    */
7108   public static String substringBefore(String str, String separator) {
7109      if (isEmpty(str) || separator == null)
7110         return str;
7111      var pos = str.indexOf(separator);
7112      if (pos == -1)
7113         return str;
7114      return str.substring(0, pos);
7115   }
7116
7117   /**
7118    * Returns the substring between two delimiters.
7119    *
7120    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7121    * <p class='bjava'>
7122    *    substringBetween(<jk>null</jk>, <js>"&lt;"</js>, <js>"&gt;"</js>);              <jc>// null</jc>
7123    *    substringBetween(<js>"&lt;hello&gt;"</js>, <js>"&lt;"</js>, <js>"&gt;"</js>);   <jc>// "hello"</jc>
7124    *    substringBetween(<js>"&lt;hello&gt;"</js>, <js>"["</js>, <js>"]"</js>);         <jc>// null</jc>
7125    * </p>
7126    *
7127    * @param str The string to get a substring from.
7128    * @param open The opening delimiter.
7129    * @param close The closing delimiter.
7130    * @return The substring between the delimiters, or <jk>null</jk> if delimiters not found.
7131    */
7132   public static String substringBetween(String str, String open, String close) {
7133      if (str == null || open == null || close == null)
7134         return null;
7135      var start = str.indexOf(open);
7136      if (start == -1)
7137         return null;
7138      var end = str.indexOf(close, start + open.length());
7139      if (end == -1)
7140         return null;
7141      return str.substring(start + open.length(), end);
7142   }
7143
7144   /**
7145    * Swaps the case of all characters in a string.
7146    *
7147    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7148    * <p class='bjava'>
7149    *    swapCase(<js>"Hello World"</js>);   <jc>// "hELLO wORLD"</jc>
7150    *    swapCase(<js>"ABC123xyz"</js>);     <jc>// "abc123XYZ"</jc>
7151    * </p>
7152    *
7153    * @param str The string to process.
7154    * @return The string with case swapped, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7155    */
7156   public static String swapCase(String str) {
7157      if (str == null)
7158         return null;
7159      var sb = new StringBuilder(str.length());
7160      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
7161         var c = str.charAt(i);
7162         if (LETTER_UC.contains(c))
7163            sb.append(Character.toLowerCase(c));
7164         else if (LETTER_LC.contains(c))
7165            sb.append(Character.toUpperCase(c));
7166         else
7167            sb.append(c);
7168      }
7169      return sb.toString();
7170   }
7171
7172   /**
7173    * Converts a string to Title Case format (first letter of each word capitalized, separated by spaces).
7174    *
7175    * <p>
7176    * Handles various input formats:
7177    * <ul>
7178    *   <li>CamelCase: "helloWorld" → "Hello World"</li>
7179    *   <li>PascalCase: "HelloWorld" → "Hello World"</li>
7180    *   <li>Snake_case: "hello_world" → "Hello World"</li>
7181    *   <li>Kebab-case: "hello-world" → "Hello World"</li>
7182    * </ul>
7183    *
7184    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7185    * <p class='bjava'>
7186    *    titleCase(<jk>null</jk>);                    <jc>// null</jc>
7187    *    titleCase(<js>""</js>);                      <jc>// ""</jc>
7188    *    titleCase(<js>"hello world"</js>);           <jc>// "Hello World"</jc>
7189    *    titleCase(<js>"helloWorld"</js>);            <jc>// "Hello World"</jc>
7190    *    titleCase(<js>"hello_world"</js>);           <jc>// "Hello World"</jc>
7191    *    titleCase(<js>"hello-world"</js>);           <jc>// "Hello World"</jc>
7192    * </p>
7193    *
7194    * @param str The string to convert.
7195    * @return The Title Case string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7196    */
7197   public static String titleCase(String str) {
7198      if (str == null)
7199         return null;
7200      if (isEmpty(str))
7201         return str;
7202
7203      var words = splitWords(str);
7204      if (words.isEmpty())
7205         return "";
7206
7207      var result = new StringBuilder();
7208      for (var i = 0; i < words.size(); i++) {
7209         if (i > 0)
7210            result.append(' ');
7211         result.append(capitalize(words.get(i).toLowerCase()));
7212      }
7213
7214      return result.toString();
7215   }
7216
7217   /**
7218    * Converts the specified object to a comma-delimited list.
7219    *
7220    * @param o The object to convert.
7221    * @return The specified object as a comma-delimited list.
7222    */
7223   public static String toCdl(Object o) {
7224      if (o == null)
7225         return null;
7226      if (isArray(o)) {
7227         var sb = new StringBuilder();
7228         for (int i = 0, j = Array.getLength(o); i < j; i++) {
7229            if (i > 0)
7230               sb.append(", ");
7231            sb.append(Array.get(o, i));
7232         }
7233         return sb.toString();
7234      }
7235      if (o instanceof Collection<?> c)
7236         return join(c, ", ");
7237      return o.toString();
7238   }
7239
7240   /**
7241    * Converts the specified byte into a 2 hexadecimal characters.
7242    *
7243    * @param b The number to convert to hex.
7244    * @return A <code><jk>char</jk>[2]</code> containing the specified characters.
7245    */
7246   public static String toHex(byte b) {
7247      var c = new char[2];
7248      var v = b & 0xFF;
7249      c[0] = HEX[v >>> 4];
7250      c[1] = HEX[v & 0x0F];
7251      return new String(c);
7252   }
7253
7254   /**
7255    * Converts a byte array into a simple hexadecimal character string.
7256    *
7257    * @param bytes The bytes to convert to hexadecimal.
7258    * @return A new string consisting of hexadecimal characters.
7259    */
7260   public static String toHex(byte[] bytes) {
7261      var sb = new StringBuilder(bytes.length * 2);
7262      for (var element : bytes) {
7263         var v = element & 0xFF;
7264         sb.append(HEX[v >>> 4]).append(HEX[v & 0x0F]);
7265      }
7266      return sb.toString();
7267   }
7268
7269   /**
7270    * Converts the contents of the specified input stream to a hex string.
7271    *
7272    * @param is The input stream to convert.
7273    * @return The hex string representation of the input stream contents, or <jk>null</jk> if the stream is <jk>null</jk>.
7274    */
7275   public static String toHex(InputStream is) {
7276      return safe(() -> is == null ? null : toHex(readBytes(is)));
7277   }
7278
7279   /**
7280    * Converts the specified number into a 2 hexadecimal characters.
7281    *
7282    * @param num The number to convert to hex.
7283    * @return A <code><jk>char</jk>[2]</code> containing the specified characters.
7284    */
7285   public static char[] toHex2(int num) {
7286      if (num < 0 || num > 255)
7287         throw new NumberFormatException("toHex2 can only be used on numbers between 0 and 255");
7288      var n = new char[2];
7289      var a = num % 16;
7290      n[1] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7291      a = (num / 16) % 16;
7292      n[0] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7293      return n;
7294   }
7295
7296   /**
7297    * Converts the specified number into a 4 hexadecimal characters.
7298    *
7299    * @param num The number to convert to hex.
7300    * @return A <code><jk>char</jk>[4]</code> containing the specified characters.
7301    * @throws NumberFormatException If the number is negative.
7302    */
7303   public static char[] toHex4(int num) {
7304      if (num < 0)
7305         throw new NumberFormatException("toHex4 can only be used on non-negative numbers");
7306      var n = new char[4];
7307      var a = num % 16;
7308      n[3] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7309      var base = 16;
7310      for (var i = 1; i < 4; i++) {
7311         a = (num / base) % 16;
7312         base <<= 4;
7313         n[3 - i] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7314      }
7315      return n;
7316   }
7317
7318   /**
7319    * Converts the specified number into a 8 hexadecimal characters.
7320    *
7321    * @param num The number to convert to hex.
7322    * @return A <code><jk>char</jk>[8]</code> containing the specified characters.
7323    * @throws NumberFormatException If the number is negative.
7324    */
7325   public static char[] toHex8(long num) {
7326      if (num < 0)
7327         throw new NumberFormatException("toHex8 can only be used on non-negative numbers");
7328      var n = new char[8];
7329      var a = num % 16;
7330      n[7] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7331      var base = 16;
7332      for (var i = 1; i < 8; i++) {
7333         a = (num / base) % 16;
7334         base <<= 4;
7335         n[7 - i] = (char)(a > 9 ? 'A' + a - 10 : '0' + a);
7336      }
7337      return n;
7338   }
7339
7340   /**
7341    * Converts the specified object to an ISO8601 date string.
7342    *
7343    * @param c The object to convert.
7344    * @return The converted object.
7345    */
7346   public static String toIsoDate(Calendar c) {
7347      if (c == null) {
7348         return null;
7349      }
7350      // Convert Calendar to ZonedDateTime and format as ISO8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD)
7351      ZonedDateTime zdt = c.toInstant().atZone(c.getTimeZone().toZoneId());
7352      return zdt.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE);
7353   }
7354
7355   /**
7356    * Converts the specified object to an ISO8601 date-time string.
7357    *
7358    * @param c The object to convert.
7359    * @return The converted object.
7360    */
7361   public static String toIsoDateTime(Calendar c) {
7362      if (c == null) {
7363         return null;
7364      }
7365      // Convert Calendar to ZonedDateTime and format as ISO8601 date-time with timezone
7366      ZonedDateTime zdt = c.toInstant().atZone(c.getTimeZone().toZoneId());
7367      return zdt.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME);
7368   }
7369
7370   /**
7371    * Converts the specified bytes into a readable string.
7372    *
7373    * @param b The number to convert to hex.
7374    * @return A <code><jk>char</jk>[2]</code> containing the specified characters.
7375    */
7376   public static String toReadableBytes(byte[] b) {
7377      var sb = new StringBuilder();
7378      for (var b2 : b)
7379         sb.append((b2 < ' ' || b2 > 'z') ? String.format("[%02X]", b2) : (char)b2 + "   ");
7380      sb.append("\n");
7381      for (var b2 : b)
7382         sb.append(String.format("[%02X]", b2));
7383      return sb.toString();
7384   }
7385
7386   /**
7387    * Same as {@link #toHex(byte[])} but puts spaces between the byte strings.
7388    *
7389    * @param bytes The bytes to convert to hexadecimal.
7390    * @return A new string consisting of hexadecimal characters.
7391    */
7392   public static String toSpacedHex(byte[] bytes) {
7393      var sb = new StringBuilder(bytes.length * 3);
7394      for (var j = 0; j < bytes.length; j++) {
7395         if (j > 0)
7396            sb.append(' ');
7397         var v = bytes[j] & 0xFF;
7398         sb.append(HEX[v >>> 4]).append(HEX[v & 0x0F]);
7399      }
7400      return sb.toString();
7401   }
7402
7403   /**
7404    * Safely converts an object to a string, returning <jk>null</jk> if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
7405    *
7406    * @param obj The object to convert to a string.
7407    * @return The string representation of the object, or <jk>null</jk> if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
7408    */
7409   public static String toString(Object obj) {
7410      return obj == null ? null : obj.toString();
7411   }
7412
7413   /**
7414    * Safely converts an object to a string, returning the default string if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
7415    *
7416    * @param obj The object to convert to a string.
7417    * @param defaultStr The default string to return if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
7418    * @return The string representation of the object, or the default string if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
7419    */
7420   public static String toString(Object obj, String defaultStr) {
7421      return obj == null ? defaultStr : obj.toString();
7422   }
7423
7424   /**
7425    * Converts a collection of strings to a string array.
7426    *
7427    * <p>
7428    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the collection is <jk>null</jk>.
7429    * Returns an empty array if the collection is empty.
7430    *
7431    * @param collection The collection to convert. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
7432    * @return A new string array containing the collection elements, or an empty array if the collection was <jk>null</jk>.
7433    */
7434   public static String[] toStringArray(Collection<String> collection) {
7435      if (collection == null)
7436         return new String[0];
7437      return collection.toArray(new String[collection.size()]);
7438   }
7439
7440   /**
7441    * Converts the specified object to a URI.
7442    *
7443    * @param o The object to convert to a URI.
7444    * @return A new URI, or the same object if the object was already a URI, or
7445    */
7446   public static URI toUri(Object o) {
7447      if (o == null || o instanceof URI)
7448         return (URI)o;
7449      try {
7450         return new URI(o.toString());
7451      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
7452         throw toRex(e);
7453      }
7454   }
7455
7456   /**
7457    * Converts the specified byte array to a UTF-8 string.
7458    *
7459    * @param b The byte array to convert.
7460    * @return The UTF-8 string representation, or <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
7461    */
7462   public static String toUtf8(byte[] b) {
7463      return b == null ? null : new String(b, UTF8);
7464   }
7465
7466   /**
7467    * Converts the contents of the specified input stream to a UTF-8 string.
7468    *
7469    * @param is The input stream to convert.
7470    * @return The UTF-8 string representation of the input stream contents, or <jk>null</jk> if the stream is <jk>null</jk>.
7471    */
7472   public static String toUtf8(InputStream is) {
7473      return safe(() -> is == null ? null : new String(readBytes(is), UTF8));
7474   }
7475
7476   /**
7477    * Transliterates characters in a string by mapping characters from one set to another.
7478    *
7479    * <p>
7480    * Performs character-by-character translation. If a character is found in <c>fromChars</c>,
7481    * it is replaced with the corresponding character at the same position in <c>toChars</c>.
7482    * Characters not found in <c>fromChars</c> are left unchanged.
7483    *
7484    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7485    * <p class='bjava'>
7486    *    transliterate(<js>"hello"</js>, <js>"aeiou"</js>, <js>"12345"</js>);
7487    *    <jc>// "h2ll4"</jc>
7488    *    transliterate(<js>"ABC"</js>, <js>"ABC"</js>, <js>"XYZ"</js>);
7489    *    <jc>// "XYZ"</jc>
7490    * </p>
7491    *
7492    * @param str The string to transliterate. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
7493    * @param fromChars The source character set. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
7494    * @param toChars The target character set. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
7495    * @return The transliterated string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7496    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If <c>fromChars</c> and <c>toChars</c> have different lengths.
7497    */
7498   public static String transliterate(String str, String fromChars, String toChars) {
7499      if (str == null)
7500         return null;
7501      if (fromChars == null || toChars == null || fromChars.isEmpty() || toChars.isEmpty())
7502         return str;
7503      if (fromChars.length() != toChars.length())
7504         throw new IllegalArgumentException("fromChars and toChars must have the same length");
7505
7506      var sb = new StringBuilder(str.length());
7507      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
7508         var c = str.charAt(i);
7509         var index = fromChars.indexOf(c);
7510         if (index >= 0)
7511            sb.append(toChars.charAt(index));
7512         else
7513            sb.append(c);
7514      }
7515      return sb.toString();
7516   }
7517
7518   /**
7519    * Same as {@link String#trim()} but prevents <c>NullPointerExceptions</c>.
7520    *
7521    * @param s The string to trim.
7522    * @return The trimmed string, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
7523    */
7524   public static String trim(String s) {
7525      if (s == null)
7526         return null;
7527      return s.trim();
7528   }
7529
7530   /**
7531    * Trims whitespace characters from the end of the specified string.
7532    *
7533    * @param s The string to trim.
7534    * @return The trimmed string, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
7535    */
7536   public static String trimEnd(String s) {
7537      if (nn(s))
7538         while (ne(s) && isWhitespace(s.charAt(s.length() - 1)))
7539            s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
7540      return s;
7541   }
7542
7543   /**
7544    * Trims <js>'/'</js> characters from the beginning of the specified string.
7545    *
7546    * @param s The string to trim.
7547    * @return A new trimmed string, or the same string if no trimming was necessary.
7548    */
7549   public static String trimLeadingSlashes(String s) {
7550      if (s == null)
7551         return null;
7552      while (ne(s) && s.charAt(0) == '/')
7553         s = s.substring(1);
7554      return s;
7555   }
7556
7557   /**
7558    * Trims <js>'/'</js> characters from both the start and end of the specified string.
7559    *
7560    * @param s The string to trim.
7561    * @return A new trimmed string, or the same string if no trimming was necessary.
7562    */
7563   public static String trimSlashes(String s) {
7564      if (s == null)
7565         return null;
7566      if (s.isEmpty())
7567         return s;
7568      while (endsWith(s, '/'))
7569         s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
7570      while (ne(s) && s.charAt(0) == '/')
7571         s = s.substring(1);
7572      return s;
7573   }
7574
7575   /**
7576    * Trims <js>'/'</js> and space characters from both the start and end of the specified string.
7577    *
7578    * @param s The string to trim.
7579    * @return A new trimmed string, or the same string if no trimming was necessary.
7580    */
7581   public static String trimSlashesAndSpaces(String s) {
7582      if (s == null)
7583         return null;
7584      while (ne(s) && (s.charAt(s.length() - 1) == '/' || isWhitespace(s.charAt(s.length() - 1))))
7585         s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
7586      while (ne(s) && (s.charAt(0) == '/' || isWhitespace(s.charAt(0))))
7587         s = s.substring(1);
7588      return s;
7589   }
7590
7591   /**
7592    * Trims whitespace characters from the beginning of the specified string.
7593    *
7594    * @param s The string to trim.
7595    * @return The trimmed string, or <jk>null</jk> if the string was <jk>null</jk>.
7596    */
7597   public static String trimStart(String s) {
7598      if (nn(s))
7599         while (ne(s) && isWhitespace(s.charAt(0)))
7600            s = s.substring(1);
7601      return s;
7602   }
7603
7604   /**
7605    * Trims <js>'/'</js> characters from the end of the specified string.
7606    *
7607    * @param s The string to trim.
7608    * @return A new trimmed string, or the same string if no trimming was necessary.
7609    */
7610   public static String trimTrailingSlashes(String s) {
7611      if (s == null)
7612         return null;
7613      while (endsWith(s, '/'))
7614         s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
7615      return s;
7616   }
7617
7618   /**
7619    * Uncapitalizes the first character of a string.
7620    *
7621    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7622    * <p class='bjava'>
7623    *    uncapitalize(<jk>null</jk>);          <jc>// null</jc>
7624    *    uncapitalize(<js>""</js>);            <jc>// ""</jc>
7625    *    uncapitalize(<js>"Hello"</js>);       <jc>// "hello"</jc>
7626    *    uncapitalize(<js>"hello"</js>);       <jc>// "hello"</jc>
7627    *    uncapitalize(<js>"HELLO"</js>);       <jc>// "hELLO"</jc>
7628    * </p>
7629    *
7630    * @param str The string to uncapitalize.
7631    * @return The string with the first character uncapitalized, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7632    */
7633   public static String uncapitalize(String str) {
7634      if (isEmpty(str))
7635         return str;
7636      return Character.toLowerCase(str.charAt(0)) + str.substring(1);
7637   }
7638
7639   /**
7640    * Removes escape characters from the specified characters.
7641    *
7642    * @param s The string to remove escape characters from.
7643    * @param escaped The characters escaped.
7644    * @return A new string if characters were removed, or the same string if not or if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
7645    */
7646   @SuppressWarnings({
7647      "java:S127",  // Loop counter advances to skip escape sequences
7648      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for character unescaping logic
7649   })
7650   public static String unescapeChars(String s, AsciiSet escaped) {
7651      if (s == null || s.isEmpty())
7652         return s;
7653      var count = 0;
7654      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++)
7655         if (escaped.contains(s.charAt(i)))
7656            count++;
7657      if (count == 0)
7658         return s;
7659      var sb = new StringBuffer(s.length() - count);
7660      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7661         var c = s.charAt(i);
7662
7663         if (c == '\\' && i + 1 != s.length()) {
7664            var c2 = s.charAt(i + 1);
7665            if (escaped.contains(c2)) {
7666               i++;
7667            }
7668         }
7669         sb.append(s.charAt(i));
7670      }
7671      return sb.toString();
7672   }
7673
7674   /**
7675    * Unescapes HTML entities in a string.
7676    *
7677    * <p>
7678    * Unescapes the following HTML entities:
7679    * <ul>
7680    *   <li><js>"&amp;amp;"</js> → <js>'&amp;'</js></li>
7681    *   <li><js>"&amp;lt;"</js> → <js>'&lt;'</js></li>
7682    *   <li><js>"&amp;gt;"</js> → <js>'&gt;'</js></li>
7683    *   <li><js>"&amp;quot;"</js> → <js>'"'</js></li>
7684    *   <li><js>"&amp;#39;"</js> or <js>"&amp;apos;"</js> → <js>'\''</js></li>
7685    * </ul>
7686    *
7687    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7688    * <p class='bjava'>
7689    *    unescapeHtml(<js>"&amp;lt;script&amp;gt;"</js>);     <jc>// Returns: "&lt;script&gt;"</jc>
7690    * </p>
7691    *
7692    * @param str The string to unescape.
7693    * @return The unescaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7694    */
7695   public static String unescapeHtml(String str) {
7696      if (str == null)
7697         return null;
7698      // Must unescape &amp; last to avoid interfering with other entities
7699      return str.replace("&lt;", "<").replace("&gt;", ">").replace("&quot;", "\"").replace("&#39;", "'").replace("&apos;", "'").replace("&amp;", "&");
7700   }
7701
7702   /**
7703    * Unescapes XML entities in a string.
7704    *
7705    * <p>
7706    * Unescapes the following XML entities:
7707    * <ul>
7708    *   <li><js>"&amp;amp;"</js> → <js>'&amp;'</js></li>
7709    *   <li><js>"&amp;lt;"</js> → <js>'&lt;'</js></li>
7710    *   <li><js>"&amp;gt;"</js> → <js>'&gt;'</js></li>
7711    *   <li><js>"&amp;quot;"</js> → <js>'"'</js></li>
7712    *   <li><js>"&amp;apos;"</js> → <js>'\''</js></li>
7713    * </ul>
7714    *
7715    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7716    * <p class='bjava'>
7717    *    unescapeXml(<js>"&amp;lt;tag&amp;gt;"</js>);     <jc>// Returns: "&lt;tag&gt;"</jc>
7718    * </p>
7719    *
7720    * @param str The string to unescape.
7721    * @return The unescaped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7722    */
7723   public static String unescapeXml(String str) {
7724      if (str == null)
7725         return null;
7726      // Must unescape &amp; last to avoid interfering with other entities
7727      return str.replace("&lt;", "<").replace("&gt;", ">").replace("&quot;", "\"").replace("&apos;", "'").replace("&amp;", "&");
7728   }
7729
7730   /**
7731    * Creates an escaped-unicode sequence (e.g. <js>"\\u1234"</js>) for the specified character.
7732    *
7733    * @param c The character to create a sequence for.
7734    * @return An escaped-unicode sequence.
7735    */
7736   public static String unicodeSequence(char c) {
7737      var sb = new StringBuilder(6);
7738      sb.append('\\').append('u');
7739      for (var cc : toHex4(c))
7740         sb.append(cc);
7741      return sb.toString();
7742   }
7743
7744   /**
7745    * Null-safe convenience method for {@link String#toUpperCase()}.
7746    *
7747    * <p>
7748    * Converts the string to uppercase if not null.
7749    *
7750    * @param s The string to convert.
7751    * @return The uppercase string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
7752    * @see #lowerCase(String)
7753    * @see Utils#uc(String)
7754    */
7755   public static String upperCase(String s) {
7756      return s == null ? null : s.toUpperCase();
7757   }
7758
7759   /**
7760    * Decodes a <c>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</c> string using <c>UTF-8</c> encoding scheme.
7761    *
7762    * @param s The string to decode.
7763    * @return The decoded string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7764    */
7765   public static String urlDecode(String s) {
7766
7767      if (s == null)
7768         return s;
7769
7770      var needsDecode = false;
7771      for (var i = 0; i < s.length() && ! needsDecode; i++) {
7772         var c = s.charAt(i);
7773         if (c == '+' || c == '%')
7774            needsDecode = true;
7775      }
7776
7777      if (needsDecode) {
7778         return safe(()->URLDecoder.decode(s, UTF_8.name()));
7779      }
7780      return s;
7781   }
7782
7783   /**
7784    * Encodes a <c>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</c> string using <c>UTF-8</c> encoding scheme.
7785    *
7786    * @param s The string to encode.
7787    * @return The encoded string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7788    */
7789   public static String urlEncode(String s) {
7790
7791      if (s == null)
7792         return null;
7793
7794      var needsEncode = false;
7795
7796      for (var i = 0; i < s.length() && ! needsEncode; i++)
7797         needsEncode |= (! URL_UNENCODED_CHARS.contains(s.charAt(i)));
7798
7799      if (needsEncode) {
7800         return safe(()->URLEncoder.encode(s, UTF_8.name()));
7801      }
7802
7803      return s;
7804   }
7805
7806   /**
7807    * Same as {@link #urlEncode(String)} except only escapes characters that absolutely need to be escaped.
7808    *
7809    * @param s The string to escape.
7810    * @return The encoded string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7811    */
7812   public static String urlEncodeLax(String s) {
7813      if (s == null)
7814         return null;
7815      var needsEncode = false;
7816      for (var i = 0; i < s.length() && ! needsEncode; i++)
7817         needsEncode |= (! URL_UNENCODED_LAX_CHARS.contains(s.charAt(i)));
7818      if (needsEncode) {
7819         var sb = new StringBuilder(s.length() * 2);
7820         for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7821            var c = s.charAt(i);
7822            if (URL_UNENCODED_LAX_CHARS.contains(c))
7823               sb.append(c);
7824            else if (c == ' ')
7825               sb.append("+");
7826            else if (c <= 127)
7827               sb.append('%').append(toHex2(c));
7828            else
7829               safe(()->sb.append(URLEncoder.encode("" + c, UTF_8.name())));  // Yuck.
7830         }
7831         s = sb.toString();
7832      }
7833      return s;
7834   }
7835
7836   /**
7837    * Similar to {@link URLEncoder#encode(String, String)} but doesn't encode <js>"/"</js> characters.
7838    *
7839    * @param o The object to encode.
7840    * @return The URL encoded string, or <jk>null</jk> if the object was null.
7841    */
7842   @SuppressWarnings({
7843      "java:S127",  // Loop counter advances variably for surrogate pairs and multi-byte sequences
7844      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for URL path encoding
7845   })
7846   public static String urlEncodePath(Object o) {
7847
7848      if (o == null)
7849         return null;
7850
7851      var s = s(o);
7852
7853      var needsEncode = false;
7854      for (var i = 0; i < s.length() && ! needsEncode; i++)
7855         needsEncode = URL_ENCODE_PATHINFO_VALIDCHARS.contains(s.charAt(i));
7856      if (! needsEncode)
7857         return s;
7858
7859      var sb = new StringBuilder();
7860      var caw = new CharArrayWriter();
7861      var caseDiff = ('a' - 'A');
7862
7863      for (var i = 0; i < s.length();) {
7864         var c = s.charAt(i);
7865         if (URL_ENCODE_PATHINFO_VALIDCHARS.contains(c)) {
7866            sb.append(c);
7867            i++;
7868         } else {
7869            if (c == ' ') {
7870               sb.append('+');
7871               i++;
7872            } else {
7873               do {
7874                  caw.write(c);
7875                  if (c >= 0xD800 && c <= 0xDBFF && (i + 1) < s.length()) {
7876                     int d = s.charAt(i + 1);
7877                     if (d >= 0xDC00 && d <= 0xDFFF) {
7878                        caw.write(d);
7879                        i++;
7880                     }
7881                  }
7882                  i++;
7883                  if (i < s.length()) {
7884                     c = s.charAt(i);
7885                  }
7886               } while (i < s.length() && ! URL_ENCODE_PATHINFO_VALIDCHARS.contains(c));
7887
7888               caw.flush();
7889               var s2 = new String(caw.toCharArray());
7890               var ba = s2.getBytes(UTF8);
7891               for (var element : ba) {
7892                  sb.append('%');
7893                  var ch = forDigit((element >> 4) & 0xF, 16);
7894                  if (isLetter(ch)) {
7895                     ch -= caseDiff;
7896                  }
7897                  sb.append(ch);
7898                  ch = forDigit(element & 0xF, 16);
7899                  if (isLetter(ch)) {
7900                     ch -= caseDiff;
7901                  }
7902                  sb.append(ch);
7903               }
7904               caw.reset();
7905            }
7906         }
7907      }
7908      return sb.toString();
7909   }
7910
7911   /**
7912    * Counts the number of words in a string.
7913    *
7914    * <p>
7915    * A word is defined as a sequence of one or more word characters (letters, digits, underscores)
7916    * separated by non-word characters.
7917    *
7918    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7919    * <p class='bjava'>
7920    *    wordCount(<js>"Hello world"</js>);              <jc>// 2</jc>
7921    *    wordCount(<js>"The quick brown fox"</js>);      <jc>// 4</jc>
7922    *    wordCount(<js>"Hello, world! How are you?"</js>); <jc>// 5</jc>
7923    * </p>
7924    *
7925    * @param str The string to count words in. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
