7926    * @return The number of words, or <c>0</c> if the string is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
7927    */
7928   public static int wordCount(String str) {
7929      if (isEmpty(str))
7930         return 0;
7931
7932      var count = 0;
7933      var inWord = false;
7934
7935      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
7936         var c = str.charAt(i);
7937         if ((LETTER.contains(c) || DIGIT.contains(c)) || c == '_') {
7938            if (! inWord) {
7939               count++;
7940               inWord = true;
7941            }
7942         } else {
7943            inWord = false;
7944         }
7945      }
7946
7947      return count;
7948   }
7949
7950   /**
7951    * Wraps text to a specified line length.
7952    *
7953    * <p>
7954    * Wraps text by breaking at word boundaries (spaces). Words longer than the wrap length
7955    * will be broken at the wrap length. Existing newlines are preserved.
7956    *
7957    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7958    * <p class='bjava'>
7959    *    wrap(<js>"hello world test"</js>, 10);  <jc>// "hello world\ntest"</jc>
7960    *    wrap(<jk>null</jk>, 10);                <jc>// null</jc>
7961    * </p>
7962    *
7963    * @param str The string to wrap.
7964    * @param wrapLength The maximum line length (must be &gt; 0).
7965    * @return The wrapped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7966    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if wrapLength is &lt;= 0.
7967    */
7968   public static String wrap(String str, int wrapLength) {
7969      return wrap(str, wrapLength, "\n");
7970   }
7971
7972   /**
7973    * Wraps text to a specified line length with a custom newline string.
7974    *
7975    * <p>
7976    * Wraps text by breaking at word boundaries (spaces). Words longer than the wrap length
7977    * will be broken at the wrap length. Existing newlines are preserved.
7978    *
7979    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
7980    * <p class='bjava'>
7981    *    wrap(<js>"hello world test"</js>, 10, <js>"<br>"</js>);  <jc>// "hello world<br>test"</jc>
7982    *    wrap(<jk>null</jk>, 10, <js>"\n"</js>);                  <jc>// null</jc>
7983    * </p>
7984    *
7985    * @param str The string to wrap.
7986    * @param wrapLength The maximum line length (must be &gt; 0).
7987    * @param newline The string to use as line separator.
7988    * @return The wrapped string, or <jk>null</jk> if input is <jk>null</jk>.
7989    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if wrapLength is &lt;= 0 or newline is <jk>null</jk>.
7990    */
7991   @SuppressWarnings({
7992      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for text wrapping algorithm
7993      "java:S6541" // Thread-safe singleton pattern acceptable
7994   })
7995   public static String wrap(String str, int wrapLength, String newline) {
7996      if (str == null)
7997         return null;
7998      if (isEmpty(str))
7999         return str;
8000      if (wrapLength <= 0)
8001         throw illegalArg("wrapLength must be > 0: {0}", wrapLength);
8002      if (newline == null)
8003         throw illegalArg("newline cannot be null");
8004
8005      var result = new StringBuilder();
8006      var lines = str.split("\r?\n", -1);  // Preserve empty lines
8007
8008      for (var lineIdx = 0; lineIdx < lines.length; lineIdx++) {
8009         var line = lines[lineIdx];
8010         if (line.isEmpty()) {
8011            if (lineIdx < lines.length - 1)
8012               result.append(newline);
8013            continue;
8014         }
8015
8016         // Split into words first, then combine words that fit
8017         var words = line.split(" +");  // Split on one or more spaces
8018         var currentLine = new StringBuilder();
8019
8020         for (var word : words) {
8021            if (word.isEmpty())
8022               continue;
8023
8024            var wordLength = word.length();
8025            var currentLength = currentLine.length();
8026
8027            if (currentLength == 0) {
8028               // First word on line
8029               // Only break single words if there are multiple words in the input
8030               // (single long words should not be broken for readability)
8031               if (wordLength > wrapLength && words.length > 1) {
8032                  // Word is too long and there are other words, break it
8033                  if (!result.isEmpty())
8034                     result.append(newline);
8035                  var wordPos = 0;
8036                  while (wordPos < wordLength) {
8037                     if (wordPos > 0)
8038                        result.append(newline);
8039                     var remaining = wordLength - wordPos;
8040                     if (remaining <= wrapLength) {
8041                        result.append(word.substring(wordPos));
8042                        break;
8043                     }
8044                     result.append(word.substring(wordPos, wordPos + wrapLength));
8045                     wordPos += wrapLength;
8046                  }
8047               } else {
8048                  currentLine.append(word);
8049               }
8050            } else {
8051               // Check if we can add this word to current line
8052               var neededLength = currentLength + 1 + wordLength;  // current + space + word
8053               // Break if it would fit exactly or exceed - prefer breaking for readability
8054               if (neededLength < wrapLength) {
8055                  // Fits with room to spare
8056                  currentLine.append(' ').append(word);
8057               } else {
8058                  // Doesn't fit or fits exactly - start new line
8059                  if (!result.isEmpty())
8060                     result.append(newline);
8061                  result.append(currentLine);
8062                  currentLine.setLength(0);
8063                  if (wordLength > wrapLength && words.length > 1) {
8064                     // Word is too long and there are other words, break it
8065                     result.append(newline);
8066                     var wordPos = 0;
8067                     while (wordPos < wordLength) {
8068                        if (wordPos > 0)
8069                           result.append(newline);
8070                        var remaining = wordLength - wordPos;
8071                        if (remaining <= wrapLength) {
8072                           result.append(word.substring(wordPos));
8073                           break;
8074                        }
8075                        result.append(word.substring(wordPos, wordPos + wrapLength));
8076                        wordPos += wrapLength;
8077                     }
8078                  } else {
8079                     currentLine.append(word);
8080                  }
8081               }
8082            }
8083         }
8084
8085         // Append any remaining line
8086         if (!currentLine.isEmpty()) {
8087            if (!result.isEmpty())
8088               result.append(newline);
8089            result.append(currentLine);
8090         }
8091      }
8092
8093      return result.toString();
8094   }
8095
8096   /**
8097    * Helper method to estimate the number of syllables in a word.
8098    */
8099   private static int estimateSyllables(String word) {
8100
8101      var lower = word.toLowerCase();
8102      var count = 0;
8103      var prevWasVowel = false;
8104
8105      for (var i = 0; i < lower.length(); i++) {
8106         var c = lower.charAt(i);
8107         var isVowel = (VOWEL.contains(c) || c == 'y');
8108
8109         if (isVowel && ! prevWasVowel) {
8110            count++;
8111         }
8112         prevWasVowel = isVowel;
8113      }
8114
8115      // Handle silent 'e' at the end
8116      if (lower.endsWith("e") && count > 1) {
8117         count--;
8118      }
8119
8120      // At least one syllable
8121      return Math.max(1, count);
8122   }
8123
8124   /**
8125    * Finds the first non-whitespace, non-comment character in a string.
8126    *
8127    * @param s The string to analyze.
8128    * @return The first real character, or <c>-1</c> if none found.
8129    */
8130   private static int firstRealCharacter(String s) {
8131      return safe(()-> {
8132         var r = new StringReader(s);
8133         var c = 0;
8134         while ((c = r.read()) != -1) {
8135            if (! isWhitespace(c)) {
8136               if (c == '/') {
8137                  skipComments(r);
8138               } else {
8139                  return c;
8140               }
8141            }
8142         }
8143         return -1;
8144      });
8145   }
8146
8147   /**
8148    * Helper method to get Soundex code for a character.
8149    */
8150   private static char getSoundexCode(char c) {
8151      if (c == 'A' || c == 'E' || c == 'I' || c == 'O' || c == 'U' || c == 'H' || c == 'W' || c == 'Y')
8152         return '0';
8153      if (c == 'B' || c == 'F' || c == 'P' || c == 'V')
8154         return '1';
8155      if (c == 'C' || c == 'G' || c == 'J' || c == 'K' || c == 'Q' || c == 'S' || c == 'X' || c == 'Z')
8156         return '2';
8157      if (c == 'D' || c == 'T')
8158         return '3';
8159      if (c == 'L')
8160         return '4';
8161      if (c == 'M' || c == 'N')
8162         return '5';
8163      if (c == 'R')
8164         return '6';
8165      return '0'; // Non-letter characters
8166   }
8167
8168   /**
8169    * Validates if a string is a valid IPv6 address format (without network operations).
8170    *
8171    * <p>
8172    * This method performs pure string-based validation and does not perform any DNS lookups
8173    * or network operations, making it fast and suitable for validation purposes.
8174    *
8175    * @param ip The IPv6 address string to validate.
8176    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the string is a valid IPv6 address format, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
8177    */
8178   @SuppressWarnings({
8179      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for IPv6 validation
8180      "java:S6541", // Thread-safe singleton pattern acceptable
8181      "java:S1313" // IPv6 validation intentionally compares against canonical literals like :: and ::ffff
8182   })
8183   public static boolean isValidIPv6Address(String ip) {
8184      if (ip == null || ip.isEmpty())
8185         return false;
8186
8187      // IPv6 addresses can be:
8188      // 1. Full format: 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334 (8 groups of 4 hex digits)
8189      // 2. Compressed format: 2001:db8::1 (uses :: to represent consecutive zeros)
8190      // 3. IPv4-mapped: ::ffff:192.168.1.1 (last 32 bits as IPv4)
8191      // 4. Loopback: ::1
8192      // 5. Unspecified: ::
8193
8194      // Cannot start or end with a single colon (except ::)
8195      if (ip.startsWith(":") && !ip.startsWith("::"))
8196         return false;
8197      if (ip.endsWith(":") && !ip.endsWith("::"))
8198         return false;
8199
8200      // Check for IPv4-mapped format (contains both : and .)
8201      if (ip.contains(".")) {
8202         // Must be in format ::ffff:x.x.x.x or similar
8203         var lastColon = ip.lastIndexOf(":");
8204         if (lastColon < 0)
8205            return false;
8206         var ipv4Part = ip.substring(lastColon + 1);
8207         // Validate IPv4 part
8208         var ipv4Parts = ipv4Part.split("\\.");
8209         if (ipv4Parts.length != 4)
8210            return false;
8211         for (var part : ipv4Parts) {
8212            try {
8213               var num = Integer.parseInt(part);
8214               if (num < 0 || num > 255)
8215                  return false;
8216            } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
8217               return false;
8218            }
8219         }
8220         // Validate IPv6 part before the IPv4
8221         var ipv6Part = ip.substring(0, lastColon);
8222         // Accept empty, ::, ::ffff, ::FFFF, or : (when string starts with ::)
8223         if (ipv6Part.isEmpty() || ipv6Part.equals("::") || ipv6Part.equals("::ffff") || ipv6Part.equals("::FFFF") || (ipv6Part.equals(":") && ip.startsWith("::")))
8224            return true;
8225         // More complex validation would be needed for other IPv4-mapped formats
8226         // For now, accept common formats
8227      }
8228
8229      // Check for :: (compression) - only one allowed
8230      var doubleColonCount = 0;
8231      for (var i = 1; i < ip.length(); i++) {
8232         if (ip.charAt(i) == ':' && ip.charAt(i - 1) == ':') {
8233            doubleColonCount++;
8234            if (doubleColonCount > 1)
8235               return false; // Only one :: allowed
8236         }
8237      }
8238
8239      // Split by ::
8240      var parts = ip.split("::", -1);
8241
8242      if (parts.length == 2) {
8243         // Compressed format
8244         var leftParts = parts[0].isEmpty() ? new String[0] : parts[0].split(":");
8245         var rightParts = parts[1].isEmpty() ? new String[0] : parts[1].split(":");
8246         var totalParts = leftParts.length + rightParts.length;
8247         if (totalParts > 7)
8248            return false; // Too many groups (max 8, but :: counts as one or more)
8249      } else {
8250         // Full format (no compression)
8251         var groups = ip.split(":");
8252         if (groups.length != 8)
8253            return false;
8254      }
8255
8256      // Validate each hex group
8257      var groups = ip.split("::");
8258      for (var groupSection : groups) {
8259         if (groupSection.isEmpty())
8260            continue; // Skip empty section from ::
8261         var groupParts = groupSection.split(":");
8262         for (var group : groupParts) {
8263            if (group.length() > 4)
8264               return false; // Each group is max 4 hex digits
8265            // Validate hex digits
8266            for (var i = 0; i < group.length(); i++) {
8267               var c = group.charAt(i);
8268               if (! HEXADECIMAL_CHARS.contains(c))
8269                  return false;
8270            }
8271         }
8272      }
8273
8274      return true;
8275   }
8276
8277
8278   private static class Readifier {
8279      private final Class<?> type;
8280      private final Function<Object,String> bridge;
8281
8282      private <T> Readifier(Class<T> type, Function<? super T,String> converter) {
8283         this.type = type;
8284         this.bridge = o -> converter.apply(type.cast(o));
8285      }
8286
8287      Class<?> forClass() {
8288         return type;
8289      }
8290
8291      Function<Object,String> toFunction() {
8292         return bridge;
8293      }
8294   }
8295
8296   private static <T> Readifier readifier(Class<T> type, Function<? super T,String> converter) {
8297      return new Readifier(type, converter);
8298   }
8299
8300   private static List<Readifier> loadReadifiers() {
8301      var list = new ArrayList<Readifier>();
8302
8303      // More specific types first - order matters!
8304      list.add(readifier(Map.Entry.class, x -> readable(x.getKey()) + '=' + readable(x.getValue())));
8305      list.add(readifier(Collection.class, x -> ((Collection<?>)x).stream().map(StringUtils::readable).collect(joining(",", "[", "]"))));
8306      list.add(readifier(Map.class, x -> ((Map<?,?>)x).entrySet().stream().map(StringUtils::readable).collect(joining(",", "{", "}"))));
8307      list.add(readifier(Iterable.class, x -> readable(toList(x))));
8308      list.add(readifier(Iterator.class, x -> readable(toList(x))));
8309      list.add(readifier(Enumeration.class, x -> readable(toList(x))));
8310      list.add(readifier(Optional.class, x -> readable(((Optional<?>)x).orElse(null))));
8311      list.add(readifier(GregorianCalendar.class, x -> x.toZonedDateTime().format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT)));
8312      list.add(readifier(Date.class, x -> x.toInstant().toString()));
8313      list.add(readifier(InputStream.class, x -> toHex(x)));
8314      list.add(readifier(Reader.class, (Reader x) -> safe(() -> read(x))));
8315      list.add(readifier(File.class, (File x) -> safe(() -> read(x))));
8316      list.add(readifier(byte[].class, x -> toHex(x)));
8317      list.add(readifier(Enum.class, x -> ((Enum<?>)x).name()));
8318      list.add(readifier(Class.class, Utils::cns));
8319      list.add(readifier(Constructor.class, x -> ConstructorInfo.of(x).getNameFull()));
8320      list.add(readifier(Method.class, x -> MethodInfo.of(x).getNameFull()));
8321      list.add(readifier(Field.class, x -> FieldInfo.of(x).toString()));
8322      list.add(readifier(Parameter.class, x -> ParameterInfo.of(x).toString()));
8323      list.add(readifier(ClassInfo.class, ClassInfo::toString));
8324      list.add(readifier(MethodInfo.class, MethodInfo::toString));
8325      list.add(readifier(ConstructorInfo.class, ConstructorInfo::toString));
8326      list.add(readifier(FieldInfo.class, FieldInfo::toString));
8327      list.add(readifier(ParameterInfo.class, ParameterInfo::toString));
8328
8329      return Collections.unmodifiableList(list);
8330   }
8331
8332   /**
8333    * Determines the multiplier value based on the suffix character in a string.
8334    *
8335    * @param s The string to analyze for multiplier suffix.
8336    * @return The multiplier value (1 if no valid suffix found).
8337    */
8338   private static int multiplierInt(String s) {
8339      var c = isEmpty(s) ? 'z' : s.charAt(s.length() - 1);
8340      if (c == 'G')
8341         return 1024 * 1024 * 1024;
8342      if (c == 'M')
8343         return 1024 * 1024;
8344      if (c == 'K')
8345         return 1024;
8346      if (c == 'g')
8347         return 1000 * 1000 * 1000;
8348      if (c == 'm')
8349         return 1000 * 1000;
8350      if (c == 'k')
8351         return 1000;
8352      return 1;
8353   }
8354
8355   /**
8356    * Determines the long multiplier value based on the suffix character in a string.
8357    *
8358    * @param s The string to analyze for multiplier suffix.
8359    * @return The multiplier value (1 if no valid suffix found).
8360    */
8361   private static long multiplierLong(String s) {
8362      if (isEmpty(s))
8363         return 1;
8364      var c = s.charAt(s.length() - 1);
8365      if (c == 'P')
8366         return 1125899906842624L;  // 1024^5
8367      if (c == 'T')
8368         return 1099511627776L;  // 1024^4
8369      if (c == 'G')
8370         return 1073741824L;  // 1024^3
8371      if (c == 'M')
8372         return 1048576L;  // 1024^2
8373      if (c == 'K')
8374         return 1024L;
8375      if (c == 'p')
8376         return 1000000000000000L;  // 1000^5
8377      if (c == 't')
8378         return 1000000000000L;  // 1000^4
8379      if (c == 'g')
8380         return 1000000000L;  // 1000^3
8381      if (c == 'm')
8382         return 1000000L;  // 1000^2
8383      if (c == 'k')
8384         return 1000L;
8385      return 1;
8386   }
8387
8388   /**
8389    * Parses a unit string and converts the value to milliseconds.
8390    *
8391    * @param unit The unit string (case-insensitive, already lowercased).
8392    * @param value The numeric value.
8393    * @return The value in milliseconds, or <c>-1</c> if the unit is invalid.
8394    */
8395   @SuppressWarnings({
8396      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for unit parsing logic
8397   })
8398   private static long parseUnit(String unit, double value) {
8399      if (isEmpty(unit)) {
8400         // No unit means milliseconds
8401         return (long)value;
8402      }
8403
8404      // Check milliseconds first (before minutes) - must check exact "ms" before checking "m"
8405      if (unit.equals("ms") || unit.equals("millis") || unit.equals("milliseconds"))
8406         return (long)value;
8407
8408      // Seconds
8409      if (unit.startsWith("s") && !unit.startsWith("sec"))
8410         return (long)(value * 1000);
8411      if (unit.startsWith("sec") || unit.startsWith("second"))
8412         return (long)(value * 1000);
8413
8414      // Minutes (must check after milliseconds and months)
8415      if (unit.startsWith("m") && !unit.startsWith("mo") && !unit.startsWith("mill") && !unit.startsWith("ms"))
8416         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60);
8417      if (unit.startsWith("min") || unit.startsWith("minute"))
8418         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60);
8419
8420      // Hours
8421      if (unit.startsWith("h") || unit.startsWith("hour"))
8422         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60);
8423
8424      // Days
8425      if (unit.startsWith("d") && !unit.startsWith("da"))
8426         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
8427      if (unit.startsWith("day"))
8428         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
8429
8430      // Weeks
8431      if (unit.startsWith("w") || unit.startsWith("week"))
8432         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 7);
8433
8434      // Months (30 days)
8435      if (unit.startsWith("mo") || unit.startsWith("month"))
8436         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 30);
8437
8438      // Years (365 days)
8439      if (unit.startsWith("y") && !unit.startsWith("yr"))
8440         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 365);
8441      if (unit.startsWith("yr") || unit.startsWith("year"))
8442         return (long)(value * 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 365);
8443
8444      // Unknown unit
8445      return -1;
8446   }
8447
8448   /**
8449    * Skips over a single comment sequence in a StringReader.
8450    *
8451    * <p>
8452    * The reader must be positioned at the first <js>'/'</js> character of a comment.
8453    * This method will skip only the comment it's currently positioned on, not all comments in the reader.
8454    *
8455    * <p>
8456    * Supports both <js>"/* * /"</js> style block comments and <js>"//"</js> style line comments.
8457    *
8458    * @param r The StringReader positioned at the start of a comment (at the first <js>'/'</js>).
8459    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs.
8460    */
8461   @SuppressWarnings({
8462      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for comment skipping logic
8463   })
8464   public static void skipComments(StringReader r) throws IOException {
8465      var c = r.read();
8466      //  "/* */" style comments
8467      if (c == '*') {
8468         c = r.read();
8469         while (c != -1) {
8470            if (c == '*') {
8471               c = r.read();
8472               if (c == '/')
8473                  return;
8474               // If not '/', continue checking from this character
8475               // Don't read again, just continue the loop
8476            } else {
8477               c = r.read();
8478            }
8479         }
8480         //  "//" style comments
8481      } else if (c == '/') {
8482         while ((c = r.read()) != -1) {
8483            if (c == '\n')
8484               return;
8485         }
8486      }
8487   }
8488
8489   /**
8490    * Gets or creates an AsciiSet for escaping the specified character.
8491    *
8492    * @param c The character to create an escape set for.
8493    * @return An AsciiSet containing the character and backslash.
8494    */
8495   private static AsciiSet getEscapeSet(char c) {
8496      return ESCAPE_SETS.computeIfAbsent(c, key -> AsciiSet.create().chars(key, '\\').build());
8497   }
8498
8499   /**
8500    * Helper method to split a string into words.
8501    * Detects word boundaries from separators (spaces, underscores, hyphens) and case changes.
8502    *
8503    * @param str The string to split.
8504    * @return A list of words, or empty list if input is null or empty.
8505    */
8506   @SuppressWarnings({
8507      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for word splitting logic
8508   })
8509   private static List<String> splitWords(String str) {
8510      if (str == null || isEmpty(str))
8511         return Collections.emptyList();
8512
8513      var words = new ArrayList<String>();
8514      var sb = new StringBuilder();
8515      var wasLowerCase = false;
8516      var wasUpperCase = false;
8517      var consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8518
8519      for (var i = 0; i < str.length(); i++) {
8520         var c = str.charAt(i);
8521         var isSeparator = (c == ' ' || c == '_' || c == '-' || c == '\t');
8522         var isUpperCase = LETTER_UC.contains(c);
8523         var isLowerCase = LETTER_LC.contains(c);
8524         var isLetter = LETTER.contains(c);
8525
8526         if (isSeparator) {
8527            if (!sb.isEmpty()) {
8528               words.add(sb.toString());
8529               sb.setLength(0);
8530            }
8531            wasLowerCase = false;
8532            wasUpperCase = false;
8533            consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8534         } else if (isLetter) {
8535            // Detect word boundary:
8536            // 1. Uppercase after lowercase (e.g., "helloWorld" → "hello", "World")
8537            // 2. Uppercase after consecutive uppercase when next is lowercase (e.g., "XMLHttp" → "XML", "Http")
8538            // 3. Lowercase after 2+ consecutive uppercase (e.g., "XMLHttp" → "XML", "Http")
8539            if (!sb.isEmpty()) {
8540               if (isUpperCase && wasLowerCase) {
8541                  // Case 1: uppercase after lowercase (e.g., "helloWorld" → "hello", "World")
8542                  words.add(sb.toString());
8543                  sb.setLength(0);
8544                  consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8545               } else if (isUpperCase && wasUpperCase && consecutiveUpperCount >= 2) {
8546                  // Case 2: uppercase after uppercase - check if this starts a new word
8547                  // Look ahead to see if next character is lowercase
8548                  // This handles "XMLHttp" where 'H' starts "Http"
8549                  // We need at least 2 consecutive uppercase letters before this one to split
8550                  if (i + 1 < str.length()) {
8551                     var nextChar = str.charAt(i + 1);
8552                     if (LETTER_LC.contains(nextChar)) {
8553                        // This uppercase starts a new word, split before it
8554                        words.add(sb.toString());
8555                        sb.setLength(0);
8556                        consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8557                     }
8558                  }
8559               } else if (isLowerCase && wasUpperCase && consecutiveUpperCount >= 2) {
8560                  // Case 3: lowercase after 2+ consecutive uppercase
8561                  // Split all but the last uppercase (e.g., "XMLH" → "XML" + "H")
8562                  var splitPoint = sb.length() - 1;
8563                  words.add(sb.substring(0, splitPoint));
8564                  sb.delete(0, splitPoint);
8565                  consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8566               }
8567            }
8568            sb.append(c);
8569            // Update state AFTER appending
8570            wasLowerCase = isLowerCase;
8571            wasUpperCase = isUpperCase;
8572            if (isUpperCase) {
8573               consecutiveUpperCount++;
8574            } else {
8575               consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8576            }
8577         } else {
8578            // Non-letter characters (digits, etc.) - treat as part of current word
8579            sb.append(c);
8580            wasLowerCase = false;
8581            wasUpperCase = false;
8582         consecutiveUpperCount = 0;
8583      }
8584   }
8585
8586   if (!sb.isEmpty())
8587      words.add(sb.toString());
8588
8589      return words;
8590   }
8591
8592   /**
8593    * Constructor.
8594    */
8595   protected StringUtils() {}
8596}
